If the SGSN cannot process the request (e.g., the user is unknown), it will return a CLA with an appropriate experimental `Result-Code` like `DIAMETER_ERROR_USER_UNKNOWN`. (TS 29.272 §7.2.8.2 V17.4.0)
doc.title: Cancel-Location-Request

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for this Diameter session, used to correlate messages. (RFC 6733) Origin: Generated by the HSS to uniquely identify the session.

avp_auth_application_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_application_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as S6d. (TS 29.272 §7.1.2) Origin: A fixed value (16777251) defined in the 3GPP specification for the S6d interface.

avp_auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
avp_auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether state is maintained for this session. For CLR, no state is maintained. (RFC 6733) Origin: Set to a spec-defined enum value indicating no state is maintained for this one-off transaction.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0): State is maintained, subsequent messages are expected.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED (1): State is not maintained, this is a one-time transaction.

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the HSS sending the request. (RFC 6733) Origin: The configured FQDN of the sending HSS node.

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the HSS sending the request. (RFC 6733) Origin: The configured realm of the sending HSS node.

avp_destination_host.name: Destination-Host
avp_destination_host.doc: The FQDN of the target SGSN. (RFC 6733) Origin: Determined by the HSS based on the user's last known SGSN registration information.

avp_destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
avp_destination_realm.doc: The realm of the target SGSN. (RFC 6733) Origin: Determined by the HSS based on the user's last known SGSN registration information.

avp_user_name.name: User-Name
avp_user_name.doc: The IMSI of the user whose location is being cancelled. (TS 29.272 §7.3.3) Origin: The IMSI of the subscriber whose context is being managed by the HSS.

avp_cancellation_type.name: Cancellation-Type
avp_cancellation_type.doc: Specifies the reason for the location cancellation. (TS 29.272 §7.3.30) Origin: Set by the HSS based on the event that triggered the cancellation procedure.
enum_doc: MME_UPDATE_PROCEDURE (0): Cancellation due to UE registration at a new MME.
SGSN_UPDATE_PROCEDURE (1): Cancellation due to UE registration at a new SGSN.
SUBSCRIPTION_WITHDRAWAL (2): The user's subscription has been deleted.
UPDATE_PROCEDURE_IWF (3): Cancellation due to UE registration at a new IWF.
INITIAL_ATTACH_PROCEDURE (4): Cancellation due to an initial attach at a new MME/SGSN.
P-CSCF_RESTORATION (5): Cancellation due to P-CSCF restoration procedure.
SUBSCRIPTION_CHANGE_VPLMN_ID (6): Cancellation due to subscription change of VPLMN ID.

avp_clr_flags.name: CLR-Flags
avp_clr_flags.doc: Provides additional information about the cancellation request. (TS 29.272 §7.3.138) Origin: Set by the HSS to provide additional context for the cancellation, based on internal logic.
bits_doc: Initial Attach Procedure: If this bit is set, it indicates that the CLR is sent as part of an initial attach procedure.

avp_supported_features.name: Supported-Features
avp_supported_features.doc: Communicates the supported features of the HSS. (TS 29.272 §7.3.113) Origin: Configured on the HSS to advertise its capabilities to the peer SGSN.

avp_vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
avp_vendor_id.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier. For 3GPP, this is 10415. (TS 29.229) Origin: A fixed value (10415) defined by 3GPP for its vendor-specific AVPs.

avp_feature_list_id.name: Feature-List-ID
avp_feature_list_id.doc: Identifies a list of features. (TS 29.229) Origin: A configured value on the HSS that identifies a specific set of supported features.

avp_feature_list.name: Feature-List
avp_feature_list.doc: A bitmask indicating the supported features. (TS 29.229) Origin: A configured bitmask on the HSS representing the enabled features for the list identified by Feature-List-ID.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends a Cancel-Location-Answer (CLA) to the HSS to acknowledge the successful processing of a Cancel-Location-Request (CLR). This confirms the MME has removed the user's mobility and session context as requested by the HSS.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Cancel-Location-Answer (CLA) command is sent by the MME to the HSS to acknowledge a Cancel-Location-Request. It indicates the outcome of the cancellation procedure at the MME. (TS 29.272 §7.2.7.1 V18.1.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when an MME receives a Cancel-Location-Request (CLR) from the HSS. The CLR is typically sent when the HSS needs to detach a UE, or when a UE has registered in a new location (e.g., another MME or SGSN) and the HSS instructs the old MME to clear its context for that UE. (TS 29.272 §7.2.6.1 V18.1.0)

### Key AVPs
The message must include the Session-Id from the request, the MME's identity in Origin-Host and Origin-Realm, and the result of the operation in the Experimental-Result AVP. The Supported-Features AVP may also be included to convey feature support information. (TS 29.272, Table 7.2.7.2-1 V18.1.0)

### Error Handling
The outcome of the procedure is reported in the Experimental-Result-Code within the Experimental-Result AVP. A value of DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001) indicates successful processing. Other values indicate specific errors, such as DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY (5012) if the MME cannot perform the cancellation. (TS 29.272 §7.5 V18.1.0)
doc.title: Cancel-Location-Answer

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session, used to correlate messages. It is copied from the corresponding request message. Origin: Copied from the corresponding Cancel-Location-Request message.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as S6a (16777251). Origin: Set to the S6a/S6d Application ID (16777251) as defined in TS 29.272.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates that no session state is maintained on the server for this session. Origin: Set to a fixed value (NO_STATE_MAINTAINED) as specified for this procedure in TS 29.272.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0): State is maintained, subsequent messages must be sent to the same server.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED (1): State is not maintained, subsequent messages can be sent to any server.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity (FQDN) of the MME that generated the message. Origin: Configured on the MME node as its FQDN.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the MME that generated the message. Origin: Configured on the MME node as its home network domain.

experimental_result.name: Experimental-Result
experimental_result.doc: A grouped AVP containing the vendor-specific result of the request, indicating success or failure. Origin: Generated by the MME to report the outcome of processing the CLR message.

vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier for 3GPP (10415). Origin: Set to the fixed value for 3GPP (10415) as defined in the specification.

exp_result_code.name: Experimental-Result-Code
exp_result_code.doc: The 3GPP-specific result code indicating success or a specific error condition. 2001 indicates success. Origin: Determined by the MME based on the processing result of the CLR, e.g., DIAMETER_SUCCESS.

supported_features.name: Supported-Features
supported_features.doc: A grouped AVP used to exchange information about supported optional features between the MME and HSS. Origin: Configured on the MME to advertise its specific feature capabilities to the HSS.

vendor_id_sf.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id_sf.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier for 3GPP (10415), used within the Supported-Features AVP. Origin: Set to the fixed value for 3GPP (10415) as defined in the specification.

feature_list_id.name: Feature-List-ID
feature_list_id.doc: A numerical identifier for a list of features. For S6a, ID 1 is commonly used. Origin: Defined in TS 29.272 to group related features.

feature_list.name: Feature-List
feature_list.doc: A bitmask indicating the supported features corresponding to the Feature-List-ID. Origin: Set by the MME based on its implemented features corresponding to the Feature-List-ID.

doc.summary_en: The HSS sends a Cancel-Location-Request (CLR) to the MME to indicate that the MME should remove the subscriber's profile and location information. This is typically triggered by an administrative action (e.g., subscription removal) or when the subscriber registers on a different node type (e.g., SGSN).
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Cancel-Location-Request (CLR) command is sent by the HSS to the MME to inform the MME that the location of the subscriber has to be cancelled. (TS 29.272 §7.2.6.1 V18.1.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is initiated by the HSS due to:
1.  **Subscription Withdrawal**: An operator administratively removes a subscriber's subscription from the HSS.
2.  **Re-registration on another node**: The UE registers via a non-3GPP access and the HSS needs to detach the old EPC session.
3.  **Update Procedure**: Certain subscription data changes require a full re-attach.
(TS 29.272 §7.2.6.1 V18.1.0)

### Key AVPs
- **User-Name**: Contains the IMSI of the subscriber.
- **Cancellation-Type**: Indicates the reason for the cancellation, such as `SUBSCRIPTION_WITHDRAWAL`.
- **Destination-Host/Realm**: Identifies the target MME serving the subscriber.

### Expected Outcome
Upon receiving the CLR, the MME will initiate a detach procedure for the specified UE and send a Cancel-Location-Answer (CLA) back to the HSS. The UE will then need to perform a new Attach procedure to regain network services, during which the MME will fetch the updated subscription data from the HSS. (TS 29.272 §7.2.6.2 V18.1.0)
doc.title: Cancel-Location-Request

session_id.doc: A unique identifier for this Diameter session, used to correlate requests and answers. Origin: Generated by the HSS to uniquely identify this specific Diameter transaction.

auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether any state is maintained for this session. For CLR, this is always set to NO_STATE_MAINTAINED. Origin: Set to a fixed value (1) as defined by the S6a interface specification for this procedure.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0): State is maintained, subsequent messages will be part of the same session.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED (1): No state is maintained, this is a standalone transaction.

origin_host.doc: The Diameter Identity (FQDN) of the node that originated the message (the HSS). Origin: Configured on the HSS as its Diameter Identity.

origin_realm.doc: The Diameter realm of the originating node (the HSS). Origin: Configured on the HSS as its home Diameter realm.

destination_host.doc: The Diameter Identity (FQDN) of the destination node (the MME). Origin: Determined by the HSS from the subscriber's registration data.

destination_realm.doc: The Diameter realm of the destination node (the MME). Origin: Determined by the HSS from the subscriber's registration data.

user_name.doc: The identity of the user, which for the S6a interface is the International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI). Origin: The IMSI of the subscriber whose profile or location data triggered the cancellation.

cancellation_type.doc: An enumerated value indicating the reason for the cancellation procedure. Origin: Set by the HSS based on the administrative or procedural trigger, such as subscription withdrawal.
enum_doc: MME_UPDATE_PROCEDURE (0): Used when the UE registers in a new MME.
SGSN_UPDATE_PROCEDURE (1): Used when the UE registers in a new SGSN.
SUBSCRIPTION_WITHDRAWAL (2): The user's subscription has been cancelled.
UPDATE_PROCEDURE (3): The HSS needs to update session information.
INITIAL_ATTACH_PROCEDURE (4): Used during an initial attach procedure.

supported_features.doc: A grouped AVP which contains the list of features supported by the origin host (HSS). Origin: Configured on the HSS to advertise its capabilities to the peer MME.

clr_flags.doc: A bitmask of flags that provide additional information for the cancellation. For example, it can indicate that the HSS is requesting the MME to page the UE. Origin: Set by the HSS to provide specific instructions to the MME for the cancellation procedure.

doc.summary_en: The PCRF sends a Credit-Control-Answer (CCA) to the PGW to acknowledge the establishment of a Gx session. This initial response carries the PCC (Policy and Charging Control) rules, QoS parameters, and event triggers that govern the IP-CAN session.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent by the Policy and Charging Rules Function (PCRF) in response to a Credit-Control-Request [Initial] from the Packet Data Network Gateway (PGW). It establishes the Gx session and provides the initial set of policy and charging control rules to be enforced for the user's session. (TS 29.212 §4.5.2 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
The PCRF sends this message upon successfully processing a CCR [Initial] message from the PGW, which is triggered by a UE establishing a new PDN connection. The PCRF makes policy decisions based on the subscriber's profile and the session information provided in the request. (TS 29.212 §4.5.1 V18.4.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Echoed from the CCR to correlate the session.
- **Result-Code**: Indicates the success (2001) or failure of the request.
- **Charging-Rule-Install**: A grouped AVP containing one or more PCC rules to be installed at the PGW. Each rule can specify service data flow filters, QoS, and charging parameters.
- **Event-Trigger**: Specifies events that should trigger the PGW to re-request policy information from the PCRF (e.g., location change, QoS change).
- **Default-EPS-Bearer-QoS**: Provides the QoS parameters for the default EPS bearer.

### Error Handling
If the PCRF cannot authorize the session, it responds with a relevant error in the `Result-Code` AVP (e.g., DIAMETER_AUTHORIZATION_REJECTED 5003). The PGW will then reject the PDN connection establishment. (RFC 4006 §8.1 V18.4.0)
doc.title: Credit-Control-Answer

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, used to correlate messages. Origin: The value is copied from the corresponding Credit-Control-Request message.

avp_result_code.name: Result-Code
avp_result_code.doc: Indicates the result of the request processing. 2001 indicates success. Origin: Set by the PCRF to indicate the outcome of processing the request.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_SUCCESS: The request was successfully completed.
DIAMETER_AUTHORIZATION_REJECTED: The request was rejected for authorization reasons.

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that generated the message (the PCRF). Origin: The value is the configured FQDN of the responding node (PCRF).

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that generated the message. Origin: The value is the configured realm of the responding node (PCRF).

avp_auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Gx (16777238). Origin: The value is fixed for the Gx application as defined in the 3GPP specification.

avp_cc_req_type.name: CC-Request-Type
avp_cc_req_type.doc: Echoes the request type from the CCR. For an initial request, this is 1. Origin: The value is copied from the corresponding Credit-Control-Request message.

avp_cc_req_num.name: CC-Request-Number
avp_cc_req_num.doc: Echoes the request number from the CCR, used to match requests and answers. Starts at 0. Origin: The value is copied from the corresponding Credit-Control-Request message.

avp_charging_rule_install.name: Charging-Rule-Install
avp_charging_rule_install.doc: A grouped AVP that contains the PCC rules to be installed in the PGW. Origin: Determined by the PCRF based on subscription data, operator policies, and session information.

avp_charging_rule_name.name: Charging-Rule-Name
avp_charging_rule_name.doc: The name of a PCC rule to be installed, modified, or removed. Origin: Assigned by the PCRF to uniquely identify a PCC rule within the Gx session.

avp_event_trigger.name: Event-Trigger
avp_event_trigger.doc: Indicates an event that should trigger a CCR from the PGW to the PCRF. Origin: Determined by the PCRF to instruct the PGW on which events require re-interaction.
enum_doc: QOS_CHANGE (12): The QoS for a service data flow has changed.
USAGE_REPORT (17): A usage threshold has been reached.
PLMN_CHANGE (33): The serving PLMN has changed.

avp_def_eps_bearer_qos.name: Default-EPS-Bearer-QoS
avp_def_eps_bearer_qos.doc: A grouped AVP containing the QoS parameters for the default EPS bearer. Origin: Determined by the PCRF based on the subscriber's subscription profile (HSS/UDR data).

avp_qci.name: QoS-Class-Identifier
avp_qci.doc: A scalar that represents the QoS characteristics of the bearer (e.g., packet delay budget, packet error rate). Origin: The value is derived from the subscriber's profile data and operator policy at the PCRF.
enum_doc: QCI 9: Used for default internet traffic.

avp_arp.name: Allocation-Retention-Priority
avp_arp.doc: A grouped AVP that defines the priority for allocating and retaining bearer resources. Origin: The value is derived from the subscriber's profile data at the PCRF.

avp_priority_level.name: Priority-Level
avp_priority_level.doc: The priority level of the bearer, used in resource allocation decisions. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Origin: The value is part of the ARP, which is determined by the PCRF based on subscription data.

avp_pre_emption_capability.name: Pre-emption-Capability
avp_pre_emption_capability.doc: Indicates whether the bearer can preempt other bearers with lower priority. Origin: The value is part of the ARP, which is determined by the PCRF based on subscription data.

avp_pre_emption_vulnerability.name: Pre-emption-Vulnerability
avp_pre_emption_vulnerability.doc: Indicates whether the bearer is vulnerable to preemption by higher priority bearers. Origin: The value is part of the ARP, which is determined by the PCRF based on subscription data.

doc.summary_en: The PCRF sends a Credit-Control-Answer (CCA) to the PGW to acknowledge the termination of a Gx session. This message confirms that the policy and charging control session has been successfully closed, typically in response to a PGW-initiated termination request (CCR-T) triggered by events like PDN disconnection or UE detach.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is the PCRF's acknowledgment to the PGW's request to terminate a Gx session. It confirms the successful release of all resources and state associated with the session at the PCRF. (TS 29.212 §4.5.7 V18.1.0)

### Triggers
This CCA is sent in direct response to a Credit-Control-Request (CCR) with a CC-Request-Type AVP set to TERMINATION_REQUEST. The PGW initiates this termination procedure when the associated IP-CAN session is terminated. (TS 29.212 §4.5.7.1 V18.1.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the Gx session being terminated.
- **Result-Code / Experimental-Result-Code**: Indicates the success (e.g., DIAMETER_SUCCESS 2001) or failure of the termination request.
- **Origin-Host / Origin-Realm**: Identify the PCRF sending the answer.
- **CC-Request-Type**: Mirrored from the request, confirming the termination context.
- **CC-Request-Number**: Mirrored from the request for correlation.

### Error Handling
If the PGW receives a CCA with a failure Result-Code, it may retry the termination request or proceed with local resource cleanup, depending on the specific error and operator policy. If no CCA is received, the PGW may retransmit the CCR-T. (RFC 4006 §8.5)

### Ordering & State
This is the final message in a Gx session termination procedure. Upon sending this message, the PCRF deletes the session state. Upon receiving it, the PGW also finalizes the session closure. (TS 29.212 §4.5.7.2 V18.1.0)
doc.title: Credit-Control-Answer

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, used to correlate all messages belonging to the same session. Origin: Copied from the corresponding Credit-Control-Request to correlate the answer with the session.

avp_result_code.name: Result-Code
avp_result_code.doc: Indicates the outcome of the request. A value of 2001 (DIAMETER_SUCCESS) signifies successful termination. Origin: Set by the PCRF to indicate the outcome of the session termination request.

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that generated the message, in this case, the PCRF. Origin: Configured FQDN of the responding node, which is the PCRF in this case.

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The administrative realm of the Origin-Host. Origin: Configured realm of the responding node, the PCRF.

avp_auth_application_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_application_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Gx (16777238). Origin: Set to the Gx application identifier (16777238) as defined in the Diameter Gx specification.

avp_cc_request_type.name: CC-Request-Type
avp_cc_request_type.doc: Mirrors the value from the CCR, indicating the type of request. For termination, this is 3 (TERMINATION_REQUEST). Origin: Mirrored from the corresponding Credit-Control-Request which had a value of TERMINATION_REQUEST (3).

avp_cc_request_number.name: CC-Request-Number
avp_cc_request_number.doc: A sequence number for CCR messages within a session, mirrored in the CCA to match the request-answer pair. Origin: Mirrored from the corresponding Credit-Control-Request to match the request-answer pair.

avp_origin_state_id.name: Origin-State-Id
avp_origin_state_id.doc: A monotonically increasing value that represents the state of the origin host, used for failover detection. Origin: Generated by the PCRF to associate the message with its current state for failover purposes.

doc.summary_en: The PCRF sends a Credit-Control-Answer (CCA) with a CC-Request-Type of UPDATE_REQUEST to the PGW (PCEF) to acknowledge a CCR-Update. This message is used to provision, modify, or remove PCC rules, and to update QoS parameters for a specific IP-CAN session.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is the response from the Policy and Charging Rules Function (PCRF) to a Credit-Control-Request (Update) from the PGW. It is the primary mechanism for the PCRF to dynamically control the policy and charging enforcement at the PGW. (TS 29.212 §4.5.2 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
The PCRF sends this message in response to a received CCR-Update from the PGW. The CCR-Update may be triggered by events at the PGW, such as bearer modification, location change, or upon request from the PCRF via a Re-Auth-Request (RAR).

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Correlates the session.
- **Result-Code**: Indicates success (2001) or failure.
- **Charging-Rule-Install**: Contains new PCC rules to be installed at the PGW.
- **Charging-Rule-Remove**: Contains names of PCC rules to be removed.
- **Charging-Rule-Modify**: Contains modified definitions for existing PCC rules.
- **QoS-Information**: Provides updated QoS parameters for the default or a dedicated bearer.
- **Event-Trigger**: (Re-)arms triggers at the PGW for future policy interactions.

### Error Handling
If the request processing fails, the PCRF sets the Result-Code AVP to a value other than DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001). The specific error code (e.g., DIAMETER_AUTHORIZATION_REJECTED 5003) indicates the reason for failure. (RFC 4006 §8.1)
doc.title: Credit-Control-Answer

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, used to correlate all messages belonging to the same session. Origin: Copied from the corresponding request message to correlate the session.

avp_result_code.name: Result-Code
avp_result_code.doc: Indicates the outcome of the request. A value of 2001 signifies success. Origin: Set by the PCRF to indicate the outcome of processing the request.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_AUTHORIZATION_REJECTED: The request was rejected for authorization reasons. (RFC 6733)
DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY: The request could not be satisfied. (RFC 6733)

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that generated the message (the PCRF). Origin: The configured FQDN of the responding node (PCRF).

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that generated the message (the PCRF). Origin: The configured realm of the responding node (PCRF).

avp_auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Gx. Origin: A fixed value defined in the specification for the Gx application interface.

avp_cc_req_type.name: CC-Request-Type
avp_cc_req_type.doc: Mirrors the value from the CCR, indicating this is a response to an update request. Origin: Echoed from the corresponding Credit-Control-Request message.

avp_cc_req_num.name: CC-Request-Number
avp_cc_req_num.doc: A sequence number to match requests and answers within a session. Origin: Echoed from the corresponding Credit-Control-Request message to match the request/answer pair.

avp_charging_rule_install.name: Charging-Rule-Install
avp_charging_rule_install.doc: A grouped AVP that contains the PCC rules to be installed by the PGW. Origin: Determined by PCRF policy decisions based on subscriber data, network status, or AF requests.

avp_charging_rule_name.name: Charging-Rule-Name
avp_charging_rule_name.doc: The name of a PCC rule to be installed, modified, or removed. Origin: Defined by the PCRF policy configuration.

avp_qos_info.name: QoS-Information
avp_qos_info.doc: A grouped AVP that provides QoS parameters for a bearer. Origin: Determined by PCRF based on policy rules and subscription data to manage bearer QoS.

avp_qci.name: QoS-Class-Identifier
avp_qci.doc: Specifies the QoS Class Identifier (QCI) for the bearer. Origin: Derived from subscription data (HSS) or dynamic policy decisions at the PCRF.

avp_arp.name: Allocation-Retention-Priority
avp_arp.doc: Contains the priority level for allocating and retaining the bearer. Origin: Derived from subscription data (HSS) or dynamic policy decisions at the PCRF.

avp_event_trigger.name: Event-Trigger
avp_event_trigger.doc: An enumerated value that defines an event that will trigger a CCR-Update from the PGW. Origin: Set by the PCRF to instruct the PGW on which events should trigger a new policy interaction.
enum_doc: USAGE_REPORT: Triggered when usage thresholds are met. (TS 29.212)
PLMN_CHANGE: Triggered when the serving PLMN changes. (TS 29.212)

doc.summary_en: The PGW sends a Credit-Control-Request (CCR) with CC-Request-Type set to INITIAL_REQUEST to the PCRF to initiate a new IP-CAN session. This occurs when a UE establishes a new PDN connection, typically the default bearer. The PGW provides UE and session information (IMSI, IP address, APN, QoS) and requests Policy and Charging Control (PCC) rules from the PCRF.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is used by the PGW to establish a Gx session with the PCRF for a new IP-CAN session. It allows the PGW to provide session-related information to the PCRF and to request the initial set of PCC rules and event triggers that will govern the data flow for the UE's PDN connection. (TS 29.212 §4.5.5 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
The procedure is triggered when the PGW receives a Create Session Request from the SGW for a new PDN connection and needs to establish a corresponding Gx session with the PCRF. (TS 23.401 §5.3.2.1 V18.x.x)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the Gx session.
- **CC-Request-Type**: Set to `INITIAL_REQUEST` (1).
- **Subscription-Id**: Contains the UE's identity (e.g., IMSI).
- **Framed-IP-Address**: The IP address assigned to the UE.
- **Called-Station-Id**: The APN for the PDN connection.
- **User-Equipment-Info**: Contains the UE's IMEI.
- **RAT-Type**: Indicates the radio access technology in use (e.g., EUTRAN).
- **QoS-Information**: Provides the QoS parameters of the default bearer.

### Successful Outcome
Upon successful processing, the PCRF responds with a Credit-Control-Answer (CCA) containing the initial PCC rules (Charging-Rule-Install AVP) and event triggers (Event-Trigger AVP) for the session. (TS 29.212 §4.5.6 V18.3.0)

### Error Handling
If the PCRF cannot process the request, it returns a CCA with a Result-Code AVP indicating the failure reason (e.g., DIAMETER_AUTHORIZATION_REJECTED). The PGW would then typically reject the PDN connection establishment. (RFC 4006 §8.1 V18.x.x)
doc.title: Credit-Control-Request (Initial)

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, generated by the PGW. It remains constant for the life of the Gx session. (RFC 6733 §8.8) Origin: Generated by the PGW to uniquely identify this Gx session for its entire lifetime.

avp_auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Gx. The value 16777238 is assigned by 3GPP for the Gx application. (TS 29.212 §4.4.1) Origin: This is a fixed value defined in 3GPP specifications for the Gx Diameter application.

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity (FQDN) of the node sending the message, in this case, the PGW. (RFC 6733 §6.3) Origin: This value is based on the configured identity of the sending PGW node.

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The administrative domain of the Origin-Host. (RFC 6733 §6.4) Origin: This value is based on the configured administrative domain of the sending PGW node.

avp_dest_realm.name: Destination-Realm
avp_dest_realm.doc: The administrative domain of the destination node (PCRF). This is used for message routing. (RFC 6733 §6.5) Origin: Determined by the PGW's configuration for routing Gx messages to the correct PCRF.

avp_cc_req_type.name: CC-Request-Type
avp_cc_req_type.doc: Indicates the type of credit-control request. For the first message in a session, this is set to INITIAL_REQUEST (1). (RFC 4006 §8.3) Origin: Set to the spec-defined enum value 'INITIAL_REQUEST' because this is the first message establishing the Gx session.

avp_cc_req_num.name: CC-Request-Number
avp_cc_req_num.doc: A sequence number for requests within a session, starting at 0 for the initial request. (RFC 4006 §8.4) Origin: Set to 0 as defined by the specification for the first request in any credit-control session.

avp_subscription_id.name: Subscription-Id
avp_subscription_id.doc: A grouped AVP containing the identity of the end user. (TS 29.212 §5.3.1) Origin: This information is received by the PGW from the SGW/MME, which in turn gets it from the UE or HSS during the attach procedure.

avp_sub_id_type.name: Subscription-Id-Type
avp_sub_id_type.doc: Specifies the type of identifier in Subscription-Id-Data. Common values are END_USER_IMSI (1) or END_USER_E164 (0 for MSISDN). (TS 29.212 §5.3.2) Origin: Set to a spec-defined enum value corresponding to the type of identifier provided for the subscriber.

avp_sub_id_data.name: Subscription-Id-Data
avp_sub_id_data.doc: The actual user identifier, such as the IMSI. (TS 29.212 §5.3.3) Origin: The value is the subscriber's permanent identity (IMSI), which is provided to the PGW during the session setup.

avp_framed_ip.name: Framed-IP-Address
avp_framed_ip.doc: The IPv4 address assigned to the UE for this PDN connection. (TS 29.212 §5.3.7) Origin: Allocated by the PGW from its local address pool or via RADIUS/DHCP for the UE's PDN connection.

avp_ue_info.name: User-Equipment-Info
avp_ue_info.doc: A grouped AVP containing information about the user's terminal, typically the IMEI. (TS 29.212 §5.3.10) Origin: The UE provides its equipment identifier to the network during the attach procedure, and it is forwarded to the PGW.

avp_ue_info_type.name: User-Equipment-Info-Type
avp_ue_info_type.doc: Specifies the type of equipment identifier. IMEI (0) is commonly used. (TS 29.212 §5.3.11) Origin: Set to the spec-defined enum value 'IMEI' to indicate the type of the accompanying equipment identifier.

avp_ue_info_val.name: User-Equipment-Info-Value
avp_ue_info_val.doc: The value of the user equipment identifier, e.g., the IMEI. (TS 29.212 §5.3.12) Origin: This is the actual IMEI of the user's device, provided to the network during initial access.

avp_called_station_id.name: Called-Station-Id
avp_called_station_id.doc: Contains the Access Point Name (APN) for the PDN connection. (TS 29.212 §5.3.17) Origin: This is the APN requested by the UE or selected by the network based on the UE's subscription.

avp_rat_type.name: RAT-Type
avp_rat_type.doc: Indicates the Radio Access Technology being used by the UE. EUTRAN (1004) is used for LTE. (TS 29.212 §5.3.27) Origin: Signaled from the radio access network through the MME and SGW to the PGW during session establishment.

doc.summary_en: The PGW sends a Credit-Control-Request (CCR) with a CC-Request-Type of TERMINATION_REQUEST to the PCRF to indicate that an IP-CAN session has been terminated.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is used by the PGW (as the Credit Control Client) to inform the PCRF (Credit Control Server) about the termination of a Gx session, which corresponds to the termination of a user's IP-CAN session (e.g., a PDN connection). This allows the PCRF to release any resources and state associated with that session. (TS 29.212 §5.2.1 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
The procedure is triggered by any event leading to the termination of an IP-CAN session at the PGW. Common triggers include:
- UE-initiated detach or PDN disconnection.
- MME/SGSN-initiated detach.
- Administrative reasons at the PGW.
- Loss of bearer context.
(TS 29.212 §4.5.4 V18.4.0)

### Key IEs
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the Gx session.
- **CC-Request-Type**: Must be set to `TERMINATION_REQUEST (3)`.
- **Termination-Cause**: Provides the reason for the session termination (e.g., `DIAMETER_LOGOUT`, `DIAMETER_SERVICE_NOT_PROVIDED`).
- **Subscription-Id**: Identifies the user (e.g., IMSI).
- **User-Equipment-Info**: Provides the UE's identity (e.g., IMEISV).

### Error Handling
The PCRF responds with a Credit-Control-Answer (CCA). A successful termination is indicated by a Result-Code of `DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001)`. If the PCRF cannot process the request, it may return an error code. Since the session is already terminated on the PGW side, this response is mainly for logging and state cleanup at the PCRF. (RFC 6733 §8.16)
doc.title: Credit-Control-Request

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Gx session, including the originator's FQDN and session-specific numbers. Origin: Generated by the PGW to uniquely identify this specific Gx credit-control session for the user's PDN connection.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the originating node (PGW). Origin: Configured identity of the PGW node sending the request.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the originating node (PGW). Origin: Configured network realm of the PGW node.

destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
destination_realm.doc: The realm of the destination node (PCRF). Origin: Determined by the PGW, typically through configuration or DNS/DRA procedures, to route to the correct PCRF.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Gx (16777238). Origin: A fixed value (16777238) defined in the 3GPP specification for the Gx interface.

cc_request_type.name: CC-Request-Type
cc_request_type.doc: Indicates the type of credit-control request. For session termination, this is set to TERMINATION_REQUEST. Origin: Set to a spec-defined enum value (3) because this message is specifically for terminating a session.

cc_request_number.name: CC-Request-Number
cc_request_number.doc: A sequence number for requests within a session, incremented for each new request. Origin: Maintained and incremented by the PGW for each request sent within this Gx session.

destination_host.name: Destination-Host
destination_host.doc: The FQDN of the destination node (PCRF). This AVP is optional and used if the message is to be routed to a specific PCRF instance. Origin: Determined by the PGW if a specific PCRF instance is targeted, otherwise omitted to allow routing by a DRA.

termination_cause.name: Termination-Cause
termination_cause.doc: Specifies the reason for the session termination. Origin: Set by the PGW based on the specific event that triggered the PDN connection release (e.g., UE detach, radio link loss).

subscription_id.name: Subscription-Id
subscription_id.doc: Contains the user's identity, such as IMSI or MSISDN. Origin: Retrieved from the user's context stored at the PGW, originally provided during the session establishment.

subscription_id_type.name: Subscription-Id-Type
subscription_id_type.doc: Specifies the type of identifier in Subscription-Id. Origin: A spec-defined enum value corresponding to the type of identifier (e.g., IMSI) being sent.

subscription_id_data.name: Subscription-Id-Data
subscription_id_data.doc: The actual user identifier string (e.g., the IMSI value). Origin: The value of the user's identifier stored in the PGW's session context.

user_equipment_info.name: User-Equipment-Info
user_equipment_info.doc: Contains the UE's identity, such as IMEI or IMEISV. Origin: Retrieved from the UE's context at the PGW, provided during the attach/PDN connection setup procedure.

ue_info_type.name: User-Equipment-Info-Type
ue_info_type.doc: Specifies the type of UE identity. Origin: A spec-defined enum value corresponding to the type of UE identifier (e.g., IMEISV) being sent.

ue_info_value.name: User-Equipment-Info-Value
ue_info_value.doc: The actual UE identifier string. Origin: The value of the UE's identifier stored in the PGW's session context.

rat_type.name: RAT-Type
rat_type.doc: Indicates the Radio Access Technology type being used. Origin: Reflects the last known RAT type for the user's session as maintained by the PGW.

user_location_info.name: User-Location-Info
user_location_info.doc: Provides the last known location of the UE (e.g., TAI, ECGI). The encoding follows TS 29.061. Origin: Represents the last location information reported to the PGW for this session.

called_station_id.name: Called-Station-Id
called_station_id.doc: Identifies the Access Point Name (APN) for the session. Origin: The APN value associated with the PDN connection being terminated, stored in the PGW's session context.

doc.summary_en: The PGW (PCEF) sends a Credit-Control-Request with CC-Request-Type set to UPDATE_REQUEST to the PCRF to report a specific event that occurred for an established IP-CAN session, request PCC rules, or report the status of PCC rules.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is used by the PCEF to inform the PCRF about events that have occurred, such as changes in UE location, QoS, or access technology, which may require new or modified PCC rules. It is also used to report usage against monitored charging keys. (TS 29.212 §4.5.4 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
Common triggers include: UE IP address allocation/release, serving network change, RAT change, user location change, QoS change, or when usage thresholds are reached for monitored services. (TS 29.212 §4.4.5.2 V18.3.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the Gx session.
- **CC-Request-Type**: Set to `UPDATE_REQUEST` (2).
- **Event-Trigger**: Indicates the specific event that triggered this message.
- **Subscription-Id**: Identifies the user (e.g., IMSI).
- **User-Location-Info**: Provides the UE's current location (e.g., TAI/ECGI).
- **Charging-Rule-Report**: Reports usage and status for installed PCC rules.

### Error Handling
The PCRF responds with a Credit-Control-Answer (CCA). If the request is invalid or the user profile is not found, the PCRF may return a CCA with a specific Result-Code AVP (e.g., DIAMETER_UNKNOWN_SESSION_ID, DIAMETER_AUTHORIZATION_REJECTED) and may instruct the PGW to terminate the session. (RFC 4006 §8.1 V18.3.0)
doc.title: Credit-Control-Request

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, formatted as <host>;<high32>;<low32>[;<optional>]. It remains constant for the lifetime of the Gx session. Origin: Generated by the PGW at the start of the Gx session to uniquely identify it.

avp_auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Gx. The value 16777238 is assigned by IANA for the 3GPP Gx application. Origin: A fixed value defined in TS 29.212 for the Gx application interface.

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that originated the Diameter message (the PGW). Origin: The configured FQDN of the sending node (PGW).

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated the message. Origin: The configured realm of the sending node (PGW).

avp_destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
avp_destination_realm.doc: The realm where the message is to be routed (the PCRF's realm). Origin: Determined by the PGW via configuration or DNS/DRA lookup to route the message to the correct PCRF.

avp_cc_req_type.name: CC-Request-Type
avp_cc_req_type.doc: Indicates the type of credit-control request. For an update, this is set to UPDATE_REQUEST. Origin: Set to a specific enum value (2) because this message's purpose is to update an existing session.
enum_doc: INITIAL_REQUEST - Used to initiate a session.
UPDATE_REQUEST - Used to update an existing session.
TERMINATION_REQUEST - Used to terminate a session.
EVENT_REQUEST - Used for one-time event-based credit control.

avp_cc_req_num.name: CC-Request-Number
avp_cc_req_num.doc: A sequence number for the request within a session, starting at 0 for the initial request and incrementing for each subsequent request. Origin: A counter maintained by the PGW for this specific session, incremented for each new request.

avp_subscription_id.name: Subscription-Id
avp_subscription_id.doc: A grouped AVP containing the identity of the end user. Origin: Contains user identity information (like IMSI) that the PGW received during the UE's network attachment procedure.

avp_subscription_id_type.name: Subscription-Id-Type
avp_subscription_id_type.doc: Defines the type of identifier present in Subscription-Id-Data. Origin: Set to a specific enum value corresponding to the type of identifier available in the PGW's session context.

avp_subscription_id_data.name: Subscription-Id-Data
avp_subscription_id_data.doc: The actual user identifier (e.g., the IMSI). Origin: The specific user identifier value stored in the PGW's session context for this UE.

avp_event_trigger.name: Event-Trigger
avp_event_trigger.doc: Indicates the specific event that caused the PCEF to send this request. (TS 29.212 Table 4.4.5.2-1). Origin: The value corresponds to a specific event (e.g., location change) detected by the PGW, as defined in the 3GPP specification.
enum_doc: USER_LOCATION_CHANGE - The UE's user location has changed.
RAT_CHANGE - The Radio Access Technology has changed.
TFT_CHANGE - The Traffic Flow Template has changed.

avp_user_location_info.name: User-Location-Info
avp_user_location_info.doc: A grouped AVP providing the user's location. Origin: Contains location information (e.g., TAI, ECGI) reported to the PGW from the serving network (e.g., SGW).

avp_3gpp_user_loc_info.name: 3GPP-User-Location-Info
avp_3gpp_user_loc_info.doc: The value of the 3GPP-User-Location-Info AVP is a TAI and/or ECGI, encoded as an OCTET STRING. Origin: The raw location data provided by the access network and forwarded to the PGW.

avp_charging_rule_report.name: Charging-Rule-Report
avp_charging_rule_report.doc: A grouped AVP used by the PCEF to report the status of PCC rules. Origin: Generated by the PGW to report on the state or usage of PCC rules previously installed by the PCRF.

avp_charging_rule_name.name: Charging-Rule-Name
avp_charging_rule_name.doc: The name of the PCC rule for which this report is being generated. Origin: The identifier of a specific PCC rule that the PCRF previously provided to the PGW.

avp_pcc_rule_status.name: PCC-Rule-Status
avp_pcc_rule_status.doc: Indicates the status of the PCC rule(s) identified in the Charging-Rule-Name AVP. Origin: Reflects the current operational state of the PCC rule at the PGW (e.g., active, inactive).
enum_doc: INACTIVE - The PCC rule is installed but not active.
ACTIVE - The PCC rule is active.
TEMPORARILY_INACTIVE - The PCC rule is temporarily disabled.

doc.summary_en: The Equipment-Check-Answer (ECA) is sent by the Equipment Identity Register (EIR) to the Mobility Management Entity (MME) in response to an Equipment-Check-Request (ECR). It provides the status of the mobile equipment, indicating whether it is whitelisted, blacklisted, or greylisted.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The ECA command is used by the EIR to inform the MME about the status of a given piece of equipment, identified by its IMEI. This procedure is part of the S13 interface functionality. (TS 29.272 §5.2.1.2.1 V18.1.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when the EIR receives an Equipment-Check-Request (ECR) command from an MME or SGSN. The EIR performs a lookup in its database for the IMEI provided in the ECR and formulates the ECA with the result. (TS 29.272 §5.2.1.2.1 V18.1.0)

### Key IEs
- **Session-Id**: Correlates the answer with the request.
- **Result-Code / Experimental-Result**: Indicates the outcome of the operation. A successful check will return DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001).
- **Equipment-Status**: The core information of the response, indicating if the UE is allowed on the network from an equipment perspective.
- **Origin-Host / Origin-Realm**: Identifies the responding EIR.

### Error Handling
Failures are indicated by the Result-Code or Experimental-Result AVP. For example, DIAMETER_UNKNOWN_SESSION_ID (5002) is returned if the Session-Id is not recognized. Application-specific errors are conveyed in the Experimental-Result AVP. (TS 29.272 §5.2.1.2.2 V18.1.0)
doc.title: Equipment-Check-Answer

session_id.doc: Uniquely identifies the session, copied from the corresponding ECR message. Origin: Copied from the corresponding request message.

vendor_specific_app_id.doc: Groups AVPs related to the 3GPP S13/S13' application. Origin: This grouped AVP is mandated by the Diameter application definition in the specification.

vendor_id.doc: Identifies the vendor as 3GPP. The value 10415 is assigned to 3GPP. Origin: Spec-defined constant for 3GPP (TS 32.299).

auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as S13/S13'. The value is 16777252. Origin: Spec-defined constant for the S13/S13' application (TS 29.272).

auth_session_state.doc: Indicates that no session state is maintained on the server (EIR) for this session. Origin: Spec-defined fixed value for this stateless procedure (TS 29.272).
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0) - State is maintained for the session.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED (1) - No state is maintained for the session.

origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the node that originated this message (the EIR). Origin: The FQDN or DiameterIdentity of the sending node (EIR).

origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated this message (the EIR). Origin: The realm of the sending node (EIR).

result_code.doc: Indicates the success or failure of the request at the base protocol level. 2001 indicates success. Origin: Determined by the outcome of processing the request at the Diameter base protocol level.

equipment_status.doc: Indicates the status of the equipment as determined by the EIR. Origin: Determined by the EIR's database lookup for the provided IMEI.
enum_doc: BLACKLISTED (1) - The equipment is barred from service.
GREYLISTED (2) - The equipment is being tracked.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends a ME-Identity-Check-Request (ECR) to the EIR to verify the status of a mobile equipment's identity (IMEI or IMEISV). This is part of the equipment validation procedure, typically performed during Attach or Tracking Area Update procedures.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The ME-Identity-Check-Request (ECR) command is sent from an MME or SGSN to the EIR to request the mobile equipment status. (TS 29.272 §5.2.1.3.1 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when the MME receives an IMEI or IMEISV from a UE during procedures such as Attach or Tracking Area Update, and local policy requires an equipment check. (TS 23.401 §5.3.2.1 V18.3.0)

### Key IEs
- **ME-Identity**: Contains the IMEI or IMEISV of the equipment to be checked.
- **User-Name**: May contain the IMSI of the subscriber using the equipment.
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies this Diameter session.

### Successful Outcome
The EIR responds with a ME-Identity-Check-Answer (ECA) command indicating the equipment status (e.g., whitelist, blacklist, greylist) in the Equipment-Status AVP.

### Error Handling
If the EIR cannot process the request, it returns an ECA with an appropriate Result-Code AVP (e.g., DIAMETER_UNKNOWN_SESSION_ID, DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY). The MME may proceed with the UE procedure without the equipment check result, based on local configuration. (TS 29.272 §5.2.1.3.2 V18.3.0)
doc.title: ME-Identity-Check-Request

diameter_header.name: Diameter Header
diameter_header.doc: Standard Diameter header containing version, length, flags, command code, application ID, and hop-by-hop/end-to-end identifiers. Origin: The fields are populated by the Diameter stack based on the message being sent and the session context.

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, used to correlate messages. It is generated by the client (MME). (RFC 6733 §8.8). Origin: Generated by the sending node (MME) to uniquely identify this transaction.

vendor_specific_app_id.name: Vendor-Specific-Application-Id
vendor_specific_app_id.doc: Groups the Vendor-Id and the 3GPP-specific Application-Id for the S13 interface. (RFC 6733 §8.15). Origin: This is a container AVP whose children are determined by the specific Diameter application being used.

vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier for 3GPP (10415). (TS 29.272 §5.2.3). Origin: This is a fixed value assigned to 3GPP by IANA.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the S13 Diameter application (16777252). (TS 29.272 §5.2.3). Origin: This is a fixed value defined in the 3GPP specification for the S13 interface.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether state is maintained for the session. For S13, this is always set to NO_STATE_MAINTAINED. (RFC 6733 §8.16). Origin: Set to a specific enum value (1) as required by the stateless nature of this procedure.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED: State is maintained for this session.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED: State is not maintained for this session.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity (FQDN) of the node that originated the request (MME). (RFC 6733 §6.3). Origin: This value is based on the configured FQDN of the sending node (MME).

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated the request (MME). (RFC 6733 §6.4). Origin: This value is based on the configured network domain of the sending node (MME).

destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
destination_realm.doc: The realm to which the request is to be routed (EIR's realm). (RFC 6733 §6.6). Origin: This value is based on the configured network domain of the destination node (EIR).

me_identity.name: ME-Identity
me_identity.doc: Contains the Mobile Equipment identity, which can be an IMEI or IMEISV. (TS 29.272 §5.2.3.14). Origin: The value is the IMEI or IMEISV provided by the UE to the MME during an access procedure.

user_name.name: User-Name
user_name.doc: The identity of the user, typically the IMSI. (RFC 6733 §8.14). Origin: The value is the IMSI provided by the UE to the MME during an access procedure.

destination_host.name: Destination-Host
destination_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity (FQDN) of the destination node (EIR). Used for direct routing if known. (RFC 6733 §6.5). Origin: This value is based on the configured FQDN of the destination node (EIR), if known.

doc.summary_en: The Re-Auth-Answer (RAA) is sent by the PGW-C (TDF) to the PCRF in response to a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) message. It acknowledges the actions taken on PCC/ADC rules (e.g., installation, modification, removal) and reports the outcome.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The RAA message is used by the PGW-C to inform the PCRF about the result of a PCRF-initiated Gx session modification. This includes acknowledging the enforcement of new or modified PCC rules, reporting on event triggers, or indicating failures in rule operations. (TS 29.212 §5.3.2 V17.8.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when a PGW-C receives a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) from the PCRF. The PGW-C processes the instructions within the RAR (e.g., activating/deactivating rule sets) and formulates the RAA to report the result. (TS 29.212 §5.3.1 V17.8.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Correlates the answer with the ongoing Gx session.
- **Result-Code / Experimental-Result-Code**: Indicates the success or failure of the operation requested in the RAR. A value of DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001) indicates success.
- **Origin-Host / Origin-Realm**: Identifies the PGW-C sending the response.
- **Charging-Rule-Report**: A grouped AVP used to report the status of individual PCC rules that were acted upon. It can indicate if a rule is active, inactive, or if its enforcement failed, along with a failure code.
- **Event-Trigger**: Reports event triggers that have been met at the PGW-C.

### Error Handling
If the PGW-C cannot successfully process the RAR, it sets the Result-Code AVP to an appropriate failure value (e.g., DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY). The Charging-Rule-Report AVP can also contain rule-specific failure codes if only certain operations failed. (TS 29.212 §5.3.2 V17.8.0)
doc.title: Re-Auth-Answer

session_id.doc: The Session-Id AVP (AVP Code 263) is a unique identifier for the Gx session and is used to correlate messages. It is copied from the corresponding request. Origin: Copied from the corresponding request message (RAR) to correlate the session.

result_code.doc: The Result-Code AVP (AVP Code 268) indicates the outcome of the request. 2001 (DIAMETER_SUCCESS) means the request was successfully handled. Origin: Set by the PGW-C to indicate the outcome of processing the Re-Auth-Request.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY - The request could not be satisfied.

origin_host.doc: The Origin-Host AVP (AVP Code 264) contains the DiameterIdentity (FQDN) of the node that generated the message, in this case, the PGW-C. Origin: The configured FQDN of the responding node (PGW-C).

origin_realm.doc: The Origin-Realm AVP (AVP Code 296) contains the realm of the node that generated the message. Origin: The configured realm of the responding node (PGW-C).

charging_rule_report.doc: The Charging-Rule-Report AVP (AVP Code 1018) is a grouped AVP used to report the status of PCC rules that the PCRF requested to be enforced, modified, or removed. Origin: Generated by the PGW-C to report the status of PCC rules as requested by the PCRF.

pcc_rule_status.doc: The PCC-Rule-Status AVP (AVP Code 1019) indicates the status of a specific PCC rule. It is included within a Charging-Rule-Report. Origin: Set by the PGW-C to indicate the status of a specific PCC rule (e.g., active, inactive).
enum_doc: ACTIVE (0) - The PCC rule is active.
INACTIVE (1) - The PCC rule is inactive.
TEMPORARILY INACTIVE (2) - The PCC rule is temporarily inactive.
RULE CONFLICT (3) - The PCC rule could not be enforced due to a conflict.

rule_failure_code.doc: The Rule-Failure-Code AVP (AVP Code 1031) is included within a Charging-Rule-Report when a PCC rule could not be successfully enforced, indicating the reason for the failure. Origin: Included by the PGW-C when a rule operation fails, indicating the reason for failure.
enum_doc: UNKNOWN_RULE_NAME (1) - The rule name is unknown.
RATING_GROUP_ERROR (2) - The rating group is invalid.
SERVICE_ID_ERROR (3) - The service identifier is invalid.
RESOURCES_LIMITATION (5) - Insufficient resources to enforce the rule.
MAX_NR_BEARERS_REACHED (6) - Maximum number of bearers reached.
UNKNOWN_BEARER_ID (7) - The bearer ID is unknown.
MISSING_BEARER_ID (8) - The bearer ID is missing.
MISSING_GATING_KEY (9) - The gating key is missing.
RELOCATION_ONGOING (10) - A relocation is in progress.
BEARER_NOT_AUTHORIZED (11) - The bearer is not authorized.
TRAFFIC_MAPPING_INFO_REJECTED (12) - Traffic mapping info was rejected.
PCC_RULE_EVENT (15) - A PCC rule event occurred.
INVALID_SERVICE_INFO (16) - The service information is invalid.

doc.summary_en: The PCRF sends a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) to the PGW-C (PCEF) to initiate a re-authentication and re-authorization process for an established Gx session. This is typically used to provision, modify, or remove PCC (Policy and Charging Control) rules, or to update QoS and event triggers based on changes in subscription, operator policy, or application function (AF) requests.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Re-Auth-Request (RAR) command, indicated by the Command-Code field set to 258 and the 'R' bit set in the Command Flags field, is sent by the PCRF to the PCEF in order to request the re-authentication and re-authorization of an authorized Gx session. (TS 29.212 §4.5.6 V17.4.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered by the PCRF due to an internal policy decision, a change in the user's subscription information received from the SPR, or a request from an Application Function (AF). For example, an AF might request a specific QoS for a new media flow, prompting the PCRF to install a new PCC rule at the PGW-C.

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Identifies the Gx session.
- **Re-Auth-Request-Type**: Specifies the reason for the re-authentication (e.g., AUTH_REQUEST).
- **Charging-Rule-Install**: Contains new PCC rules to be installed at the PCEF.
- **Charging-Rule-Remove**: Contains existing PCC rules to be removed from the PCEF.
- **QoS-Information**: Provides updated QoS parameters for a bearer.
- **Event-Trigger**: Instructs the PCEF to report on the occurrence of specific events.

### Error Handling
The PGW-C responds with a Re-Auth-Answer (RAA). If the PGW-C cannot process the request, it will return an RAA with a Result-Code AVP indicating the failure reason (e.g., DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY). (TS 29.212 §4.5.7 V17.4.0)
doc.title: Re-Auth-Request

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: Uniquely identifies the Gx session. It must be globally and eternally unique. Origin: Generated by the PCRF at the start of the session and used in all subsequent messages for that session.

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the node that originated the message (the PCRF). Origin: Configured on the PCRF node as its Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN).

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the originating node (the PCRF). Origin: Configured on the PCRF node, representing its administrative domain.

avp_destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
avp_destination_realm.doc: The realm of the destination node (the PGW-C). Origin: Learned by the PCRF from the Destination-Realm AVP in the initial CCR message from the PGW-C.

avp_destination_host.name: Destination-Host
avp_destination_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the destination node (the PGW-C). Origin: Learned by the PCRF from the Origin-Host AVP in the initial CCR message from the PGW-C.

avp_auth_application_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_application_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Gx. Origin: Set to the fixed value 16777238, as defined for the Gx application in TS 29.212.

avp_re_auth_request_type.name: Re-Auth-Request-Type
avp_re_auth_request_type.doc: Indicates the type of the re-authentication request. For Gx, this is typically AUTH_REQUEST. Origin: Set by the PCRF based on the triggering event; for Gx policy modifications, AUTH_REQUEST is the standard value.
enum_doc: AUTHORIZE_ONLY (0) - The request is for authorization only.
AUTH_AND_AUTHORIZE (1) - The request is for authentication and authorization.
AUTHORIZE_AUTHENTICATED (2) - The request is for authorization of an already authenticated user.

avp_charging_rule_install.name: Charging-Rule-Install
avp_charging_rule_install.doc: A grouped AVP that contains the PCC rules to be installed by the PCEF. Origin: Generated by the PCRF based on a policy decision trigger, such as a subscription change or an AF request.

avp_charging_rule_definition.name: Charging-Rule-Definition
avp_charging_rule_definition.doc: A grouped AVP that defines a PCC rule, including its name, filters, QoS, and charging parameters. Origin: Constructed by the PCRF according to its configured policies and the specific requirements of the service or subscription.

avp_charging_rule_name.name: Charging-Rule-Name
avp_charging_rule_name.doc: The name of the PCC rule, used to uniquely identify it. Origin: Assigned by the PCRF from its policy configuration or dynamically generated.

avp_flow_information.name: Flow-Information
avp_flow_information.doc: A grouped AVP containing packet filters used to identify traffic flows for the PCC rule. Origin: Defined within the PCRF's policy configuration, often based on service data flow templates or information from an AF.

avp_charging_rule_remove.name: Charging-Rule-Remove
avp_charging_rule_remove.doc: A grouped AVP that contains the names of PCC rules to be removed by the PCEF. Origin: Populated by the PCRF when a policy decision dictates that one or more active PCC rules are no longer valid.

avp_event_trigger.name: Event-Trigger
avp_event_trigger.doc: Informs the PCEF of events that should be reported to the PCRF. Origin: Set by the PCRF to subscribe to specific network events (e.g., location change, QoS change) needed for dynamic policy control.
enum_doc: SGSN_CHANGE (0) - Report a change of SGSN.
QOS_CHANGE (1) - Report a change in QoS.
USAGE_REPORT (33) - Report usage according to thresholds.

doc.summary_en: The PCRF sends a Credit-Control-Answer (CCA) to the PGW-C (PCEF) to provision the initial Policy and Charging Control (PCC) rules and QoS parameters for a newly established IP-CAN session. This message is a response to a Credit-Control-Request (CCR) with a CC-Request-Type of INITIAL_REQUEST.
doc.steps:
1. The PGW-C (PCEF) detects the establishment of a new IP-CAN session (e.g., PDN connection activation) and sends a CCR (Initial) to the PCRF. (TS 29.212 §4.5.1)
2. The PCRF receives the CCR, identifies the user's subscription data (e.g., from the SPR), and makes policy decisions.
3. The PCRF constructs the CCA message, including the authorized QoS, PCC rules to be installed (via Charging-Rule-Install AVP), and event triggers that instruct the PCEF when to re-request policies. (TS 29.212 §4.5.2)
4. The PGW-C receives the CCA, installs the provided PCC rules, and applies the authorized QoS to the IP-CAN session. It then starts enforcing the policies for user traffic.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is used by the Policy and Charging Rules Function (PCRF) to provide initial policy and charging control decisions to the Policy and Charging Enforcement Function (PCEF) located in the PGW-C. It establishes the rules of engagement for a user's data session. (TS 29.212 §4.5.2 V17.8.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when the PCRF responds to a Credit-Control-Request (CCR) with a CC-Request-Type value of 'INITIAL_REQUEST'. (TS 29.212 §4.5.2 V17.8.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the Gx session.
- **Result-Code**: Indicates the success or failure of the operation. A value of DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001) indicates that the PCRF has successfully authorized the session.
- **Charging-Rule-Install**: A grouped AVP that contains the PCC rules to be installed at the PCEF.
- **QoS-Information**: Specifies the authorized Quality of Service for the IP-CAN session.
- **Event-Trigger**: Informs the PCEF about events that should trigger a new CCR to the PCRF.

### Error Handling
If the PCRF cannot authorize the session, it will return a CCA with a 'Result-Code' AVP indicating the failure reason (e.g., DIAMETER_AUTHORIZATION_REJECTED 5003). The PGW-C must then reject the IP-CAN session establishment. (TS 29.212 §4.5.2 V17.8.0)
doc.title: Credit-Control-Answer

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, used to correlate messages. Origin: Generated by the PGW-C in the initial request and copied by the PCRF in the response.

result_code.name: Result-Code
result_code.doc: Indicates the outcome of the request. 2001 signifies success. Origin: Set by the PCRF to indicate the result of the policy authorization decision.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_AUTHORIZATION_REJECTED: The request was rejected by the PCRF.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that generated the message (the PCRF). Origin: Configured on the PCRF node.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that generated the message. Origin: Configured on the PCRF node.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Gx. Origin: A spec-defined value for the Gx application (TS 29.212).

cc_request_type.name: CC-Request-Type
cc_request_type.doc: Mirrors the type from the CCR, indicating this is a response to an initial request. Origin: Copied from the corresponding CCR message.

cc_request_number.name: CC-Request-Number
cc_request_number.doc: A sequence number for requests within a session, starting at 0. Origin: Copied from the corresponding CCR message.

origin_state_id.name: Origin-State-Id
origin_state_id.doc: A monotonically increasing number used to detect node reboots and handle failover. Origin: Maintained and incremented by the PCRF for session management.

charging_rule_install.name: Charging-Rule-Install
charging_rule_install.doc: A grouped AVP containing the PCC rules to be installed by the PCEF. Origin: Determined by the PCRF based on subscription data, operator policies, and service information.

charging_rule_definition.name: Charging-Rule-Definition
charging_rule_definition.doc: A grouped AVP that defines a single PCC rule. Origin: Constructed by the PCRF to define a specific policy for a service data flow.

charging_rule_name.name: Charging-Rule-Name
charging_rule_name.doc: The name of the PCC rule being installed. Origin: Assigned by the PCRF to uniquely identify a PCC rule.

qos_info_rule.name: QoS-Information
qos_info_rule.doc: Specifies the QoS parameters to be applied for service data flows matching this specific rule, overriding session-level QoS. Origin: Determined by the PCRF for the specific service associated with this PCC rule.

qos_info_session.name: QoS-Information
qos_info_session.doc: Specifies the default QoS parameters for the entire IP-CAN session. Origin: Determined by the PCRF based on the subscriber's default APN or session-level policies.

qci.name: QoS-Class-Identifier
qci.doc: The QoS Class Identifier, which defines a set of packet forwarding treatments (e.g., scheduling weights, admission thresholds). Origin: Selected by the PCRF based on the service requirements and subscription profile.

max_bw_ul.name: Max-Requested-Bandwidth-UL
max_bw_ul.doc: Maximum requested bandwidth in bits per second for the uplink direction. Origin: Determined by the PCRF based on subscription limits (AMBR) or service-specific policies.

max_bw_dl.name: Max-Requested-Bandwidth-DL
max_bw_dl.doc: Maximum requested bandwidth in bits per second for the downlink direction. Origin: Determined by the PCRF based on subscription limits (AMBR) or service-specific policies.

gbr_ul.name: Guaranteed-Bitrate-UL
gbr_ul.doc: Guaranteed bitrate in bits per second for the uplink direction for GBR bearers. Origin: Set by the PCRF for services requiring guaranteed bandwidth, based on policy.

gbr_dl.name: Guaranteed-Bitrate-DL
gbr_dl.doc: Guaranteed bitrate in bits per second for the downlink direction for GBR bearers. Origin: Set by the PCRF for services requiring guaranteed bandwidth, based on policy.

arp.name: Allocation-Retention-Priority
arp.doc: Contains the priority level, pre-emption capability, and pre-emption vulnerability for resource allocation. Origin: Determined by the PCRF based on subscription information and operator policy for bearer handling.

event_trigger.name: Event-Trigger
event_trigger.doc: Informs the PCEF of events that shall trigger a new interaction with the PCRF. Origin: Set by the PCRF to manage the lifecycle of the policy session and ensure policies are updated when conditions change.
enum_doc: QOS_CHANGE: Report a change in QoS.
USAGE_REPORT: Report when usage thresholds are reached.
DEFAULT_EPS_BEARER_QOS_CHANGE: Report a change in the default bearer's QoS.

doc.summary_en: The PGW-C (PCEF) sends a Re-Auth-Answer (RAA) to the PCRF to acknowledge a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) and report the outcome of the requested PCC/QoS rule operations.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Re-Auth-Answer (RAA) command, indicated by the Command-Code field set to 258 and the 'R' bit cleared in the Command Flags field, is sent by the PCEF to the PCRF in response to the Re-Auth-Request command. (TS 29.212 §4.5.7.2 V17.8.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when a PGW-C (PCEF) receives a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) from a PCRF. The PGW-C processes the instructions within the RAR (e.g., install, modify, or remove PCC rules) and formulates the RAA to report the result. (TS 29.212 §4.5.7.1 V17.8.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Correlates the answer with the original request.
- **Result-Code**: Indicates success (e.g., DIAMETER_SUCCESS 2001) or failure of the operation.
- **Origin-Host/Realm**: Identifies the responding PGW-C.
- **Charging-Rule-Report**: Provides detailed status for each PCC rule affected by the RAR, indicating if it was successfully applied or if an error occurred. (TS 29.212 §5.3.18 V17.8.0)
- **Failed-AVP**: Included in case of certain failures to pinpoint the problematic AVP from the request.

### Error Handling
If the PGW-C cannot comply with the RAR, it sets the Result-Code to a failure value (e.g., DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY 5012). The Charging-Rule-Report AVP can contain a Rule-Failure-Code to specify the reason for a particular rule's failure. (TS 29.212 §5.3.12, §5.3.28 V17.8.0)
doc.title: Re-Auth-Answer

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: The Session-Id AVP (AVP Code 263) is a unique identifier for a session, used to correlate messages. Origin: The value is copied from the corresponding request message as per RFC 6733.

result_code.name: Result-Code
enum_doc: DIAMETER_SUCCESS: The request was successfully completed. (TS 29.212 §5.3.1)
DIAMETER_UNKNOWN_SESSION_ID: The requested session could not be found. (TS 29.212 §5.3.2)
DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY: The request could not be satisfied. (TS 29.212 §5.3.12)
result_code.doc: The Result-Code AVP (AVP Code 268) indicates whether a particular request was completed successfully or whether an error occurred. Origin: Set by the PGW-C based on its ability to process the RAR message and apply the requested rules.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The Origin-Host AVP (AVP Code 264) contains the Diameter Identity of the node that originated the message. Origin: Set to the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the PGW-C as configured in the node.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The Origin-Realm AVP (AVP Code 296) contains the realm of the originator of any Diameter message. Origin: Set to the administrative domain (realm) of the PGW-C as configured in the node.

origin_state_id.name: Origin-State-Id
origin_state_id.doc: The Origin-State-Id AVP (AVP Code 278) is a monotonically increasing number that is advanced whenever a Diameter entity restarts with loss of previous state. Origin: Generated by the PGW-C to allow the PCRF to detect if the PGW-C has rebooted.

charging_rule_report.name: Charging-Rule-Report
charging_rule_report.doc: The Charging-Rule-Report AVP (AVP Code 1018) is of type Grouped and is used to report the status of PCC rules. Origin: Generated by the PGW-C to report the outcome of applying specific PCC rules requested in the preceding RAR.

charging_rule_name.name: Charging-Rule-Name
charging_rule_name.doc: The Charging-Rule-Name AVP (AVP Code 1005) is of type OctetString and defines a name for a PCC rule. Origin: Copied from the Charging-Rule-Install or Charging-Rule-Modify AVP in the corresponding RAR message to identify which rule is being reported on.

pcc_rule_status.name: PCC-Rule-Status
enum_doc: ACTIVE (0): The PCC rule is active. (TS 29.212 §5.3.19)
INACTIVE (1): The PCC rule is inactive. (TS 29.212 §5.3.19)
TEMPORARILY INACTIVE (2): The PCC rule is temporarily inactive. (TS 29.212 §5.3.19)
OBSOLETE (3): The PCC rule is obsolete. (TS 29.212 §5.3.19)
pcc_rule_status.doc: The PCC-Rule-Status AVP (AVP Code 1019) is of type Enumerated and indicates the status of a PCC rule. Origin: Set by the PGW-C to reflect the current state of the specified PCC rule after processing the RAR.

rule_failure_code.name: Rule-Failure-Code
enum_doc: UNKNOWN_RULE_NAME (1): The rule name is unknown. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
RATING_GROUP_ERROR (2): The rating group is invalid. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
SERVICE_IDENTIFIER_ERROR (3): The service identifier is invalid. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
GW/PCEF_MALFUNCTION (4): General gateway or PCEF malfunction. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
RESOURCES_LIMITATION (5): The PCEF cannot provide the requested resources. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
MAX_NR_BEARERS_REACHED (6): The maximum number of bearers has been reached. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
UNKNOWN_BEARER_ID (7): The bearer identifier is unknown. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
MISSING_BEARER_ID (8): The bearer identifier is missing. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
MISSING_GATING_RULE (9): A required gating rule is missing. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
OPERATOR_DETERMINED_BARRING (10): The action is barred by operator policy. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
MULTIPLE_BBERF_NOT_SUPPORTED (11): Multiple BBERFs are not supported. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
NO_BEARER_BOUND (12): The PCC rule is not bound to any bearer. (TS 29.212 §5.3.28)
rule_failure_code.doc: The Rule-Failure-Code AVP (AVP Code 1031) is of type Enumerated and indicates the reason for a PCC rule failure. It is only present if a rule operation failed. Origin: Set by the PGW-C when a rule operation fails, providing a specific reason for the failure.

doc.summary_en: The PCRF sends a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) to the PGW-C (PCEF) to initiate the modification of an established IP-CAN session. This message is used to provision, modify, or remove PCC rules, or to update QoS parameters for the session.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Re-Auth-Request (RAR) command is sent by the PCRF to the PCEF in order to proactively provision PCC rules. This can be triggered by changes in subscription data, AF interaction, or other internal policy decisions at the PCRF. (TS 29.212 §4.5.2 V17.4.0)

### Triggers
- A change in the user's subscription profile in the SPR.
- A request from an Application Function (AF) that affects the session's policy.
- An internal policy decision or timer expiry within the PCRF.

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Identifies the IP-CAN session to be modified.
- **Re-Auth-Request-Type**: Indicates if the request is for authorization only or for both authentication and authorization.
- **Charging-Rule-Install**: Contains new or modified PCC rules to be applied.
- **Charging-Rule-Remove**: Contains the names of PCC rules to be removed.
- **QoS-Information**: Provides updated QoS parameters for the default or a dedicated bearer.

### Successful Outcome
The PGW-C (PCEF) successfully applies the new policy and QoS information and responds with a Re-Auth-Answer (RAA) with a Result-Code indicating success (e.g., DIAMETER_SUCCESS 2001).

### Unsuccessful Outcome
If the PGW-C cannot fulfill the request, it responds with an RAA containing an appropriate experimental Result-Code (e.g., DIAMETER_ERROR_PCC_RULE_EVENT). (TS 29.212 §5.6.3 V17.4.0)
doc.title: Re-Auth-Request

diameter_header.name: Diameter Header
diameter_header.doc: Defines the Diameter command code (258 for RAR), flags, and Application ID (16777238 for Gx). The 'R' (Request) and 'P' (Proxiable) bits are set. Origin: Based on the Diameter base protocol (RFC 6733) and Gx interface specification (TS 29.212).

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the user's IP-CAN session, used to correlate messages. It is constructed by the first Diameter node involved in the session (the PGW-C). Origin: Mandated by RFC 6733 for all session-based Diameter messages.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Gx (16777238). Origin: Set to the Gx Application ID as defined in 3GPP TS 29.212.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity (FQDN) of the node sending the message, in this case, the PCRF. Origin: A mandatory AVP in all Diameter messages as defined by RFC 6733.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node sending the message (PCRF). Origin: A mandatory AVP in all Diameter messages as defined by RFC 6733.

dest_realm.name: Destination-Realm
dest_realm.doc: The realm of the destination node (PGW-C). This is used for routing the message. Origin: A mandatory AVP in all Diameter messages as defined by RFC 6733.

dest_host.name: Destination-Host
dest_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity (FQDN) of the destination node, the PGW-C. Origin: A mandatory AVP in all Diameter messages as defined by RFC 6733.

re_auth_req_type.name: Re-Auth-Request-Type
re_auth_req_type.doc: Indicates the type of re-authentication requested. For Gx, this is typically AUTHORIZE_ONLY. Origin: Defined in RFC 6733 and its value is determined by the PCRF's policy decision.
enum_doc: AUTHORIZE_ONLY (0): The user is to be authorized only. (TS 29.212)
AUTH_AND_AUTHORIZE (1): The user is to be authenticated and authorized.
AUTHORIZE_AUTHENTICATED (2): The user, who has already been authenticated, is to be authorized.

charging_rule_install.name: Charging-Rule-Install
charging_rule_install.doc: A grouped AVP that contains the PCC rules to be installed or modified at the PGW-C. Origin: Included by the PCRF when a policy decision requires provisioning new or modified PCC rules.

charging_rule_name.name: Charging-Rule-Name
charging_rule_name.doc: The name of a PCC rule to be installed, modified, or removed. Origin: The value is assigned by the PCRF to uniquely identify a PCC rule.

charging_rule_def.name: Charging-Rule-Definition
charging_rule_def.doc: A grouped AVP that defines the parameters of a PCC rule, including service data flow filters, precedence, and charging information. Origin: Constructed by the PCRF based on subscription data or AF input to define a specific policy.

charging_rule_remove.name: Charging-Rule-Remove
charging_rule_remove.doc: A grouped AVP that contains the names of PCC rules to be removed from the PGW-C. Origin: Included by the PCRF when a policy decision requires the removal of existing PCC rules.

qos_info.name: QoS-Information
qos_info.doc: A grouped AVP that provides the QoS parameters to be applied to the default bearer or a dedicated bearer. Origin: Included by the PCRF to enforce QoS policies, which may be derived from subscription data (SPR) or AF requests.
