qci.name: QoS-Class-Identifier
qci.doc: An integer representing the QoS Class Identifier (QCI) for the bearer, defining its packet forwarding treatment. Origin: The value is selected by the PCRF based on the service requirements, as defined in TS 23.203.

max_req_bw_ul.name: Max-Requested-Bandwidth-UL
max_req_bw_ul.doc: The maximum requested bandwidth in bits per second for the uplink direction. Origin: Determined by the PCRF based on policy and subscription limits.

max_req_bw_dl.name: Max-Requested-Bandwidth-DL
max_req_bw_dl.doc: The maximum requested bandwidth in bits per second for the downlink direction. Origin: Determined by the PCRF based on policy and subscription limits.

gbr_ul.name: Guaranteed-Bitrate-UL
gbr_ul.doc: The guaranteed bitrate in bits per second for the uplink direction for a GBR bearer. Origin: Determined by the PCRF for services requiring guaranteed QoS, based on policy.

gbr_dl.name: Guaranteed-Bitrate-DL
gbr_dl.doc: The guaranteed bitrate in bits per second for the downlink direction for a GBR bearer. Origin: Determined by the PCRF for services requiring guaranteed QoS, based on policy.

doc.summary_en: The Insert-Subscriber-Data-Answer (IDA) is sent from the MME to the HSS in response to an Insert-Subscriber-Data-Request (IDR). It acknowledges the receipt and processing of the subscriber data update from the HSS.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Insert-Subscriber-Data-Answer (IDA) command, indicated by the Command-Code field set to 319 and the 'R' bit cleared in the Command Flags field, is sent from MME/SGSN to HSS. (TS 29.272 §5.2.3.2 V18.1.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when an MME receives an Insert-Subscriber-Data-Request (IDR) from the HSS and has successfully processed the request to update or add subscriber data. It is also sent to indicate a processing failure.

### Key IEs
- **Session-Id**: Must be the same as in the received IDR.
- **Result-Code / Experimental-Result**: Indicates the outcome of the operation. DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001) means the HSS data was accepted by the MME. Other codes indicate specific errors.
- **IMS-Voice-Over-PS-Sessions-Supported**: Informs the HSS about the MME's capability to support IMS voice over PS sessions.
- **Supported-Features**: A list of features supported by the MME.

### Error Handling
The outcome of the procedure is indicated by the Result-Code or Experimental-Result AVP. If the MME cannot process the IDR, it returns an IDA with the appropriate error code. For example, DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY (5012) is used if the MME cannot fulfill the request. (TS 29.272 §5.2.3.2 V18.1.0)

### Ordering & State
The IDA message concludes the HSS-initiated subscriber data update procedure. The Auth-Session-State AVP must be included and set to NO_STATE_MAINTAINED as per the specification. (TS 29.272 §5.2.3.2 V18.1.0)
doc.title: Insert-Subscriber-Data-Answer

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session, copied from the request message. Origin: The value is copied from the corresponding Insert-Subscriber-Data-Request (IDR) message.

vendor_specific_app_id.name: Vendor-Specific-Application-Id
vendor_specific_app_id.doc: A grouped AVP that identifies the Diameter application (S6a). Origin: This is a standard grouped AVP required by the Diameter protocol for vendor-specific applications.

vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier for 3GPP. Origin: This is a fixed value defined by 3GPP in relevant specifications.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the S6a application. Origin: This is a fixed value for the S6a interface as defined in TS 29.272.

result_code.name: Result-Code
result_code.doc: Indicates the result of the request, used for base protocol errors. It is mutually exclusive with Experimental-Result. Origin: The value is set by the MME to reflect the outcome of processing the IDR message at the Diameter base protocol level.

experimental_result.name: Experimental-Result
experimental_result.doc: A grouped AVP indicating the 3GPP-specific result of the request. It is mutually exclusive with Result-Code. Origin: This grouped AVP is populated by the MME to convey a 3GPP-specific outcome of the IDR processing.

experimental_result_code.name: Experimental-Result-Code
experimental_result_code.doc: A 3GPP-specific result code. Origin: The value is set by the MME based on 3GPP-defined success or error conditions for the S6a interface.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether the session state is maintained. For IDA, it must be set to NO_STATE_MAINTAINED. Origin: The value is fixed to NO_STATE_MAINTAINED as mandated by the S6a specification for this procedure.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The Diameter Identity of the node that originated the message (MME). Origin: This value is the configured Diameter Host identity of the sending MME.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the originating node (MME). Origin: This value is the configured Diameter Realm of the sending MME.

ims_voice_over_ps_sessions_supported.name: IMS-Voice-Over-PS-Sessions-Supported
ims_voice_over_ps_sessions_supported.doc: Indicates MME support for IMS voice over PS sessions. Origin: The value reflects the configured capability of the MME regarding IMS voice support.

homogeneous_support_of_ims_voice_over_ps.name: Homogeneous-Support-of-IMS-Voice-Over-PS-Sessions
homogeneous_support_of_ims_voice_over_ps.doc: Indicates if IMS voice over PS is supported homogeneously in all TAs served by the MME. Origin: The value reflects the MME's configured support for IMS voice across its serving areas.

supported_features.name: Supported-Features
supported_features.doc: A grouped AVP that contains a list of features supported by the MME. Origin: This AVP is populated based on the MME's configured and supported features relevant to the S6a interface.

feature_list_id.name: Feature-List-ID
feature_list_id.doc: Identifies a list of features. Origin: This is a configured identifier for a specific set of supported features on the MME.

feature_list.name: Feature-List
feature_list.doc: A bitmask indicating the supported features. Origin: This bitmask is generated by the MME based on its specific software configuration and capabilities.

doc.summary_en: The HSS sends an Insert-Subscriber-Data-Request (IDR) to the MME to provide it with updated subscriber data. This can happen due to administrative changes in the HSS or upon registration in a new MME if the HSS determines the MME's data is outdated.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Insert-Subscriber-Data-Request (IDR) command, indicated by the Command-Code field set to 318 and the 'R' bit set in the Command Flags field, is sent from the HSS to the MME to update the subscription data of a user in the MME. (TS 29.272 §5.2.3.1 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is initiated by the HSS when the subscriber data for a specific UE is modified and needs to be pushed to the serving MME. This can be triggered by an operator action or other network events. The HSS may also send it after a UE registers if it detects the MME holds stale data. (TS 29.272 §5.2.1 V18.4.0)

### Key AVPs
- **User-Name**: Contains the IMSI of the subscriber.
- **Subscription-Data**: A grouped AVP that carries the actual subscription profile information being updated, such as AMBR, APN configurations, and access restrictions.
- **IDR-Flags**: Indicates the reason for the data insertion (e.g., UE data is being updated).

### Error Handling
Upon receiving the IDR, the MME processes the data and responds with an Insert-Subscriber-Data-Answer (IDA). If the MME cannot fulfill the request, it returns an IDA with a Result-Code or Experimental-Result-Code AVP indicating the specific error, such as DIAMETER_USER_UNKNOWN or DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY. (TS 29.272 §5.2.3.2 V18.4.0)
doc.title: Insert-Subscriber-Data-Request

diameter_session_id.name: Session-Id
diameter_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, used to correlate messages. Origin: Generated by the sending node (HSS) to uniquely identify this Diameter session.

auth_application_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_application_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as S6a. The value 16777251 is assigned to the S6a/S6d application. Origin: A fixed value defined in the 3GPP specification for the S6a interface.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether state is maintained for the session. For IDR, no state is maintained. Origin: A spec-defined enum value set by the HSS according to the procedure rules.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0): State is maintained for the session.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that originated the message (the HSS). Origin: The configured FQDN of the sending HSS instance.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated the message. Origin: The configured realm of the sending HSS instance.

destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
destination_realm.doc: The realm of the destination node (the MME). Origin: Determined by the HSS based on the subscriber's location or registration information.

user_name.name: User-Name
user_name.doc: The IMSI of the subscriber whose data is being updated. Origin: The unique identifier of the subscriber whose profile is being modified in the HSS.

subscription_data.name: Subscription-Data
subscription_data.doc: A grouped AVP containing the subscriber's profile data. Origin: A container for subscriber profile data provisioned in the HSS.

ambr.name: AMBR
ambr.doc: Aggregate Maximum Bit Rate for the subscriber for all non-GBR bearers. Origin: Provisioned in the HSS as part of the subscriber's QoS profile.

max_req_bw_ul.name: Max-Requested-Bandwidth-UL
max_req_bw_ul.doc: Maximum requested bandwidth for uplink in bits per second. Origin: A component of the AMBR value provisioned in the HSS.

max_req_bw_dl.name: Max-Requested-Bandwidth-DL
max_req_bw_dl.doc: Maximum requested bandwidth for downlink in bits per second. Origin: A component of the AMBR value provisioned in the HSS.

apn_config_profile.name: APN-Configuration-Profile
apn_config_profile.doc: Contains the user's subscribed APN configurations. Origin: A container for APN settings provisioned in the HSS for the subscriber.

context_identifier.name: Context-Identifier
context_identifier.doc: Identifies the context to which this APN configuration profile applies. The value 0 is the default context. Origin: A unique identifier for a specific APN profile, provisioned in the HSS.

all_apn_configs_included_indicator.name: All-APN-Configurations-Included-Indicator
all_apn_configs_included_indicator.doc: Indicates if all APN configurations for the user are included. Origin: A flag set by the HSS to indicate completeness of the APN data being sent.
enum_doc: ALL_APN_CONFIGURATIONS_INCLUDED (0): All APN configurations are present.
MODIFIED_ADDED_APN_CONFIGURATIONS_INCLUDED (1): Only modified or added APNs are present.

apn_configuration.name: APN-Configuration
apn_configuration.doc: A grouped AVP defining the configuration for a single APN. Origin: A set of parameters for a specific APN, provisioned in the HSS.

destination_host.name: Destination-Host
destination_host.doc: The FQDN of the destination node (the MME). This is required if the Destination-Realm is not sufficient for routing. Origin: Determined by the HSS, often from a previous ULR message or other routing database.

idr_flags.name: IDR-Flags
idr_flags.doc: Flags providing additional information about the Insert-Subscriber-Data operation. Origin: Set by the HSS to indicate the reason for the data update, as defined in the specification.

doc.summary_en: The HSS sends a Multimedia-Authentication-Answer (MAA) to the AAA server to provide authentication vectors for a user, enabling the AAA server to proceed with the user's authentication for non-3GPP access.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent by the HSS in response to a Multimedia-Authentication-Request (MAR) from an AAA Server. It carries the requested authentication data for a specific user. (TS 29.273 §8.2.2.2 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
The HSS receives a MAR from the AAA server. After successfully retrieving or generating the authentication vectors for the user identified by the IMSI in the User-Name AVP, the HSS constructs and sends the MAA. (TS 29.273 §8.2.2.1 V18.3.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Result-Code**: Indicates success (2001) or failure.
- **User-Name**: The IMSI of the subscriber.
- **SIP-Number-Auth-Items**: The number of authentication vectors provided.
- **SIP-Auth-Data-Item**: A grouped AVP containing the authentication vector (e.g., RAND, AUTN, XRES, CK, IK).

### Error Handling
If the HSS cannot provide authentication vectors, it sets the Result-Code or Experimental-Result AVP to an appropriate failure code, such as DIAMETER_ERROR_USER_UNKNOWN (5001) or DIAMETER_ERROR_AUTH_SCHEME_NOT_SUPPORTED (5017). (TS 29.273 §8.2.2.2 V18.3.0)
doc.title: Multimedia-Authentication-Answer

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: Uniquely identifies the session, allowing requests and responses to be correlated. Origin: Copied from the corresponding Multimedia-Authentication-Request (MAR) message.

vendor_specific_app_id.name: Vendor-Specific-Application-Id
vendor_specific_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as a vendor-specific application. Origin: A fixed value group identifying the 3GPP SWx application as defined in the specification.

vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id.doc: Contains the IANA-assigned 'SMI Network Management Private Enterprise Codes' value for 3GPP (10415). Origin: A spec-defined constant for 3GPP.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: Contains the 3GPP SWx Application (16777265) identifier. Origin: A spec-defined constant for the SWx Diameter application.

result_code.name: Result-Code
result_code.doc: Indicates the result of the request processing. 2001 indicates success. Origin: Set by the HSS to indicate the success or failure of processing the authentication data request.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_SUCCESS - The request was successfully completed.
DIAMETER_ERROR_USER_UNKNOWN - The user is unknown in the HSS.
DIAMETER_ERROR_AUTH_SCHEME_NOT_SUPPORTED - The requested authentication scheme is not supported.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether session state is maintained by the HSS for the subscriber. Origin: Configured in the HSS policy, indicating if it will maintain the session state for this user.
enum_doc: NO_STATE_MAINTAINED - Session state is not maintained.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the HSS that generated this message. Origin: The configured FQDN of the HSS node sending the message.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The Diameter realm of the HSS that generated this message. Origin: The configured realm of the HSS node sending the message.

user_name.name: User-Name
user_name.doc: Contains the IMSI of the user for whom authentication data is provided. Origin: Copied from the User-Name AVP in the incoming Multimedia-Authentication-Request (MAR).

sip_num_auth_items.name: SIP-Number-Auth-Items
sip_num_auth_items.doc: Indicates the number of authentication vectors (SIP-Auth-Data-Item) provided in the answer. Origin: Determined by the HSS based on how many authentication vectors it generated and is providing in this response.

sip_auth_data_item.name: SIP-Auth-Data-Item
sip_auth_data_item.doc: A grouped AVP containing a single authentication vector. Origin: Generated by the HSS/AuC function to be used for authenticating the user.

sip_item_number.name: SIP-Item-Number
sip_item_number.doc: A number to identify and order the authentication vector within the message. Origin: Assigned by the HSS to sequentially number the authentication vectors in the response.

sip_auth_scheme.name: SIP-Authentication-Scheme
sip_auth_scheme.doc: Specifies the authentication scheme to be used, e.g., 'EAP-AKA''. Origin: A spec-defined string indicating the authentication method supported and generated by the HSS.

sip_authenticate.name: SIP-Authenticate
sip_authenticate.doc: Contains the challenge data, which is a concatenation of the random challenge (RAND) and the Authentication Token (AUTN). Origin: Generated by the HSS/AuC as part of the authentication vector.

sip_authorization.name: SIP-Authorization
sip_authorization.doc: Contains the expected response (XRES) from the UE. This is used by the AAA server to verify the UE's response. Origin: Generated by the HSS/AuC as part of the authentication vector; it is the expected result of the algorithm run on the UE.

confidentiality_key.name: Confidentiality-Key
confidentiality_key.doc: The Confidentiality Key (CK) generated during the AKA procedure, used for ciphering. Origin: Generated by the HSS/AuC as part of the authentication vector.

integrity_key.name: Integrity-Key
integrity_key.doc: The Integrity Key (IK) generated during the AKA procedure, used for integrity protection. Origin: Generated by the HSS/AuC as part of the authentication vector.

doc.summary_en: The Multimedia-Authentication-Request (MAR) is sent by a AAA Server to the HSS to retrieve authentication vectors for a user attempting to access IMS services via a non-3GPP access network. This is part of the SWx interface procedures.
doc.steps: ['1. A UE initiates an access procedure via a non-3GPP network (e.g., WLAN).', '2. The non-3GPP network forwards the access request to the AAA Server.', '3. The AAA Server identifies the user (e.g., via IMSI in the User-Name AVP) and determines the need for IMS authentication.', '4. The AAA Server constructs and sends a Multimedia-Authentication-Request (MAR) to the HSS, requesting one or more authentication vectors (SIP-Number-Auth-Items). (TS 29.273, §8.2.2.1)', '5. The HSS receives the MAR, validates the request, generates the requested authentication vectors, and responds with a Multimedia-Authentication-Answer (MAA).']
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The MAR command is used by the AAA Server to request authentication information from the HSS for a user. This procedure is invoked when a user needs to be authenticated for non-3GPP access to the 3GPP network. (TS 29.273 §8.2.2.1.1 V18.1.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when the AAA Server receives a request from a non-3GPP access network that requires user authentication with the HSS.

### Key AVPs
- **User-Name**: Contains the IMSI of the user to be authenticated.
- **SIP-Number-Auth-Items**: Specifies the number of authentication vectors the AAA Server is requesting.
- **SIP-Auth-Data-Item**: A grouped AVP that includes the authentication scheme (e.g., EAP-AKA').
- **RAT-Type**: Indicates the Radio Access Technology from which the user is connecting.

### Error Handling
If the HSS cannot process the request (e.g., user unknown, user barred), it will return an MAA with a relevant Result-Code or Experimental-Result-Code AVP indicating the failure reason, such as DIAMETER_ERROR_USER_UNKNOWN. (TS 29.273 §8.2.2.2.1 V18.1.0)
doc.title: Multimedia-Authentication-Request

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: Uniquely identifies this session. It is created by the client (AAA) and must be globally and eternally unique. Origin: Generated by the Diameter client (AAA) to uniquely identify the session.

vsa_id.name: Vendor-Specific-Application-Id
vsa_id.doc: A grouped AVP that identifies the Diameter application as a vendor-specific one, in this case for 3GPP's SWx interface. Origin: A grouped AVP containing fixed and configured values for the 3GPP SWx application.

vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id.doc: Contains the IANA-assigned vendor identifier for 3GPP, which is 10415. Origin: A spec-defined constant (10415) for 3GPP.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the specific 3GPP Diameter application, which is 16777265 for the SWx interface. Origin: A spec-defined constant (16777265) for the SWx Diameter application.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether the AAA server is maintaining session state for this user. 'NO_STATE_MAINTAINED' is typical for authentication-only requests. Origin: Set by the AAA server based on its session handling policy.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0): State is maintained, subsequent messages must have this AVP.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED (1): State is not maintained, this is a one-off transaction.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that originated the request (the AAA Server). Origin: The configured FQDN of the sending node (AAA server).

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The administrative realm of the originator of the request. Origin: The configured home realm of the sending node (AAA server).

destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
destination_realm.doc: The realm the request is to be routed to. This is the home realm of the user. Origin: The configured realm of the destination HSS, used for routing.

user_name.name: User-Name
user_name.doc: Contains the user's identity, which for the SWx interface is the IMSI. Origin: The user's IMSI, provided to the AAA server during the non-3GPP access attempt.

sip_auth_data_item.name: SIP-Auth-Data-Item
sip_auth_data_item.doc: A grouped AVP containing the authentication scheme requested by the AAA server. Origin: A grouped AVP specifying the requested authentication details.

sip_auth_scheme.name: SIP-Authentication-Scheme
sip_auth_scheme.doc: Specifies the authentication scheme to be used. For 3GPP access, this is typically 'EAP-AKA' or 'EAP-AKA''. Origin: A configured value on the AAA server indicating the required authentication method, typically EAP-AKA'.

sip_num_auth_items.name: SIP-Number-Auth-Items
sip_num_auth_items.doc: Indicates the number of authentication vectors requested by the AAA Server from the HSS. Origin: A configured value on the AAA server specifying how many authentication vectors to request.

rat_type.name: RAT-Type
rat_type.doc: Indicates the Radio Access Technology being used by the UE. This is crucial for non-3GPP access scenarios. Origin: Determined by the AAA server based on the type of non-3GPP access network the UE is using.
enum_doc: WLAN (1004): Indicates the access is over a Wireless LAN.

destination_host.name: Destination-Host
destination_host.doc: The FQDN of the destination HSS. This is used if the originator knows the specific HSS to contact, bypassing realm-based routing. Origin: The FQDN of the destination HSS, used for direct routing if known.

route_record.name: Route-Record
route_record.doc: This AVP is inserted by Diameter proxies to record the path of the request, allowing the response to be routed back along the same path. Origin: Inserted by Diameter proxies/agents along the path to record the route taken.

doc.summary_en: The HSS sends a Purge-UE-Answer (PUA) to the MME to acknowledge the Purge-UE-Request. This message confirms that the HSS has marked the UE as purged for EPS services, meaning the MME registration is no longer valid in the HSS.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Purge-UE-Answer (PUA) command is sent by the HSS to an MME in response to a Purge-UE-Request command. It indicates the result of the purge operation. (TS 29.272 §7.2.8 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when the HSS receives a Purge-UE-Request (PUR) from an MME. The MME sends a PUR when it determines that a UE's context should be removed, for example, due to an ISR deactivation or administrative reasons. (TS 29.272 §7.2.7 V18.4.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Echoed from the request to correlate the transaction.
- **Result-Code / Experimental-Result**: Indicates success (e.g., DIAMETER_SUCCESS) or failure of the purge operation.
- **PUA-Flags**: An optional flag that can instruct the MME to freeze the M-TMSI if the UE is still considered attached in the HSS for non-EPS services.

### Successful Outcome
If the HSS successfully marks the UE as purged for EPS, it returns a PUA with a Result-Code of DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001).

### Error Handling
If the HSS cannot process the request (e.g., user is unknown), it returns a PUA with an appropriate error code in the Experimental-Result AVP, such as DIAMETER_ERROR_USER_UNKNOWN (5001). (TS 29.272 §7.4 V18.4.0)
doc.title: Purge-UE-Answer

avp_auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the specific Diameter application (S6a). Origin: A spec-defined constant (16777251) for the 3GPP S6a/S6d interface.

avp_auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
avp_auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether any state is maintained for this session. For S6a, this is typically set to NO_STATE_MAINTAINED. Origin: A spec-defined value indicating that no state is maintained for the session.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0): State is maintained, and subsequent messages must be sent to the same server.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED (1): No state is maintained, and subsequent messages can be sent to any server.

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the node that generated the message (the HSS). Origin: The configured Diameter identity of the sending node (HSS).

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that generated the message. Origin: The configured Diameter realm of the sending node (HSS).

avp_pua_flags.name: PUA-Flags
avp_pua_flags.doc: A bitmask of flags providing instructions to the MME. Bit 0 indicates 'Freeze M-TMSI'. Origin: Set by the HSS to instruct the MME on specific actions, such as freezing the M-TMSI.

bits_doc.0: Freeze M-TMSI: If set, the MME should freeze the M-TMSI if the UE is still attached for non-EPS services in the HSS.

avp_result_code.name: Result-Code
avp_result_code.doc: Indicates the outcome of the request. 2001 indicates success. Origin: Indicates the outcome of the Purge-UE operation at the HSS.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001): The request was successfully completed.
DIAMETER_ERROR_USER_UNKNOWN (5001): The user (IMSI) is not found in the HSS.
DIAMETER_ERROR_UNKNOWN_EPS_SUBSCRIPTION (5420): The EPS subscription for the user is not found.

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session, used to correlate messages. It is copied from the request. Origin: Echoed from the corresponding Purge-UE-Request message.

avp_vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
avp_vendor_id.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier for 3GPP (10415). Origin: A spec-defined constant (10415) for 3GPP.

avp_vendor_specific_app_id.name: Vendor-Specific-Application-Id
avp_vendor_specific_app_id.doc: A grouped AVP that identifies the 3GPP-specific application. Origin: A grouped AVP containing identifiers for the 3GPP S6a application.

doc.summary_en: The HSS sends a Purge-UE-Answer (PUA) to the SGSN to acknowledge the purging of a UE's subscription data from the HSS. This message confirms the outcome of the Purge-UE-Request (PUR) procedure.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Purge-UE-Answer (PUA) command is sent by the HSS to an SGSN in response to a Purge-UE-Request (PUR) command. It is used to inform the SGSN about the result of the purge operation. (TS 29.272 §7.2.10.1 V17.9.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when the HSS receives a PUR command from an SGSN and has completed the requested purge operation for the specified IMSI. (TS 29.272 §7.2.9.2 V17.9.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Carries the identifier of the session, copied from the PUR.
- **Result-Code / Experimental-Result**: Indicates the outcome. A `Result-Code` of `DIAMETER_SUCCESS` (2001) signifies a successful purge.
- **Origin-Host / Origin-Realm**: Identify the HSS that performed the purge.
- **PUA-Flags**: An optional bitmask that can convey additional information, such as the 'Freeze-M-TMSI' flag.

### Success and Error Handling
- **Success**: The HSS sets the `Result-Code` AVP to `DIAMETER_SUCCESS`.
- **Failure**: For application-specific errors, the `Experimental-Result` AVP is used with codes like `DIAMETER_ERROR_USER_UNKNOWN` (5001) if the IMSI is not found in the HSS. (TS 29.272 §7.4 V17.9.0)
doc.title: Purge-UE-Answer

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session, used to correlate messages. Origin: Copied from the corresponding request message as per RFC 6733.

vendor_specific_app_id.name: Vendor-Specific-Application-Id
vendor_specific_app_id.doc: A grouped AVP containing the Vendor-Id and the 3GPP-specific application identifier. Origin: Defined in the Diameter Base Protocol (RFC 6733) to carry vendor-specific application identifiers.

vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier for 3GPP, which is 10415. Origin: Set to the 3GPP's specific Vendor ID (10415) as defined in TS 29.229.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the 3GPP S6d application, with a value of 16777251. Origin: Set to the S6d Application ID as specified in TS 29.272.

result_code.name: Result-Code
result_code.doc: Indicates the outcome of the request. A value of 2001 signifies success. Origin: Set by the HSS to indicate the success or failure of the purge operation, based on standard Diameter result codes from RFC 6733.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_SUCCESS: The request was successfully completed.
DIAMETER_ERROR_USER_UNKNOWN: The user (IMSI) is unknown in the HSS.
DIAMETER_ERROR_IDENTITIES_DONT_MATCH: The identities included in the request do not match.
DIAMETER_ERROR_RAT_NOT_ALLOWED: The requested Radio Access Technology is not allowed for the user.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether the session state is maintained by the application. Origin: Set by the HSS based on its session handling policy, as defined in RFC 6733.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED: The session state is maintained, and subsequent messages for this session are expected.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED: The session state is not maintained, and this is the last message for the session.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the HSS that sent this message. Origin: Set to the Diameter Identity of the responding node (the HSS) as per RFC 6733.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the HSS that sent this message. Origin: Set to the Diameter realm of the responding node (the HSS) as per RFC 6733.

pua_flags.name: PUA-Flags
pua_flags.doc: A bitmask indicating specific information from the HSS. Bit 0: Freeze-M-TMSI. Origin: Set by the HSS to convey specific flags related to the purge operation as defined in TS 29.272.

bits_doc.0: Freeze-M-TMSI: If set, it indicates that the M-TMSI is frozen in the HSS.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends a Purge-UE-Request (PUR) to the HSS to inform it that the MME has deleted the EPS subscription data and MM context for a specific UE. This allows the HSS to update the UE's registration status.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This procedure is used by the MME to inform the HSS that it has deleted the MM context and EPS subscription data for a UE. The HSS can then remove the MME identity from the UE's context and will not attempt to send any further update or notification messages for that UE to this MME. (TS 29.272 §5.2.4.1 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
The MME initiates this procedure when it deletes a UE's context due to events like the UE being implicitly detached, an operator policy, or the UE registering with a new MME. (TS 23.401 §5.3.8.3 V18.3.0)

### Key AVPs
- **User-Name**: Contains the IMSI of the UE to be purged.
- **PUR-Flags**: Optional flags to request specific actions, such as freezing the M-TMSI or P-TMSI. (TS 29.272 §5.2.4.1 V18.3.0)
doc.title: Purge-UE-Request

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, used to correlate messages. (RFC 6733 §8.8) Origin: Generated by the MME to uniquely identify this Diameter transaction.

vendor_specific_app_id.name: Vendor-Specific-Application-Id
vendor_specific_app_id.doc: Identifies the 3GPP-specific S6a application. (RFC 6733 §6.4) Origin: This is a standardized container AVP for vendor-specific application identifiers.

vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor ID for 3GPP (10415). (RFC 6733 §5.3.3) Origin: This is a fixed value defined by the 3GPP specification for all 3GPP-defined Diameter applications.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: The application identifier for the S6a interface (16777251). (TS 29.272 §5.1) Origin: This is a fixed value defined in the specification for the S6a Diameter application.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether state is maintained for the session. For PUR, which is a single request-answer pair, no state is maintained. (RFC 6733 §8.11) Origin: Set to NO_STATE_MAINTAINED as per Diameter base protocol requirements for single transaction messages.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that originated the message (the MME). (RFC 6733 §6.2) Origin: This is the configured FQDN of the sending MME node.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated the message (the MME's realm). (RFC 6733 §6.3) Origin: This is the configured home network domain of the sending MME.

destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
destination_realm.doc: The realm of the destination node (the HSS's realm). (RFC 6733 §6.6) Origin: This value is determined by the MME, typically from the UE's IMSI, to route the message to the correct HSS.

user_name.name: User-Name
user_name.doc: Contains the International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) of the UE being purged. (TS 29.272 §5.2.4.1) Origin: The value is the IMSI of the UE that the MME has decided to purge from its context.

pur_flags.name: PUR-Flags
pur_flags.doc: A bitmask of flags indicating specific purge actions requested by the MME. (TS 29.272 §7.3.51) Origin: The flags are set by the MME to indicate specific purge actions required at the HSS.

bits_doc.name: ULP
bits_doc.doc: UE Location Purged. Set to 1 if the MME has purged the UE location information.

bits_doc.name: S-CSCF-Restoration-Info-Purged
bits_doc.doc: Set to 1 if the MME has purged the S-CSCF restoration information.

bits_doc.name: Freeze-M-TMSI
bits_doc.doc: Set to 1 to request the HSS to freeze the M-TMSI.

bits_doc.name: Freeze-P-TMSI
bits_doc.doc: Set to 1 to request the HSS to freeze the P-TMSI.

doc.summary_en: The SGSN sends a Purge-UE-Request (PUR) to the HSS to request the removal of the UE's subscription data from the HSS for a specific access type. This is typically done when the SGSN has determined that the UE context is no longer needed, for example, after an implicit detach.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent by the SGSN to the HSS to indicate that the SGSN has deleted the subscription data and MM context of a detached UE. The HSS can then mark the UE as detached for the SGSN. (TS 29.272 §5.2.4.1 V17.4.0)

### Triggers
The SGSN initiates this procedure when it deletes an MM context for a detached UE, for instance, due to the expiration of the Mobile Reachable timer or Super-Charger timer. (TS 23.060 §6.8.3)

### Key AVPs
- **User-Name**: Contains the IMSI of the UE to be purged.
- **PUR-Flags**: A bitmask indicating specific conditions for the purge, such as 'UE Purged in SGSN'.

### Error Handling
If the HSS cannot process the request, it returns a Purge-UE-Answer (PUA) with a Result-Code indicating the failure reason (e.g., DIAMETER_USER_UNKNOWN). (TS 29.272 §5.2.4.2 V17.4.0)
doc.title: Purge-UE-Request

avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for this Diameter session, used to correlate requests and answers. Origin: Generated by the Diameter client (SGSN) to uniquely identify the session.

avp_auth_application_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as S6d. The value 16777251 is assigned to the S6a/S6d application. Origin: A spec-defined constant from TS 29.272 for the S6d interface.

avp_auth_session_state.doc: Indicates that no state is maintained for this session on the Diameter server. For PUR, this is always set to NO_STATE_MAINTAINED. Origin: A spec-defined value (1) indicating this is a stateless request as per RFC 6733 and TS 29.272.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0): State is maintained for the session.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED (1): No state is maintained for the session.

avp_origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that originated the request (the SGSN). Origin: The configured DiameterIdentity (FQDN) of the sending SGSN.

avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated the request. Origin: The configured Diameter realm of the sending SGSN's network.

avp_destination_realm.doc: The realm of the destination node (the HSS). This is used for routing the request. Origin: The configured Diameter realm of the destination HSS's network, used for routing.

avp_user_name.doc: The identity of the user, which for S6d is the IMSI. Origin: The IMSI of the UE whose context is being purged by the SGSN.

avp_destination_host.doc: The FQDN of the destination HSS. This is included if the SGSN knows the specific HSS instance to contact. Origin: The configured FQDN of the target HSS, if known through previous interactions or local configuration.

avp_pur_flags.doc: A bitmask providing additional information about the purge operation. Origin: Set by the SGSN based on the internal reason for the purge operation.

bits_doc.name: UE Purged in SGSN
bits_doc.doc: This bit, when set, indicates that the UE has been purged in the SGSN.

avp_supported_features.doc: Communicates features supported by the originating node (SGSN). Origin: A configured list of features supported by the SGSN software/hardware version.

avp_vendor_id.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier. 10415 is for 3GPP. Origin: A spec-defined constant (10415) for 3GPP-specific AVPs.

avp_feature_list_id.doc: A unique identifier for a list of features. Origin: A configured identifier for a specific set of supported features.

avp_feature_list.doc: A bitmask representing the supported features. Origin: A bitmask value corresponding to the enabled features identified by the Feature-List-ID.

doc.summary_en: The HSS sends a Purge-UE-Answer (PUA) to the MME to acknowledge the Purge-UE-Request and inform the MME about the outcome of the operation.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Purge-UE-Answer (PUA) command is sent by the HSS to an MME in response to a Purge-UE-Request (PUR) command. It indicates whether the HSS has successfully marked the UE as purged for the requesting MME. (TS 29.272 §7.2.10.1 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when the HSS receives a Purge-UE-Request (PUR) from an MME. The HSS processes the request to remove the MME identity associated with the specified IMSI and sends the PUA to confirm the result. (TS 29.272 §7.2.10.2 V18.3.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Result-Code / Experimental-Result**: Indicates the success or failure of the operation. A successful result is DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001).
- **PUA-Flags**: A bitmask providing additional information. For example, bit 0 indicates 'Freeze M-TMSI'.
- **Session-Id**: Echoed from the PUR message to correlate the request and answer.

### Error Handling
If the purge operation fails, the HSS sets the Result-Code or Experimental-Result AVP to an appropriate error value (e.g., DIAMETER_USER_UNKNOWN, DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY). The MME then handles the failure according to its local policies. (TS 29.272 §7.2.10.3 V18.3.0)
doc.title: Purge-UE-Answer

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session, used to correlate messages. It is created by the client (MME) and echoed by the server (HSS). Origin: Generated by the Diameter client (MME) in the request and echoed by the server (HSS) in the answer.

vendor_specific_app_id.name: Vendor-Specific-Application-Id
vendor_specific_app_id.doc: Identifies the vendor-specific Diameter application (S6a). It contains the Vendor-Id and the specific application identifier. Origin: This grouped AVP identifies the 3GPP-specific Diameter application as defined in the specification.

vendor_id.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier for 3GPP (10415). Origin: The value 10415 is the IANA-assigned vendor ID for 3GPP.

auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id.doc: The IANA-assigned application identifier for the 3GPP S6a/S6d interface (16777251). Origin: The value 16777251 is the IANA-assigned application ID for the 3GPP S6a/S6d interface.

result_code.name: Result-Code
result_code.doc: Indicates the outcome of the request. 2001 (DIAMETER_SUCCESS) signifies a successful operation. Other values indicate specific errors. Origin: Set by the HSS to indicate the outcome of the Purge-UE operation, as defined in IETF RFC 6733 and 3GPP TS 29.272.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether any state is maintained for this session. For S6a, this is always set to NO_STATE_MAINTAINED. Origin: Set by the HSS to indicate that no state is maintained for the session, as required by the S6a specification (TS 29.272).
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0) - State is maintained for this session.
NO_STATE_MAINTAINED (1) - No state is maintained for this session.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the node that generated the message (the HSS). Origin: Set by the HSS to its DiameterIdentity to identify the source of this answer message.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that generated the message (the HSS). Origin: Set by the HSS to its Diameter realm to identify the source network of this answer message.

pua_flags.name: PUA-Flags
pua_flags.doc: A bitmask that provides additional information about the purge operation. Bit 0: Freeze M-TMSI. Origin: This bitmask is set by the HSS to indicate specific outcomes, such as Freeze M-TMSI, as defined in TS 29.272.
bits_doc: If this bit is set, the MME shall consider the M-TMSI as frozen.

supported_features.name: Supported-Features
supported_features.doc: If present, this AVP contains the list of features supported by the origin host (HSS). Origin: Optionally included by the HSS to advertise its supported features for the S6a application to the MME.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends a Purge-UE-Request (PUR) to the HSS to request the removal of its MME identity from the UE's subscription data. This is typically done during an MME-initiated detach procedure when the MME decides not to maintain the UE context.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This procedure is used by the MME to inform the HSS that it has deleted the subscription data and MM context for a specific UE. The HSS then removes the MME identity associated with that UE. (TS 29.272 §7.2.8.1 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
The MME initiates this procedure as part of an MME-initiated detach process, for example, due to an O&M intervention or if an implicit detach timer expires and the MME chooses not to maintain the UE context for an extended period. (TS 23.401 §5.3.8.3 V18.4.0)

### Key AVPs
- **User-Name**: Contains the IMSI of the UE to be purged.
- **PUR-Flags**: A bitmask indicating specific actions requested by the MME, such as freezing the M-TMSI.

### Successful Outcome
The HSS processes the request, removes the MME identity from the UE's profile, and responds with a Purge-UE-Answer (PUA) with a Result-Code of DIAMETER_SUCCESS.

### Error Handling
If the HSS cannot process the request (e.g., the user is unknown), it returns a PUA with an appropriate experimental Result-Code, such as DIAMETER_ERROR_USER_UNKNOWN. (TS 29.272 §7.2.8.3 V18.3.0)
doc.title: Purge-UE-Request

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for this Diameter session, used to correlate requests and answers. The format is defined in RFC 6733. Origin: Generated by the MME to uniquely identify this Diameter session.

vendor_specific_app_id.name: Vendor-Specific-Application-Id
vendor_specific_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as S6a, which is vendor-specific to 3GPP. It groups the Vendor-Id and the Auth-Application-Id. Origin: A grouped AVP containing spec-defined values for the 3GPP S6a application.

vendor_id_3gpp.name: Vendor-Id
vendor_id_3gpp.doc: The IANA-assigned vendor identifier for 3GPP. Origin: A spec-defined constant (10415) for 3GPP.

auth_app_id_s6a.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_app_id_s6a.doc: The application identifier for the 3GPP S6a interface. Origin: A spec-defined constant (16777251) for the S6a Diameter application.

auth_session_state.name: Auth-Session-State
auth_session_state.doc: Indicates whether state is maintained for this session. For PUR, no state is maintained. Origin: A spec-defined enum value (NO_STATE_MAINTAINED) required by the procedure.
enum_doc: STATE_MAINTAINED (0): State is maintained for the session.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The Diameter Identity (FQDN) of the node that originated the request (the MME). Origin: The Diameter Identity of the sending MME node.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The Diameter realm of the originating node (the MME). Origin: The Diameter Realm of the sending MME node.

destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
destination_realm.doc: The Diameter realm of the destination node (the HSS). This is used for routing the request. Origin: The Diameter Realm of the target HSS, typically derived from the UE's IMSI.

user_name.name: User-Name
user_name.doc: The identity of the user, which for the S6a interface is the International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI). Origin: The IMSI of the UE whose context is being purged.

pur_flags.name: PUR-Flags
pur_flags.doc: A bitmask of flags that control the purge behavior at the HSS. (TS 29.272 §7.3.36). Origin: A bitmask of flags set by the MME to control the purge behavior, as defined in the specification.
bits_doc: Freeze-M-TMSI: If set, HSS shall not allocate the M-TMSI to another MME.
Freeze-P-TMSI: If set, HSS shall not allocate the P-TMSI to another SGSN.
UDR-Request-Indicator: If set, indicates that the request is triggered by a UDR message.

supported_features.name: Supported-Features
supported_features.doc: An optional AVP used to convey information about supported features between the MME and HSS. Origin: An optional AVP indicating features supported by the MME.

destination_host.name: Destination-Host
destination_host.doc: The Diameter Identity of the destination HSS. It is included if the MME has a direct route to the HSS, otherwise it is omitted and routing is based on Destination-Realm. Origin: The Diameter Identity of the target HSS, if known by the MME; otherwise omitted.

doc.summary_en: The Re-Auth-Answer (RAA) is sent by the P-CSCF to the PCRF in response to a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) message. It acknowledges the request and indicates the outcome of the re-authorization process initiated by the PCRF.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The RAA command, indicated by the Command-Code field set to 258 and the 'R' bit cleared in the Command Flags field, is sent by the P-CSCF to the PCRF in response to an RAR. The Result-Code or Experimental-Result AVP informs the PCRF of the status of the request. (TS 29.214 §5.3.2 V18.2.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when a P-CSCF receives a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) from a PCRF for an existing Rx session. The P-CSCF processes the request and sends the RAA to complete the transaction.

### Key IEs
- **Session-Id**: Correlates the answer with the request.
- **Result-Code**: Indicates success (e.g., DIAMETER_SUCCESS 2001) or a general failure.
- **Experimental-Result**: Provides 3GPP-specific result codes in case of failure.
- **Origin-Host / Origin-Realm**: Identifies the P-CSCF that is sending the answer.

### Error Handling
If the RAR processing fails, the P-CSCF includes a Result-Code AVP indicating the failure category (e.g., DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY 5012). For application-specific errors, it sets Result-Code to DIAMETER_EXPERIMENTAL_RESULT (5001) and includes the specific error in the Experimental-Result AVP. (RFC 6733 §7.2)
doc.title: Re-Auth-Answer

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, used to correlate messages. It is copied from the corresponding request. Origin: Copied from the Session-Id AVP of the corresponding Re-Auth-Request message.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that generated the message, in this case, the P-CSCF. Origin: The value is the configured FQDN of the sending node, in this case, the P-CSCF.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that generated the message. Origin: The value is the configured realm of the sending node, in this case, the P-CSCF.

result_code.name: Result-Code
result_code.doc: Indicates the outcome of the request. A value of 2001 (DIAMETER_SUCCESS) signifies success. Other values indicate different types of errors. Origin: Set by the P-CSCF to indicate the processing outcome of the RAR, as defined in RFC 6733.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_SUCCESS: The request was successfully completed.
DIAMETER_EXPERIMENTAL_RESULT: An experimental, vendor-specific, or 3GPP-specific error occurred. See Experimental-Result AVP for details.
DIAMETER_UNABLE_TO_COMPLY: The request could not be satisfied.

auth_application_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_application_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as 3GPP Rx. The value is fixed for the Rx interface. Origin: A fixed value (16777236) defined in the specification for the 3GPP Rx application.

origin_state_id.name: Origin-State-Id
origin_state_id.doc: A monotonically increasing value that is advanced whenever a Diameter entity restarts with loss of session state. Used for failover. Origin: A locally generated value by the P-CSCF used to track session state for failover purposes.

experimental_result.name: Experimental-Result
experimental_result.doc: Used to return 3GPP-specific result codes when the Result-Code is DIAMETER_EXPERIMENTAL_RESULT. Contains Vendor-Id and Experimental-Result-Code. Origin: Set by the P-CSCF to provide a 3GPP-specific result code when the main Result-Code is DIAMETER_EXPERIMENTAL_RESULT_SUCCESS.

proxy_info.name: Proxy-Info
proxy_info.doc: Contains information added by Diameter proxy agents that were in the path of the request. Origin: Added by a Diameter proxy agent (e.g., DRA) if it handles the message during transit.

route_record.name: Route-Record
route_record.doc: Contains the identity of a Diameter proxy that handled the request, used for routing the answer back along the same path. Origin: Added by each Diameter node in the path of the request message to enable loop detection.

doc.summary_en: The PCRF sends a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) to the P-CSCF to initiate a re-authorization of an existing Rx session. This is typically triggered by a change in policy, subscription data, or network conditions that affect the service flows associated with the session.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Re-Auth-Request (RAR) command is sent by the PCRF to the P-CSCF in order to request the re-authorization of an existing Rx session. (TS 29.214 §5.3.2 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when the PCRF needs to update the session information in the P-CSCF. Common triggers include:
- A change in the user's subscription profile (e.g., QoS change) received by the PCRF.
- A network-initiated resource modification or pre-emption.
- An internal policy decision within the PCRF.
- Expiration of the Authorization-Lifetime timer.

### Key IEs
- **Session-Id**: Identifies the Rx session to be modified.
- **Re-Auth-Request-Type**: Indicates the reason for the re-authorization (e.g., AUTHORIZE_ONLY, AUTHORIZE_AUTHENTICATE).
- **Specific-Action**: Instructs the P-CSCF to take a specific action, such as terminating the session or updating QoS for media flows.
- **Flows**: Provides updated information for specific media flows, including media components and flow descriptions.

### Error Handling
If the P-CSCF receives an RAR for an unknown Session-Id, it must return a Re-Auth-Answer (RAA) with the Result-Code set to DIAMETER_UNKNOWN_SESSION_ID. If it cannot comply with the request, it returns an RAA with an appropriate failure Result-Code. (TS 29.214 §5.3.3 V18.3.0)
doc.title: Re-Auth-Request

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session, created by the P-CSCF. It must be globally and eternally unique. Origin: Defined by RFC 6733 as a mandatory AVP for session-based messages.

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the node that originated the message (the PCRF). Origin: Defined by RFC 6733 as a mandatory AVP to identify the message originator.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated the message. Origin: Defined by RFC 6733 as a mandatory AVP to identify the realm of the message originator.

destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
destination_realm.doc: The realm of the intended destination of the message. Origin: Defined by RFC 6733 as a mandatory AVP to identify the realm of the message destination.

destination_host.name: Destination-Host
destination_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the intended destination of the message (the P-CSCF). Origin: Defined by RFC 6733 as a mandatory AVP to identify the message destination.

auth_application_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
auth_application_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as 3GPP Rx. The value for Rx is 16777236. Origin: Defined by RFC 6733 and specified for the Rx interface in TS 29.214.

re_auth_request_type.name: Re-Auth-Request-Type
re_auth_request_type.doc: Indicates the type of re-authorization being requested. Origin: Defined by RFC 6733 to indicate the type of re-authentication requested.
enum_doc: AUTHORIZE_ONLY - The server is requested to authorize the session, without authenticating the user.
AUTHORIZE_AUTHENTICATE - The server is requested to authorize the session and to authenticate the user.

specific_action.name: Specific-Action
specific_action.doc: Instructs the P-CSCF to take a specific action related to the Rx session. Origin: Defined in TS 29.214 to instruct the P-CSCF to perform a specific action.

flows.name: Flows
flows.doc: Contains information about the IP flows for a specific media component. It is used to add, modify, or remove service data flows. Origin: Defined in TS 29.214 to provide information about IP flows.

doc.summary_en: The OCS sends a Credit-Control-Answer (CCA) to the PGW-C in response to an initial credit request. This message grants a specific amount of service units (e.g., data volume, time) for a new data session and defines the conditions under which the PGW-C must request more units.
doc.steps:
1. The PGW-C initiates a new data session and sends a Credit-Control-Request (CCR) with CC-Request-Type set to INITIAL_REQUEST to the OCS. (TS 32.299 §5.2.2.1)
2. The OCS receives the CCR, authenticates the session, and consults the subscriber's charging profile to determine the appropriate quota.
3. The OCS constructs a CCA message containing the granted service units within the Multiple-Services-Credit-Control AVP.
4. The CCA is sent back to the PGW-C. If the Result-Code is DIAMETER_SUCCESS, the PGW-C allows the data session to proceed using the granted quota.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Credit-Control-Answer (CCA) message is used by the credit-control server (OCS) to respond to a Credit-Control-Request (CCR) from a credit-control client (PGW-C). For an initial request, it is used to provision service units for a new session. (TS 32.299 §5.2.2.2 V17.4.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered by the OCS upon receiving a valid CCR message with a CC-Request-Type AVP set to 'INITIAL_REQUEST (1)'.

### Key IEs
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the charging session.
- **Result-Code**: Indicates the outcome of the request (e.g., success, failure).
- **CC-Request-Type**: Echoes 'INITIAL_REQUEST' from the CCR.
- **Multiple-Services-Credit-Control**: A grouped AVP that contains the granted quota and triggers for re-authorization for one or more services.
- **Granted-Service-Unit**: Specifies the amount of data volume and/or time granted to the user.

### Error Handling
The `Result-Code` AVP at the top level or within the `Multiple-Services-Credit-Control` AVP indicates the status. A non-2xxx code signifies failure, and the PGW-C must act according to the `Credit-Control-Failure-Handling` policy, which may involve terminating the session. (TS 32.299 §5.2.2.2 V17.4.0)
doc.title: Credit-Control-Answer (Initial)

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, created by the client. Origin: Echoed from the corresponding CCR message to correlate the response with the request.

avp_result_code.name: Result-Code
avp_result_code.doc: Indicates whether the request was successful or if an error occurred. Origin: Set by the OCS to reflect the outcome of the credit authorization decision.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_CREDIT_LIMIT_REACHED: The user's credit limit has been reached.
DIAMETER_USER_UNKNOWN: The user could not be found in the OCS.
DIAMETER_RATING_FAILED: The OCS failed to rate the service.

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the node that originated the message. Origin: Set by the OCS to its own FQDN.

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated the message. Origin: Set by the OCS to its own realm.

avp_auth_application_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_application_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Ro (Charging). Origin: A spec-defined constant value (4) for the Ro interface.

avp_cc_request_type.name: CC-Request-Type
avp_cc_request_type.doc: Indicates the type of credit-control request this answer corresponds to. Origin: Echoed from the CC-Request-Type AVP in the corresponding CCR message.
enum_doc: INITIAL_REQUEST: Request for initial credit authorization.
UPDATE_REQUEST: Request to update the credit authorization.
TERMINATION_REQUEST: Request to terminate the session.
EVENT_REQUEST: Request for one-time event charging.

avp_cc_request_number.name: CC-Request-Number
avp_cc_request_number.doc: A sequence number for CCR messages within a session, starting at 0. Origin: Echoed from the CC-Request-Number AVP in the corresponding CCR message.

avp_multiple_services_credit_control.name: Multiple-Services-Credit-Control
avp_multiple_services_credit_control.doc: A grouped AVP containing credit-control information for one or more services. Origin: Constructed by the OCS to convey the credit grant details for each applicable rating group.

avp_granted_service_unit.name: Granted-Service-Unit
avp_granted_service_unit.doc: A grouped AVP that contains the amount of service units granted by the OCS. Origin: Determined by the OCS based on the subscriber's plan, balance, and operator policies.

avp_cc_total_octets.name: CC-Total-Octets
avp_cc_total_octets.doc: The total number of octets (uplink and downlink) granted to the user for this quota. Origin: Calculated by the OCS based on the subscriber's available credit and service plan.

avp_rating_group.name: Rating-Group
avp_rating_group.doc: An identifier for a specific group of service flows that are subject to the same charging treatment. Origin: Echoed from the CCR or determined by OCS based on service information.

avp_validity_time.name: Validity-Time
avp_validity_time.doc: The duration in seconds for which the granted service units are valid. Origin: Set by the OCS according to the charging policy for the subscriber or service.

avp_trigger.name: Trigger
avp_trigger.doc: A grouped AVP that defines events that will trigger a new credit-control request for re-authorization. Origin: Constructed by the OCS based on configured policies to ensure timely credit updates.

avp_trigger_type.name: Trigger-Type
avp_trigger_type.doc: The specific event that requires the PGW-C to send a credit-control update request. Origin: Selected by the OCS from a spec-defined list based on operator policy for session control.

doc.summary_en: The PGW-C, acting as a Diameter client (CTF), sends a Credit-Control-Request (CCR) with CC-Request-Type set to INITIAL_REQUEST to the Online Charging System (OCS). This message is used to request credit authorization for a new user data session, such as a PDN connection establishment.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is the first in a session for online charging. It is sent by the network element providing the service (e.g., PGW-C) to the OCS to check if the end user has sufficient credit to use the requested service and to reserve an initial credit quota. (TS 32.299 §5.2.2.1 V17.7.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when a user initiates a new service session that requires online charging, for example, upon activating a PDP Context or establishing a PDN Connection. The PGW-C identifies that the session's APN is subject to online charging. (TS 32.251 §5.2.2.1 V17.7.0)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the charging session.
- **CC-Request-Type**: Set to INITIAL_REQUEST (1).
- **CC-Request-Number**: Set to 0.
- **Subscription-Id**: Contains the user's identity (e.g., IMSI).
- **Called-Station-Id**: Contains the APN for the data session.
- **Multiple-Services-Credit-Control**: A grouped AVP used to request quotas for specific services, identified by Rating-Group.

### Successful Outcome
A successful outcome is a Credit-Control-Answer (CCA) message from the OCS with a Result-Code of DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001). The CCA will contain the granted service units (e.g., data volume, time) in the Multiple-Services-Credit-Control AVP. (TS 32.299 §5.2.2.2 V17.7.0)

### Error Handling
If the user has insufficient credit or is not authorized, the OCS will return a CCA with a specific failure Result-Code (e.g., DIAMETER_CREDIT_LIMIT_REACHED 4012). The PGW-C must then deny the service to the user. Other errors can relate to invalid AVPs or unknown users. (RFC 4006 §8)
doc.title: Credit-Control-Request [Initial]

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the Diameter session, generated by the client. It must be globally and eternally unique. The format is typically '<host>;<high32>;<low32>[;<optional>]'. Origin: Generated by the PGW-C to uniquely identify this charging session.

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The FQDN of the node that originated the message (the PGW-C). Origin: Configured identity of the sending PGW-C node.

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated the message. Origin: Configured identity of the sending PGW-C node's realm.

avp_destination_realm.name: Destination-Realm
avp_destination_realm.doc: The realm to which the message is to be routed. This is the realm of the OCS. Origin: Determined by the PGW-C via local configuration or a DRA to locate the correct OCS.

avp_auth_app_id.name: Auth-Application-Id
avp_auth_app_id.doc: Identifies the Diameter application as Ro (Online Charging). The value for Ro is 4. Origin: A spec-defined constant for the Ro Diameter application (RFC 4006).

avp_service_context_id.name: Service-Context-Id
avp_service_context_id.doc: Identifies the service-specific document defining the charging application logic. For 3GPP PS charging, this is a standardized value. Origin: A spec-defined value that identifies the 3GPP charging context being used.

avp_cc_request_type.name: CC-Request-Type
avp_cc_request_type.doc: Indicates the type of credit-control request. For the first request in a session, this is INITIAL_REQUEST. Origin: Set to INITIAL_REQUEST (1) as this is the first message sent to establish the charging session.
enum_doc: INITIAL_REQUEST: The first request of a session.
UPDATE_REQUEST: A subsequent request within the session.
TERMINATION_REQUEST: The final request to terminate the session.

avp_cc_request_number.name: CC-Request-Number
avp_cc_request_number.doc: A sequence number for requests within a session, starting at 0 for the initial request. Origin: Set to 0 as per Diameter base protocol requirements for the first request in a session.

avp_user_name.name: User-Name
avp_user_name.doc: The Network Access Identifier (NAI) of the user, often in the format 'IMSI@realm'. Origin: Constructed by the PGW-C from the user's IMSI and home realm information.

avp_subscription_id.name: Subscription-Id
avp_subscription_id.doc: A grouped AVP used to identify the end user. It contains the type of identifier and the identifier data itself. Origin: Populated by the PGW-C with the user's primary identifier obtained during session setup.

avp_subscription_id_type.name: Subscription-Id-Type
avp_subscription_id_type.doc: Specifies the type of identifier contained in Subscription-Id-Data. Origin: Set to END_USER_IMSI to indicate the accompanying data is the user's IMSI.

avp_subscription_id_data.name: Subscription-Id-Data
avp_subscription_id_data.doc: The actual user identifier, e.g., the IMSI. Origin: The user's IMSI, which is known to the PGW-C from the bearer establishment procedure.

avp_called_station_id.name: Called-Station-Id
avp_called_station_id.doc: In the context of GPRS/EPS, this AVP contains the Access Point Name (APN) selected for the data session. Origin: The APN value requested by the UE and authorized by the network during the PDN connection establishment.

avp_mscc.name: Multiple-Services-Credit-Control
avp_mscc.doc: A grouped AVP that allows credit control for multiple services in one request. Each instance corresponds to a specific rating group. Origin: Constructed by the PGW-C based on the PCC rules that map service data flows to specific rating groups requiring online charging.

avp_rating_group.name: Rating-Group
avp_rating_group.doc: Identifies a specific service or a set of service data flows for which credit is being requested. Origin: Determined from the active PCC (Policy and Charging Control) rule that applies to the user's traffic.

avp_requested_service_unit.name: Requested-Service-Unit
avp_requested_service_unit.doc: A grouped AVP that specifies the amount of service units being requested by the client. Origin: Populated by the PGW-C based on local policy, often requesting a default initial quota.

avp_cc_total_octets.name: CC-Total-Octets
avp_cc_total_octets.doc: The requested number of total octets (uplink + downlink). Origin: A default or policy-based value configured on the PGW-C for the initial credit request.

doc.summary_en: The Abort-Session-Answer (ASA) is sent by a PCRF (e.g., V-PCRF) to its peer (e.g., H-PCRF) to acknowledge the termination of an S9 Diameter session, in response to an Abort-Session-Request (ASR).
doc.steps: ['1. A PCRF (e.g., H-PCRF) determines that a specific S9 session must be terminated (e.g., due to administrative action or loss of underlying bearer context).', '2. The H-PCRF sends an Abort-Session-Request (ASR) to its peer V-PCRF, including the Session-Id of the session to be terminated. (RFC 6733 §8.5.1)', '3. The V-PCRF receives the ASR, locates the session, and proceeds to clean up all resources associated with it.', '4. The V-PCRF then sends this Abort-Session-Answer (ASA) message back to the H-PCRF, including a Result-Code to indicate the outcome. A success code confirms the session is terminated. (TS 29.215 §4.4.2)']
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Abort-Session-Answer (ASA) command, defined in the Diameter base protocol, is used to acknowledge the termination of a session. On the S9 interface, it confirms that a policy control session between the V-PCRF and H-PCRF has been successfully torn down following a request from the peer. (RFC 6733 §8.5.2)

### Triggers
This message is triggered by the reception of an Abort-Session-Request (ASR) from the peer PCRF. The receiving PCRF must stop all activity for the session and release any associated resources before sending the ASA. (TS 29.215 §4.4.2)

### Key AVPs
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the session being terminated. It is copied from the received ASR.
- **Result-Code**: Indicates whether the session was successfully aborted. DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001) is used for successful cases.
- **Origin-Host** / **Origin-Realm**: Identify the PCRF sending the ASA.

### Error Handling
If the session identified by the Session-Id in the ASR does not exist, the PCRF returns an ASA with the Result-Code set to DIAMETER_UNKNOWN_SESSION_ID (5002). Other error codes can be used for different failure scenarios as defined in RFC 6733. (RFC 6733 §8.5.2)

### Roaming Context
In a roaming scenario, this message flows between the V-PCRF in the visited network and the H-PCRF in the home network. The roles can be reversed, with the V-PCRF also able to initiate the session abortion by sending an ASR.
doc.title: Abort-Session-Answer (ASA)

session_id.name: Session-Id
session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session being aborted. This value is copied directly from the corresponding Abort-Session-Request (ASR). Origin: The value is copied from the corresponding Abort-Session-Request (ASR) message as per RFC 6733.

result_code.name: Result-Code
result_code.doc: Indicates the outcome of the session abortion request. A value of 2001 indicates success. Origin: The value is set by the responding PCRF to indicate the success or failure of the session abortion procedure.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_SUCCESS: The request was successful and the session has been aborted. (RFC 6733)
DIAMETER_UNKNOWN_SESSION_ID: The Session-ID in the ASR was not found. (RFC 6733)
DIAMETER_COMMAND_UNSUPPORTED: The ASR command is not supported by the peer. (RFC 6733)

origin_host.name: Origin-Host
origin_host.doc: The Diameter Identity of the node sending this answer (e.g., the V-PCRF). It must be a fully qualified domain name. Origin: This is the Diameter Host identity of the responding PCRF node, configured locally.

origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node sending this answer. Origin: This is the Diameter Realm of the responding PCRF node, configured locally.

origin_state_id.name: Origin-State-Id
origin_state_id.doc: A monotonically increasing number that is associated with the Origin-Host and is advanced each time the Diameter process is restarted. It is used to detect peer reboots. Origin: This value is a locally generated sequence number on the responding PCRF, incremented upon restart to manage session state.

doc.summary_en: The Abort-Session-Request (ASR) is sent over the S9 interface by one PCRF (e.g., H-PCRF) to another (e.g., V-PCRF) to request the termination of an established session for administrative or other reasons.
doc.steps:
1. An internal trigger occurs at the H-PCRF (e.g., administrative action, subscription change) or the H-PCRF receives an ASR from another entity, requiring the termination of a specific user's S9 session.
2. The H-PCRF constructs an Abort-Session-Request (ASR) command containing the Session-Id of the session to be terminated.
3. The ASR is sent towards the V-PCRF, typically routed via home and visited DRAs.
4. The V-PCRF receives the ASR, identifies the session, and begins the process of tearing down the associated resources.
5. The V-PCRF responds with an Abort-Session-Answer (ASA) to acknowledge the request. (TS 29.215 §4.4.8)
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Abort-Session-Request (ASR) command is used to terminate a session for reasons such as administrative intervention or resource management. On the S9 interface, it allows the H-PCRF to instruct the V-PCRF to terminate a specific S9 session, or vice versa. (RFC 6733 §8.4.1)

### Triggers
"The H-PCRF may initiate this procedure due to an internal trigger (e.g. administrative reasons) or on receiving a Diameter ASR for the Gx session from the BBERF." (TS 29.215 §4.4.8.1 V17.8.0)

### Key IEs
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the S9 session to be terminated.
- **Origin-Host/Realm**: Identifies the sending PCRF.
- **Destination-Host/Realm**: Identifies the target PCRF.
- **Auth-Application-Id**: Must be set to the S9 Application ID (16777252).
- **Abort-Cause**: (Optional) Provides a reason for the session termination.

### Roaming Context
In a roaming scenario, the `from` PCRF is typically the V-PCRF and the `to` PCRF is the H-PCRF, or vice-versa, depending on which entity initiates the session termination. The message is routed through Diameter Routing Agents (DRAs) in the respective home and visited PLMNs.

### Error Handling
If the V-PCRF receives an ASR with an unknown Session-Id, it shall return an ASA with the Result-Code set to 'UNKNOWN_SESSION_ID'. (RFC 6733 §8.4.2)
doc.title: Abort-Session-Request (ASR)

session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session, constructed by the client. It must be globally and eternally unique per session. Origin: Generated by the initiating PCRF to uniquely identify the session being aborted.

origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the node that originated the message. This is used to identify the sender. Origin: Set to the FQDN of the sending PCRF as configured in the node.

origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that originated the message. This is used for message routing. Origin: Set to the home realm of the sending PCRF as configured in the node.

destination_realm.doc: The realm to which the message is to be routed. This is used by Diameter agents for routing. Origin: Determined by the sending PCRF based on the session's routing information, typically the realm of the peer PCRF.

destination_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the destination host. This is used for routing to a specific peer. Origin: Determined by the sending PCRF based on the session's routing information, typically the FQDN of the peer PCRF.

auth_application_id.doc: Identifies the authentication application for this session. For S9, this is set to the S9 Application ID. Origin: Set to the fixed value for the S9 Diameter application (16777252) as defined in TS 29.215.

user_name.doc: The User-Name AVP contains the user's identity, often the IMSI in 3GPP contexts. Origin: Populated with the user's IMSI associated with the session being aborted.

abort_cause.doc: Indicates the reason why the session was aborted. Origin: Set by the initiating PCRF to inform the peer of the reason for session termination, based on an internal trigger.
enum_doc: BEARER_RELEASED - The bearer for which the session was established was released. (TS 29.215 §5.4.1)
INSUFFICIENT_SERVER_RESOURCES - The server is overloaded and needs to abort the session. (TS 29.215 §5.4.1)
INSUFFICIENT_BEARER_RESOURCES - The bearer has insufficient resources. (TS 29.215 §5.4.1)
PS_TO_CS_HANDOVER - The user's session has been handed over to the CS domain. (TS 29.215 §5.4.1)

origin_state_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session state at the origin host, used to detect reboots and session loss. Origin: Generated by the sending PCRF to represent its current state or session database version.

doc.summary_en: The Re-Auth-Answer (RAA) message is sent by the Visited PCRF (V-PCRF) to the Home PCRF (H-PCRF) in response to a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) message. It indicates the outcome of the re-authorization and/or re-validation of an existing session over the S9 interface.
doc.steps:
1. The H-PCRF sends an RAR to the V-PCRF to request re-authorization for a specific UE session, often triggered by subscription changes or policy updates in the HPLMN. (TS 29.215 §5.3.3)
2. The V-PCRF receives the RAR and performs the necessary re-authorization procedures, which may involve interaction with the BBERF.
3. The V-PCRF formulates the RAA message, setting the Result-Code AVP to indicate success (e.g., DIAMETER_SUCCESS) or failure.
4. The V-PCRF sends the RAA back to the H-PCRF, completing the transaction. (TS 29.215 §5.3.4)
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Re-Auth-Answer (RAA) command, indicated by the Command-Code field set to 258 and the 'R' bit cleared in the Command Flags field, is sent by the V-PCRF to the H-PCRF in response to the Re-Auth-Request command. (TS 29.215 §5.3.4 V17.4.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered by the reception of a Re-Auth-Request (RAR) at the V-PCRF from the H-PCRF.

### Key IEs (AVPs)
- **Session-Id**: Uniquely identifies the session being re-authorized, copied from the RAR.
- **Result-Code**: Indicates the outcome of the re-authorization attempt. A value of DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001) indicates success.
- **Origin-Host** / **Origin-Realm**: Identify the V-PCRF sending the response.
- **DRMP**: May be included to convey dynamic roaming management policy information.

### Error Handling
The Result-Code AVP is used to report failures. In addition to the base Diameter protocol result codes (RFC 6733), TS 29.215 defines S9-specific experimental result codes for cases like unknown session or unauthorized service. (TS 29.215 §5.5)

### Roaming Context
In a roaming scenario, the `from` PCRF is the V-PCRF in the visited network, and the `to` PCRF is the H-PCRF in the home network. The S9 interface connects these two entities to enable dynamic policy and charging control for roaming users.
doc.title: Re-Auth-Answer

avp_session_id.name: Session-Id
avp_session_id.doc: A unique identifier for the session, used to correlate messages belonging to the same session. It is copied from the corresponding request message. Origin: Copied from the corresponding Re-Auth-Request (RAR) message.

avp_result_code.name: Result-Code
avp_result_code.doc: Indicates the outcome of the request. A value of 2001 signifies success. Other values indicate various failure reasons. Origin: Set by the V-PCRF based on the outcome of processing the RAR.
enum_doc: DIAMETER_SUCCESS (2001): The request was successfully completed. (RFC 6733)
DIAMETER_UNKNOWN_SESSION_ID (5002): The session identified by the Session-Id is not found. (RFC 6733)
DIAMETER_AUTHORIZATION_REJECTED (5003): The request was rejected for authorization reasons. (RFC 6733)

avp_origin_host.name: Origin-Host
avp_origin_host.doc: The DiameterIdentity of the node that generated this message (the V-PCRF). Origin: Configured FQDN of the sending V-PCRF node.

avp_origin_realm.name: Origin-Realm
avp_origin_realm.doc: The realm of the node that generated this message. Origin: Configured realm of the sending V-PCRF node.

avp_drmp.name: DRMP
avp_drmp.doc: The DRMP AVP is of type Enumerated and it is used to indicate the dynamic roaming management policy. Origin: Included by the V-PCRF if dynamic roaming policy management is supported and applicable.
enum_doc: DISABLE_OFFLINE_CHARGING (0): This value is used to indicate that offline charging is disabled in the VPLMN.
ENABLE_OFFLINE_CHARGING (1): This value is used to indicate that offline charging is enabled in the VPLMN.

doc.summary_en: The AAA server sends a Session-Termination-Request (STR) to the ePDG to inform it that a specific user session is to be terminated. This is a server-initiated message, typically triggered by administrative actions, subscription changes, or other network events requiring session teardown.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Session-Termination-Request (STR) command, indicated by the Command-Code field set to 275 and the 'R' bit set in the Command Flags field, is sent by the AAA server to the ePDG to terminate a particular session. (RFC 6733 §8.4)

### Triggers
This procedure is initiated by the AAA server for various reasons, such as:
- The user's subscription has been terminated.
- An administrator has manually disconnected the user.
