cause_flags.doc: Contains flags related to the cause, such as PCE (PDN Connection aEctivation), BCE (Bearer Context Error), and CS (Cause Source). Origin: These flags are set by the SGW-C to provide additional context about the cause, as per TS 29.274.

recovery.name: Recovery (Restart Counter)
recovery.doc: Included if the sending node has restarted. The value is a counter that is incremented upon restart. Origin: This value is maintained by the SGW-C and included if it has recently restarted, as per GTPv2-C protocol specifications.

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: Contains vendor-specific or operator-specific information. Origin: This IE is populated by the SGW-C if proprietary extensions are configured or required for the session.

doc.summary_en: The PGW-C sends a Downlink Data Notification to the SGW-C to indicate the arrival of downlink data for a UE in Idle mode. This message initiates the network-triggered Service Request procedure to re-establish the necessary bearers.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This procedure is used by the PGW to notify the SGW that downlink data has been received for a UE for which there is no active user plane S5/S8 tunnel. The SGW then forwards this notification to the MME, which triggers paging of the UE. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.3, TS 29.274 §7.2.11.1 V17.5.0)

### Triggers
The PGW-C receives a downlink data packet for a UE in ECM-IDLE state. The PGW-C buffers the packet and sends this notification to the SGW-C associated with the UE's PDN connection. (TS 29.274 §7.2.11.2 V17.5.0)

### Key IEs
- **IMSI**: Optionally included to help the SGW-C/MME identify the UE context.
- **ARP (Allocation/Retention Priority)**: Indicates the priority of the bearer associated with the downlink data, which can influence paging priority.
- **EBI (EPS Bearer ID)**: If included, specifies the dedicated bearer for which data has arrived.

### Flow
1. Downlink data packet arrives at the PGW for an idle UE.
2. PGW-C sends Downlink Data Notification to the SGW-C.
3. SGW-C forwards the Downlink Data Notification to the MME.
4. MME initiates the Paging procedure.
5. Upon successful paging, the UE initiates the Service Request procedure to re-establish the S1-U and S5/S8-U bearers.
6. The PGW forwards the buffered downlink data to the UE. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.3)

### Error Handling
The SGW-C responds with a Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge. If the SGW-C cannot find the UE's context, it returns a cause value such as 'Context not found'. (TS 29.274 §7.2.11.3 V17.5.0)
doc.title: Downlink Data Notification

imsi.name: IMSI
imsi.doc: International Mobile Subscriber Identity. This IE may be included to assist the SGW-C and MME in finding the UE's context. Origin: Retrieved from the PGW-C's session context for the UE.

cause.name: Cause
cause.doc: Indicates the reason for sending the notification. For this message, it is typically not included, but if present, it would clarify the trigger. Origin: Set by the PGW-C based on the triggering condition.

cause_value.name: Cause Value
cause_value.doc: The specific reason code for the cause. Origin: Selected from a spec-defined enum of cause codes in TS 29.274.

cause_pce.name: PCE
cause_pce.doc: P-CSCF Restoration Indication. Indicates if the cause is due to a P-CSCF restoration. Origin: A flag set by the PGW-C if applicable to the cause.

cause_bce.name: BCE
cause_bce.doc: Bearer Context IE Error. Indicates an error related to a Bearer Context IE. Origin: A flag set by the PGW-C if applicable to the cause.

cause_cs.name: CS
cause_cs.doc: Cause Source. Indicates if the cause was generated by the user or the network. Origin: A flag set by the PGW-C to indicate the source of the cause.

arp.name: Allocation/Retention Priority (ARP)
arp.doc: Contains the priority level, pre-emption capability, and pre-emption vulnerability of the bearer associated with the downlink data. Origin: Derived from the QoS parameters of the EPS bearer that received the downlink data.

arp_pci.name: Pre-emption Capability (PCI)
arp_pci.doc: Indicates whether the bearer can pre-empt other bearers with lower priority. Origin: A configured policy value associated with the bearer's QoS profile.

arp_pl.name: Priority Level (PL)
arp_pl.doc: The priority level of the bearer, used for resource allocation and pre-emption decisions. Origin: A configured policy value associated with the bearer's QoS profile.

arp_pvi.name: Pre-emption Vulnerability (PVI)
arp_pvi.doc: Indicates whether the bearer is vulnerable to pre-emption by higher priority bearers. Origin: A configured policy value associated with the bearer's QoS profile.

ebi.name: EPS Bearer ID (EBI)
ebi.doc: EPS Bearer Identity. If the downlink data is for a dedicated bearer, this IE identifies that bearer. Origin: The identifier of the specific EPS bearer for which the downlink packet was received at the PGW-C.

paging_service_info.name: Paging and Service Information
paging_service_info.doc: Provides information to the MME to assist in paging, such as the Paging Policy Indication. Origin: Constructed by the PGW-C based on local policy or traffic characteristics.

psi_ebi.name: EPS Bearer ID (EBI)
psi_ebi.doc: EPS Bearer Identity within the Paging and Service Information IE. Origin: Copied from the main EBI of the bearer that triggered the notification.

psi_ppi.name: Paging Policy Indication (PPI)
psi_ppi.doc: Paging Policy Indication. Informs the MME about the relative importance of paging for this data. Origin: Determined by the PGW-C based on operator policy, possibly linked to the bearer's ARP value.

doc.summary_en: The SGW-C sends a Downlink Data Notification to the MME when it receives downlink data for a UE that is in ECM-IDLE state. This message triggers the MME to initiate paging procedures to locate the UE.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This procedure is used by the Serving GW to notify the MME or S4-SGSN that downlink data has been received for a UE in ECM-IDLE state. The MME/S4-SGSN shall then page the UE. (TS 29.274 §7.2.10 V17.4.0)

### Triggers
The SGW receives a downlink data packet for a UE whose S1-U or S4-U tunnel is established but for which there is no downlink user plane TEID in the SGW bearer context. This indicates the UE is in an idle state from the perspective of the access network. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.3 V17.4.0)

### Key IEs
- **IMSI**: Identifies the UE to be paged.
- **Allocation/Retention Priority (ARP)**: Indicates the priority of the downlink data, which can influence paging strategy.
- **EPS Bearer ID (EBI)**: Specifies the bearer for which data has arrived, if the notification is for a specific bearer.

### State & Ordering
Upon receiving this notification, the MME moves the UE from ECM-IDLE to ECM-CONNECTED (pending response to paging) and initiates the Paging procedure towards the eNBs within the UE's Tracking Area List. If the UE responds, the Service Request procedure is initiated by the UE, leading to the re-establishment of the S1 bearer. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.3 V17.4.0)
doc.title: Downlink Data Notification

imsi.name: IMSI
imsi.doc: International Mobile Subscriber Identity. This IE contains the IMSI of the UE for whom the downlink data is intended. It is used by the MME to identify the UE subscription and initiate paging. Origin: Included by the SGW to uniquely identify the UE for whom downlink data has arrived.

arp.name: Allocation/Retention Priority
arp.doc: Indicates the priority of the downlink data packet. This information can be used by the MME to prioritize paging and resource allocation. It contains priority level, pre-emption capability, and pre-emption vulnerability settings. Origin: Included by the SGW to inform the MME about the priority level of the received downlink data packet.

ebi.name: EPS Bearer ID
ebi.doc: EPS Bearer Identity. This IE identifies the specific EPS bearer for which the downlink data has been received. If not included, the notification is considered to be for any bearer of the UE. Origin: Included by the SGW to specify the bearer on which the downlink data was received.

doc.summary_en: The SGW-C sends a Downlink Data Notification to the MME or SGSN to indicate the arrival of downlink data for a UE in ECM-IDLE state. This message triggers the MME/SGSN to initiate paging of the UE.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
"The Downlink Data Notification message is sent from the SGW to the MME/SGSN as part of the Network Triggered Service Request procedure... to inform the MME/SGSN that downlink data has been received for a UE in ECM-IDLE state." (TS 29.274 §7.2.10.1 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when the SGW-C receives an indication from the SGW-U (or from within a combined SGW) that a downlink data packet has arrived for a UE whose S1-U bearer is released. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.3 V18.x.x)

### Key IEs
- **IMSI**: Used by the MME/SGSN to identify the UE for paging if the UE is in IDLE state.
- **ARP**: The Allocation and Retention Priority of the bearer can be included to influence paging priority.
- **EBI**: If included, it indicates the specific bearer for which data has arrived.
- **Cause**: Can indicate a failure at the SGW-U, such as 'DDN Failure'.

### State & Ordering
This message is sent when the UE is in ECM-IDLE state. It is the first step in the Network Triggered Service Request procedure, which aims to re-establish the UE's connection to the network to deliver the pending downlink data. The MME/SGSN responds with a Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge message. (TS 29.274 §7.2.10 V18.4.0)
doc.title: Downlink Data Notification

imsi.doc: International Mobile Subscriber Identity. This IE is included if the UE is in IDLE state and the MME/SGSN requires the IMSI for paging. Origin: Provided by the SGW-C from the UE's context if the UE is in an idle state.

cause.doc: Indicates the reason for the notification. It is included if the SGW-C received a DDN Failure Indication from the SGW-U. Origin: Set by the SGW-C, often reflecting a status received from the SGW-U, as per TS 29.274.

arp.doc: Allocation and Retention Priority. This IE contains the priority level, pre-emption capability, and pre-emption vulnerability settings for the bearer. Origin: Forwarded by the SGW-C from the bearer context to potentially influence paging priority.

arp_pl.doc: Priority Level (PL) indicates the priority of the bearer, with lower numbers representing higher priority. Origin: Part of the QoS profile for the bearer, configured in the HSS/PCF.

arp_pci.doc: Pre-emption Capability (PCI) indicates whether the bearer can preempt other bearers with lower priority levels. Origin: Part of the QoS profile for the bearer, configured in the HSS/PCF.

arp_pvi.doc: Pre-emption Vulnerability (PVI) indicates whether the bearer is subject to pre-emption by other bearers with higher priority levels. Origin: Part of the QoS profile for the bearer, configured in the HSS/PCF.

ebi.doc: EPS Bearer ID. Identifies the specific bearer for which downlink data has arrived. Origin: Included by the SGW-C to specify which of the UE's bearers received the data.

imsi_unau.doc: IMSI Unauthenticated Indicator. If present, this IE indicates that the IMSI belongs to an unauthenticated UE. Origin: Set by the SGW-C if the UE's security context indicates it is unauthenticated.

private_ext.doc: Container for vendor-specific information. Origin: Added by the network equipment vendor for proprietary features.

enterprise_id.doc: A unique identifier for a vendor or enterprise, as assigned by IANA. Origin: A unique identifier assigned to a vendor by IANA.

proprietary_data.doc: Vendor-specific data. The content and format are defined by the vendor identified in the Enterprise ID. Origin: Vendor-defined data specific to the feature being implemented.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends the Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge to the SGW-C to acknowledge the receipt of a Downlink Data Notification message and to provide the result of the paging procedure.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent by the MME to the SGW-C in response to a Downlink Data Notification. It informs the SGW-C whether the UE was successfully paged and is now reachable, or if paging failed. (TS 29.274 §7.2.10 V17.8.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when an MME receives a Downlink Data Notification message from an SGW-C for a UE in IDLE state. The MME initiates paging for the UE and sends this acknowledgement upon completion or failure of the paging attempt. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.3)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates the outcome, such as 'Request Accepted' if the UE responded to paging, or 'UE context without S1-U connection' if paging is initiated. A value of 'Paging Failed' indicates the UE did not respond.
- **Data Notification Delay**: If paging fails, the MME can suggest a delay before the SGW-C should re-notify.
- **IMSI**: Included if the UE was successfully contacted, allowing the SGW-C to correlate the notification with a specific subscriber.

### State & Ordering
This message follows a Downlink Data Notification. If the Cause value indicates success, the SGW-C can start forwarding the buffered downlink data towards the eNB. If it indicates failure, the SGW-C may discard the data or wait for the suggested delay before retrying. The TEID-C in the header is set to 0 as the message is not sent on a specific tunnel. (TS 29.274 §7.2.10 V17.8.0)
doc.title: Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge

cause.name: Cause
cause.doc: Indicates the result of the paging procedure initiated by the MME in response to the Downlink Data Notification. A value of 'Request accepted' means the UE has been successfully contacted. Origin: The value is set by the MME to report the outcome of the paging procedure initiated by the preceding Downlink Data Notification.

cause_value.name: Cause Value
cause_value.doc: The actual cause code. e.g., 16 for 'Request accepted'. Origin: This is a spec-defined enum value selected by the MME from TS 29.274 Table 8.4-1.

cause_flags.name: Flags
cause_flags.doc: Flags associated with the cause value (PCE, BCE, CS). Origin: These flags are set by the MME according to the rules in TS 29.274 for the specific cause value.

data_notification_delay.name: Data Notification Delay
data_notification_delay.doc: Specifies the delay in 50 millisecond increments that the SGW-C should wait before re-sending a Downlink Data Notification. Included if paging fails. Origin: The value is provided by the MME to indicate a suggested delay for the SGW before resending the notification.

recovery.name: Recovery (Restart Counter)
recovery.doc: The restart counter of the MME. This IE is included if the MME has restarted since its last communication with the SGW-C peer. Origin: This value is the MME's own restart counter, included when it has restarted since last communicating with the SGW.

imsi.name: IMSI
imsi.doc: International Mobile Subscriber Identity of the UE that was successfully paged. Origin: This is the IMSI of the UE that successfully responded to the paging request.

dl_low_latency_throttling.name: DL Low Latency Traffic Throttling
dl_low_latency_throttling.doc: Provides throttling information for downlink low latency traffic, including a delay value and a factor. Origin: The value is determined by the MME based on local policy or congestion conditions to throttle low latency traffic.

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: Contains vendor-specific information. Origin: This IE contains vendor-specific information and its value is determined by the equipment manufacturer's implementation.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends the Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge to the SGW to acknowledge the receipt of a Downlink Data Notification message. This message informs the SGW about the outcome of the paging procedure initiated by the MME for the UE.
doc.steps: ['1. The SGW sends a Downlink Data Notification message to the MME/SGSN upon receiving downlink data for an idle UE.', '2. The MME/SGSN receives the notification and initiates paging for the UE.', '3. After the paging procedure concludes (successfully or not), the MME/SGSN sends a Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge message to the SGW.', "4. The 'Cause' IE in the acknowledge message indicates whether the UE was successfully contacted or if a paging failure occurred (e.g., UE temporarily not reachable)."]
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge message is sent by the MME/SGSN to the SGW as a response to a Downlink Data Notification message. (TS 29.274 §7.2.10.1 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
This message is sent by the MME/SGSN upon receiving a Downlink Data Notification from the SGW and after attempting to page the UE. (TS 29.274 §7.2.10.1 V18.3.0)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates the result of the paging procedure. A value of 'Request accepted' means the UE has been paged successfully. Other values indicate failure reasons.
- **Data Notification Delay**: If the MME/SGSN decides to buffer the notification (e.g., due to paging restrictions), this IE indicates the buffering delay.
- **Recovery (Timestamp)**: This is an echo of the restart counter from the SGW, used for node failure recovery mechanisms.

### Error Handling
The outcome, including failures, is communicated via the mandatory 'Cause' IE. For example, if paging fails, the cause value will be set to 'UE is temporarily not reachable'. (TS 29.274 §8.4 V18.3.0)
doc.title: Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge

cause.doc: Indicates the result of the paging procedure initiated in response to the Downlink Data Notification. A value of 'Request accepted' signifies success, while other values denote specific failure reasons. (TS 29.274 §8.4). Origin: This value is determined by the MME based on the outcome of the UE paging procedure.
enum_doc: Semantic error in the TFT operation

data_notification_delay.doc: Indicates the delay in 50ms increments that the MME/SGSN intends to buffer the Downlink Data Notification. This is used if the MME cannot page the UE immediately. (TS 29.274 §8.105). Origin: This value is configured in the MME policy for handling paging and network congestion.

recovery.doc: This IE contains the restart counter of the sending node. The receiving node shall echo the value of this IE in the response message. It is used to detect node restarts. (TS 29.274 §8.16). Origin: This value is copied from the Recovery IE in the corresponding request message.

private_extension.doc: A vendor-specific or operator-specific information element. (TS 29.274 §8.61). Origin: This value is provided by the network element vendor for proprietary extensions.

doc.summary_en: The SGW-C sends the Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge to the PGW-C to confirm the receipt of a Downlink Data Notification message and that the paging process for the UE has been initiated.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is used by the MME or SGW-C to acknowledge a Downlink Data Notification message from the PGW-C. It signals that the request to page the UE has been accepted and processed. (TS 29.274 §7.2.12 V17.8.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when an MME/SGW-C receives a Downlink Data Notification message from a PGW-C for a UE in an idle state. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.3)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates the result of the operation, typically 'Request accepted'.
- **Data Notification Delay**: Optionally informs the PGW-C of the expected delay for the UE to respond to paging.
- **Recovery (Restart Counter)**: Sent if the SGW-C has restarted.

### Error Handling
If the paging initiation fails, the SGW-C sends this message with a Cause value indicating the reason for failure, such as 'UE context without S11-U/S4-U F-TEID'. (TS 29.274 §7.2.12 V17.8.0)
doc.title: Downlink Data Notification Acknowledge

cause.name: Cause
cause.doc: Indicates if the Downlink Data Notification was accepted and paging was initiated. A value of 'Request accepted' (16) signifies success. (TS 29.274 §8.4). Origin: The value is determined by the SGW-C based on its ability to successfully initiate the paging procedure for the UE.

data_notification_delay.name: Data Notification Delay
data_notification_delay.doc: Optional. Informs the PGW-C of the expected delay in seconds until the UE is expected to respond to the paging request. The value is encoded in units of 50 milliseconds. (TS 29.274 §8.63). Origin: This value is based on local MME/SGW-C configuration and knowledge of the paging area's characteristics.

recovery.name: Recovery (Restart Counter)
recovery.doc: Optional. Included if the sending node (SGW-C/MME) has restarted since last communicating with the peer PGW-C. The value is a counter that is incremented upon restart. (TS 29.274 §8.13). Origin: The value is read from a persistent local restart counter maintained by the SGW-C.

imsi.name: IMSI
imsi.doc: Conditional. The International Mobile Subscriber Identity of the UE. Included if the MME/SGW-C has it and the PGW-C may not have it, to help the PGW-C identify the UE context. (TS 29.274 §8.2). Origin: This value is retrieved from the UE's context stored at the MME/SGW-C.

dl_low_latency_throttling.name: DL Low Latency Traffic Throttling
dl_low_latency_throttling.doc: Optional. Provides throttling information for downlink low latency traffic, indicating a delay value and a factor. (TS 29.274 §8.133). Origin: This value is determined by the SGW-C based on local policy and current network load conditions for low latency services.

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: Optional. Contains vendor-specific or operator-specific information. (TS 29.274 §8.20). Origin: This is a vendor-specific value configured on the SGW-C.

doc.summary_en: The old MME sends the Forward Relocation Complete Acknowledge to the new MME to acknowledge the Forward Relocation Complete Notification message. This message confirms that the old MME is aware that the handover has been successfully completed on the target side. (TS 29.274 §7.2.14 V18.4.0)
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent by the old MME/S4-SGSN to the new MME/S4-SGSN as part of an S1/X2-based handover procedure. It acknowledges the `Forward Relocation Complete Notification` and signals that the old MME can now release the resources associated with the UE context. (TS 23.401 §5.5.1.2.2)

### Triggers
The old MME receives a `Forward Relocation Complete Notification` from the new MME, indicating that the UE has successfully arrived at the target cell. The old MME then responds with this acknowledgement. (TS 29.274 §7.2.14)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates the acceptance of the notification. A value of 'Request accepted' signifies success.
- **Secondary RAT Usage Data Report**: Conditionally included if requested by the new MME in the `Forward Relocation Request` to report data usage over a secondary RAT (e.g., in an E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity scenario).

### State & Ordering
This message is one of the final steps in the handover completion phase. Upon sending this message, the old MME can proceed with releasing the S1-U bearer and other UE-associated resources. The timer at the old MME/SGSN for the Forward Relocation is stopped on receipt of the `Forward Relocation Complete Notification` message.
doc.title: Forward Relocation Complete Acknowledge

children.name: Secondary RAT Usage Data Report Item

cause.doc: Indicates if the Forward Relocation Complete Notification was accepted by the old MME. A value of 'Request accepted' is typical for success. Origin: This value is set by the old MME to inform the new MME about the outcome of processing the preceding notification message.

recovery.doc: Included by the old MME if it has restarted since the last communication with the new MME. It contains the restart counter. Origin: This value is sourced from the sending node's internal state, specifically its restart counter, to signal a recovery situation to the peer.

sec_rat_usage_data_report.doc: This IE is included if it was requested by the new MME in the Forward Relocation Request. It contains data volumes for the secondary RAT. Origin: This IE is conditionally included based on a flag in a previous message in the handover flow (Forward Relocation Request).

sec_rat_usage_data_report_item.doc: A container for usage data for a specific E-RAB over the secondary RAT. Origin: This container groups related data points for a single data radio bearer as collected by the network.

sec_rat_type.doc: Specifies the type of the secondary RAT, e.g., NR. Origin: This value is determined by the radio access technology used as the secondary leg in a dual connectivity setup.

erab_id.doc: E-UTRAN Radio Access Bearer Identifier. Identifies the bearer for which the report applies. Origin: This value is the identifier assigned to the specific E-RAB during its setup procedure.

start_timestamp.doc: The start time of the measurement period, encoded as NTP timestamp. Origin: This value is a timestamp recorded by the network node when the data collection period began.

end_timestamp.doc: The end time of the measurement period, encoded as NTP timestamp. Origin: This value is a timestamp recorded by the network node when the data collection period ended.

usage_data_dl.doc: Volume of downlink data transferred in octets. Origin: This is a counter value representing the measured downlink data volume for the bearer over the specified period.

usage_data_ul.doc: Volume of uplink data transferred in octets. Origin: This is a counter value representing the measured uplink data volume for the bearer over the specified period.

private_ext.doc: Container for vendor-specific or operator-specific information. Origin: This IE is populated with proprietary data defined by a specific vendor or operator for their own network extensions.

enterprise_id.doc: The IANA-assigned enterprise number of the vendor defining the extension. Origin: This is a standardized identifier assigned by IANA to a specific organization.

proprietary_value.doc: The vendor-specific data. Origin: The content and format of this field are defined by the vendor identified in the Enterprise ID.

doc.summary_en: The new MME sends the Forward Relocation Complete Notification to the old MME to indicate that the handover procedure has been successfully completed for the UE. This allows the old MME to release the resources associated with the UE.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent from the new MME/S4-SGSN to the old MME/S4-SGSN to indicate that the SRNS relocation procedure has been concluded. (TS 29.274 §7.2.12 V18.2.0)

### Triggers
The procedure is triggered when the new MME receives a Handover Notify message from the target eNodeB, confirming the UE has successfully accessed the target cell. This notification is a key step in the S1-based handover procedure. (TS 23.401 §5.5.1.2.2 V18.4.0, step 15a)

### State & Ordering
This message is sent after the data path has been switched to the target side and the UE is active in the target cell. Upon receiving this notification, the old MME knows it is no longer responsible for the UE and can initiate the release of the S1-MME and other resources it was holding for that UE. If the Serving GW was changed, the old MME will also delete the session on the old SGW.

### Key IEs
- **IMSI**: Included if available, to identify the UE context.
- **Indication Flags**: Can be used to signal specific outcomes, such as a change in the Serving GW (S1-UCI flag).
doc.title: Forward Relocation Complete Notification

imsi.name: IMSI
imsi.doc: International Mobile Subscriber Identity. Uniquely identifies the subscriber. This IE is included if available to the new MME. Origin: Retrieved from the UE's context which was transferred to the new MME during the handover procedure.

indication_flags.name: Indication Flags
indication_flags.doc: A bitmask of flags providing additional information to the peer entity. This IE is included if any of the flags is set to 1. Origin: Set by the new MME based on the conditions and outcome of the handover procedure.

s1_uci.name: S1-U Change Indication (S1-UCI)
s1_uci.doc: When set to 1, this flag indicates that the S1-U bearer has been changed, which implies a Serving GW relocation occurred. Origin: Set by the new MME if the Serving GW has been relocated as part of the inter-MME handover procedure.

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: Container for vendor-specific information. Origin: Populated by the network equipment vendor for proprietary features not covered by the 3GPP standard.

doc.summary_en: The Forward Relocation Request is sent from a source MME to a target MME to convey necessary information for handing over a UE from a source eNB to a target eNB. This message initiates the handover resource allocation at the target side.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This procedure is used by the source MME to request the target MME to establish the necessary resources for a UE handover. It forwards the UE's context, including security information, session details (PDN connections), and the S1AP message to be sent to the target eNB. (TS 29.274 §7.2.9 V18.3.0)

### Triggers
The source MME initiates this procedure upon receiving an S1AP Handover Required message from the source eNB, where the target eNB is served by a different MME. (TS 23.401 §5.5.1.2.2 V18.3.0)

### Key IEs
- **IMSI**: Uniquely identifies the UE.
- **Sender F-TEID for Control Plane**: The S10 interface address of the source MME.
- **PDN Connections**: Contains details for each of the UE's active data sessions.
- **MM Context**: Contains the UE's mobility management and security context.
- **F-Container**: Carries the S1AP Handover Request message, which the target MME will forward to the target eNB.
- **Target Identification**: Specifies the target cell for the handover.

### State & Ordering
This message is sent after the source MME decides to initiate a handover to a new MME. It is followed by a Forward Relocation Response from the target MME, which indicates whether the resource allocation was successful. If successful, the source MME commands the UE to hand over. (TS 23.401 §5.5.1.2.2 V18.3.0)
doc.title: Forward Relocation Request

children.name: PGW S5/S8 F-TEID for Control Plane or PMIP

imsi.doc: International Mobile Subscriber Identity. A unique identifier for the mobile subscriber. Origin: This value is part of the UE's context held by the source MME.

sender_f_teid_c.doc: Sender's Fully Qualified Tunnel Endpoint Identifier for the Control Plane. This IE contains the S10 IP address and TEID of the source MME. Origin: This F-TEID is allocated by the source MME for the S10 control plane interface.

mme_ue_s1ap_id.doc: MME UE S1AP ID. This ID uniquely identifies the UE over the S1AP interface within the source MME. Origin: This ID was allocated by the source MME when the UE's S1AP association was established.

pdn_connections.doc: Contains a list of Information Elements for each PDN connection of the UE. Origin: This container holds the context for all active PDN connections for the UE, maintained by the source MME.

apn.doc: Access Point Name. Identifies the packet data network that the UE wants to communicate with. Origin: This value was provided by the UE or subscribed in the HSS and is part of the active PDN connection context.

sgw_s11_f_teid.doc: SGW F-TEID for the S11/S4 interface. This is the control plane address of the SGW for this PDN connection. Origin: This F-TEID was provided by the SGW during the PDN connection establishment procedure.

pgw_s5s8_f_teid.doc: PGW S5/S8 F-TEID for the control plane. This is the control plane address of the PGW for this PDN connection. Origin: This F-TEID was provided by the PGW during the PDN connection establishment procedure.

target_identification.doc: Identifies the target for the handover, typically containing the Target eNB ID. Origin: This information is received by the source MME from the source eNB in the S1AP Handover Required message.

mm_context.doc: Mobility Management Context. Contains the UE's security and mobility state information. Origin: This contains the UE's mobility and security context, which is maintained by the source MME.

security_mode.doc: Indicates the selected NAS security algorithms for integrity and ciphering. Origin: This reflects the current security algorithm in use, as negotiated between the UE and the source MME.

nas_keys_and_seq.doc: Contains the NAS security keys (K-ASME) and sequence numbers. Origin: These security parameters are derived during the authentication procedure and updated by the source MME.

ue_security_capability.doc: UE Security Capability. Informs the network about the security algorithms supported by the UE. Origin: This information was provided by the UE to the source MME during the attach or a tracking area update procedure.

f_container.doc: Forwarding Container. This IE transparently carries a container from a source node to a target node. In this case, it carries the S1AP Handover Request message. Origin: This container is created by the source MME to encapsulate the S1AP Handover Request message for the target eNB.

recovery.doc: This IE is used for synchronization of restart counters between nodes. It is included if the sending node has restarted. Origin: This is a spec-defined value indicating the restart counter of the sending node.

private_extension.doc: This IE contains vendor-specific or operator-specific information. Origin: This is a placeholder for vendor-specific information as defined in the 3GPP specification.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends a Modify Bearer Request to the SGW to modify the parameters of one or more existing EPS bearers for a UE. This is commonly used during handover procedures to update the user plane tunnel endpoint (S1-U F-TEID) or to change the Quality of Service (QoS) for a bearer.
doc.steps:
1. An event triggers the MME to modify an EPS bearer, such as an S1-based handover request from an eNodeB or a network-initiated QoS update from the HSS/PCRF.
2. The MME constructs the Modify Bearer Request message, including the Bearer Context to be Modified IE which specifies the EBI and the new parameters, like the target eNodeB's S1-U F-TEID.
3. The MME sends the message to the SGW over the S11 interface, targeting the SGW's control plane TEID established for the UE's session.
4. The SGW receives the request, updates its bearer contexts, and may signal the PGW if the modification affects the S5/S8 interface. It then sends a Modify Bearer Response back to the MME.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
"The Modify Bearer Request message shall be sent from the MME to the SGW as part of E-UTRAN initiated E-RAB modification, E-UTRAN initiated E-RAB release, E-UTRAN S1 based handover, and Inter RAT handover procedures..." (TS 29.274 §7.2.7.1 V18.3.0).

### Triggers
- **S1 Handover**: When a UE moves to a new eNodeB, the MME instructs the SGW to switch the S1-U user plane path to the target eNodeB.
- **QoS Modification**: When the network (e.g., PCRF) decides to change the QoS parameters of an existing bearer.
- **UE-AMBR Modification**: When the UE's Aggregate Maximum Bit Rate for non-GBR bearers is updated based on subscription changes.

### Key IEs
- **Bearer Context to be Modified**: This grouped IE is mandatory and carries the details of the changes for each affected bearer.
- **EPS Bearer ID (EBI)**: Identifies the specific bearer to modify within the bearer context group.
- **S1-U eNB F-TEID**: Provides the new user plane tunnel endpoint at the target eNodeB, which is essential for handovers.

### Error Handling
If the SGW cannot fulfill the request, it sends a Modify Bearer Response with a Cause IE indicating the reason for failure (e.g., "Context not found", "Invalid QoS combination"). The MME then handles the failure according to the procedure that triggered the request. (TS 29.274 §7.2.7.2 V18.3.0)

### Ordering & State
A PDN connection and at least one EPS bearer must already be active for the UE before this message can be sent. It modifies an existing session context in both the MME and the SGW.
doc.title: Modify Bearer Request

imsi.name: International Mobile Subscriber Identity
imsi.doc: A globally unique identifier for a mobile subscriber. It is conditionally included if the MME has it stored in the UE context. Origin: The MME retrieves this identifier from the UE's context, which was originally obtained from the UE or HSS during the Attach procedure.

sender_f_teid_c.name: Sender F-TEID for Control Plane
sender_f_teid_c.doc: The Fully Qualified Tunnel Endpoint Identifier of the MME for the S11 control plane interface. Origin: This value is allocated by the MME to identify its control plane endpoint for this specific S11 session.

bearer_context_to_be_modified.name: Bearer Context to be Modified
bearer_context_to_be_modified.doc: A grouped Information Element that contains a list of bearers to be modified along with their new parameters. Origin: The MME populates this IE based on the specific bearers affected by the triggering event, such as a handover or QoS change.

ebi.name: EPS Bearer ID
ebi.doc: EPS Bearer Identity. This uniquely identifies an EPS bearer for a specific UE. It is mandatory within the Bearer Context group. Origin: This value is assigned by the MME during bearer establishment to uniquely identify the EPS bearer within the PDN connection.

s1u_enb_f_teid.name: S1-U eNB F-TEID
s1u_enb_f_teid.doc: The F-TEID of the target eNodeB for the S1-U user plane interface. This is critical for path switching during handovers. Origin: The MME receives this value from the target eNodeB via an S1AP message (e.g., Handover Request Acknowledge) during the handover preparation phase.

ambr.name: Aggregate Maximum Bit Rate
ambr.doc: The Aggregate Maximum Bit Rate for all non-GBR bearers of a UE. This IE is included if the UE-AMBR needs to be updated. Origin: This value is typically provided by the HSS in the subscription data and enforced by the MME and PGW.

doc.summary_en: The SGW sends a Modify Bearer Request to the PGW to modify an existing EPS bearer. This is typically triggered by a request from the MME due to changes in radio conditions, QoS requirements, or handovers.
doc.steps:
1. The MME initiates a bearer modification procedure towards the SGW (e.g., via an S11 Modify Bearer Request).
2. The SGW processes the request and determines that the modification needs to be propagated to the PGW.
3. The SGW constructs and sends the Modify Bearer Request over the S5/S8 interface to the PGW, including the Bearer Context to be modified with the new parameters (e.g., updated SGW User Plane F-TEID or QoS).
4. The PGW updates its bearer context. If dynamic PCC is deployed, the PGW may interact with the PCRF to authorize the modification.
5. The PGW responds with a Modify Bearer Response to the SGW, indicating the outcome.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
"The Modify Bearer Request message shall be sent from the SGW to the PGW as part of E-UTRAN initiated bearer modification with bearer QoS update procedure, or the HSS initiated subscribed QoS modification procedure, or the E-UTRAN initiated bearer modification procedure without bearer QoS update, or UE requested bearer resource modification procedure, or UE initiated bearer modification with bearer QoS update procedure." (TS 29.274 §7.2.7.1 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when the SGW receives a bearer modification request from the MME on the S11 interface, which could be due to a variety of reasons including service requests, handovers, or QoS updates initiated by the network or UE. (TS 23.401 §5.4.2)

### Key IEs
- **Bearer Context to be modified**: A grouped IE that specifies which bearer to modify and contains the new parameters.
  - **EPS Bearer ID (EBI)**: Identifies the bearer to be modified.
  - **S5/S8-U SGW F-TEID**: Provides the updated user plane tunnel endpoint at the SGW.
- **APN-AMBR**: May be included to update the aggregate maximum bit rate for the APN.
- **Indication Flags**: Used to convey additional information, such as overload control indications.

### Error Handling
If the PGW cannot fulfill the request, it sends a Modify Bearer Response with an appropriate Cause value, such as 'Context not found' or 'Service denied'. (TS 29.274 §7.2.8)

### References
- TS 29.274 §7.2.7
- TS 23.401 §5.4.2
doc.title: Modify Bearer Request

children.name: SGW F-TEID for S5/S8 User Plane

bearer_context_to_modify.name: Bearer Context to be modified
bearer_context_to_modify.doc: A grouped IE containing the EPS Bearer ID and other IEs to be modified for that bearer. Origin: This IE is mandated by the procedure to specify which bearer context is targeted for modification.

ebi.name: EPS Bearer ID
ebi.doc: Identifies the EPS bearer to be modified. The value is a 4-bit integer. Origin: This value is provided by the MME and uniquely identifies the bearer within the UE's PDN connection.

sgw_u_fteid.name: SGW F-TEID for S5/S8 User Plane
sgw_u_fteid.doc: The new Fully Qualified Tunnel Endpoint Identifier for the user plane traffic on the S5/S8 interface, allocated by the SGW. Origin: This value is generated by the SGW to establish or update the user plane path towards the PGW.

apn_ambr.name: APN-AMBR
apn_ambr.doc: Aggregate Maximum Bit Rate for the APN, containing separate values for uplink and downlink. This is sent if the QoS for the entire PDN connection is being updated. Origin: This value is typically derived from subscription data in the HSS and forwarded by the MME during a QoS modification procedure.

apn_ambr_ul.name: APN-AMBR for Uplink
apn_ambr_ul.doc: The uplink APN Aggregate Maximum Bit Rate in kbps. Origin: This is a component of the APN-AMBR, sourced from subscription data or policy control.

apn_ambr_dl.name: APN-AMBR for Downlink
apn_ambr_dl.doc: The downlink APN Aggregate Maximum Bit Rate in kbps. Origin: This is a component of the APN-AMBR, sourced from subscription data or policy control.

indication_flags.name: Indication Flags
indication_flags.doc: A set of flags providing additional information to the PGW. For example, it can indicate SGW overload control activation. Origin: These flags are set by the sending node (SGW) to signal its state or specific handling requests to the receiving node (PGW).

doc.summary_en: The PGW sends a Modify Bearer Response to the SGW to acknowledge the Modify Bearer Request. This message indicates the result of the bearer modification procedure, either success or failure, for the session and for individual bearers.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent by the PGW to the SGW as a response to a Modify Bearer Request. It confirms whether the requested bearer modifications (e.g., QoS update, TFT modification) have been accepted or rejected. (TS 29.274 §7.2.7.2 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when a PGW receives a Modify Bearer Request from an SGW. The PGW processes the request and formulates this response. (TS 29.274 §7.2.7.1 V18.4.0)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates the overall result of the operation (e.g., 'Request accepted').
- **Bearer Contexts Modified**: A grouped IE containing details for each bearer that was successfully modified. It includes the EBI, a cause code for that specific bearer, and potentially updated F-TEIDs.
- **Bearer Contexts Marked for Removal**: A grouped IE for bearers that the PGW has decided to deactivate as a result of the modification procedure.
- **Recovery (Restart Counter)**: Included if the PGW has recently restarted.

### Error Handling
If the overall request fails, the PGW sets the main 'Cause' IE to an appropriate rejection value. If only specific bearers fail modification, the 'Cause' IE within the 'Bearer Contexts Modified' or 'Bearer Contexts Marked for Removal' grouped IEs will indicate the specific reason for failure for that EBI. (TS 29.274 §7.2.7.2 V18.4.0)
doc.title: Modify Bearer Response

cause.doc: Indicates if the request was accepted or the reason for failure at the message level. A value of 16 means 'Request accepted'. Origin: Set by the PGW to indicate the overall outcome of the Modify Bearer Request procedure.
enum_doc: Request rejected for a PMIP reason

bearer_contexts_modified.doc: A grouped IE that contains a list of bearers that were successfully modified. For each bearer, it includes the EBI, a cause value, and optionally new F-TEIDs. Origin: Populated by the PGW with the details of each bearer context that was successfully modified as requested by the SGW.

ebi.doc: EPS Bearer Identity. A 4-bit field identifying the specific bearer context. Values 5-15 are for dedicated bearers. Origin: Copied from the corresponding bearer context in the incoming Modify Bearer Request to identify which bearer this information applies to.

cause_bearer.doc: Indicates the result of the modification for this specific bearer context. A value of 16 means 'Request accepted'. Origin: Set by the PGW to indicate the outcome for the specific bearer identified by the EBI within this grouped IE.

fteid_pgw_u.doc: Fully Qualified Tunnel Endpoint Identifier for the user plane (S5/S8 interface), provided by the PGW. It contains the interface type, TEID, and IP address for the user plane tunnel. Origin: Generated by the PGW to provide its updated user plane tunnel endpoint address and TEID for the modified bearer.

recovery.doc: The Restart Counter. This IE is included if the sending node has restarted since its last communication with the peer. The value is locally generated and incremented upon each restart. Origin: Included by the PGW if it has restarted since last communicating with this SGW, providing its current restart counter value as defined in recovery procedures.

doc.summary_en: The Serving Gateway (SGW) sends the Modify Bearer Response to the Mobility Management Entity (MME) to acknowledge a Modify Bearer Request. It indicates whether the requested modifications to bearer contexts were successful and provides updated user plane F-TEIDs if necessary.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is used by the SGW to respond to a Modify Bearer Request from the MME. It confirms the modification of one or more EPS bearers for a UE. (TS 29.274 §7.2.8 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
The SGW sends this message upon receiving and processing a Modify Bearer Request from the MME.

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates the overall result of the operation (e.g., 'Request accepted', 'Request accepted partially').
- **Bearer Contexts Modified**: A grouped IE that lists each bearer that was successfully modified. It includes the EBI, a cause value for that specific bearer, and potentially updated F-TEID(s) for the user plane.
- **Bearer Contexts Marked for Removal**: A grouped IE listing bearers that could not be modified and are now marked for deletion by the SGW.
- **Recovery (Restart Counter)**: Included if the SGW has restarted, to allow the MME to synchronize state.

### Variants / Branches
- If all bearers are modified successfully, the message contains a 'Request accepted' cause and one or more 'Bearer Contexts Modified' IEs.
- If some bearers are modified but others fail, the cause is 'Request accepted partially'. The message will contain both 'Bearer Contexts Modified' for the successful ones and 'Bearer Contexts Marked for Removal' for the failed ones.
- If the entire request fails, a relevant failure cause is returned, and no bearer context IEs are included. (TS 29.274 §7.2.8 V18.4.0)

### Ordering & State
This message is a direct response to a Modify Bearer Request and concludes that specific transaction. The MME uses the information to update its session state and may proceed with subsequent signaling towards the eNodeB.
doc.title: Modify Bearer Response

cause.name: Cause
cause.doc: Indicates the overall result of the Modify Bearer Request. A value of 'Request accepted' (16) signifies that the SGW successfully processed the request. Origin: The value is determined by the SGW based on the outcome of processing the Modify Bearer Request.
enum_doc: New PDN type due to network preference
New PDN type due to single address bearer only
Version not supported by next peer
Semantic error in the TFT operation
Syntactic error in the TFT operation
UE context without TFT already activated
Semantic error in the TAD operation
Syntactic error in the TAD operation
Unable to page UE due to Suspension

bearer_ctx_mod.name: Bearer Contexts Modified
bearer_ctx_mod.doc: A grouped IE that contains the list of bearers that were successfully modified. This IE is mandatory on success. Origin: This IE is populated by the SGW with the details of bearers that were successfully modified as requested by the MME.

ebi.name: EPS Bearer ID
ebi.doc: EPS Bearer Identity. Uniquely identifies a bearer for a specific UE. The value is 4 bits, ranging from 5 to 15. Origin: This value is copied from the corresponding bearer context in the incoming Modify Bearer Request.

bearer_cause.name: Cause
bearer_cause.doc: Indicates the result for a specific bearer within the grouped IE. It must be 'Request accepted' for bearers in this list. Origin: The value is set to 'Request accepted' by the SGW for each bearer that was successfully modified.

sgw_s1u_fteid.name: SGW F-TEID for S1-U
sgw_s1u_fteid.doc: SGW Fully Qualified TEID for the S1-U interface. Provides the SGW's IP address and TEID for the user plane path towards the eNB. Origin: This is the user plane endpoint identifier allocated or updated by the SGW for the S1-U interface.

recovery.name: Recovery
recovery.doc: Contains the Restart Counter of the SGW. This is included if the SGW has restarted since the last communication with the MME, allowing the MME to re-establish sessions. Origin: This value is the current restart counter of the SGW node, used for synchronization after a node failure.

private_ext.name: Private Extension
private_ext.doc: Container for vendor-specific or operator-specific information that is not part of the standard. Origin: This IE is populated with proprietary data by the network equipment vendor if required.

doc.summary_en: The SGW-C sends a Modify Bearer Response to the MME to acknowledge a Modify Bearer Request. It indicates the success or failure of the bearer modification procedure.
doc.steps:
1. The MME sends a Modify Bearer Request to the SGW-C to modify one or more EPS bearers. 
2. The SGW-C processes the request, potentially coordinating with the PGW-C and updating its user plane resources (SGW-U). 
3. The SGW-C responds to the MME with a Modify Bearer Response. 
4. The 'Cause' IE in the response indicates the overall outcome. If successful, the 'Bearer Contexts Modified' IE provides per-bearer results and may include updated F-TEIDs for the S1-U interface. (TS 29.274 §7.2.7)
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent by the SGW to the MME as a response to a Modify Bearer Request. (TS 29.274 §7.2.8.1 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
The SGW-C receives a Modify Bearer Request from the MME. (TS 29.274 §7.2.7.2 V18.4.0)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates the overall result of the operation (e.g., 'Request accepted').
- **Bearer Contexts Modified**: A grouped IE that contains the list of bearers that were modified, each with its own EBI and Cause value.
- **S1-U SGW F-TEID**: Provided within the Bearer Context if the SGW's user plane endpoint for the S1-U interface has changed.
- **Recovery (Restart Counter)**: Sent if the SGW-C has restarted since its last communication with the MME.

### Error Handling
The primary mechanism for error reporting is the 'Cause' IE, both at the message level and per-bearer level within the 'Bearer Contexts Modified' IE. Cause values like 'Context not found', 'Mandatory IE incorrect', or 'Service denied' indicate failure. (TS 29.274 §8.4 V18.4.0)

### Ordering & State
This message is sent in response to a Modify Bearer Request and concludes the procedure on the S11 interface between the MME and SGW-C. The MME waits for this response before proceeding with related signaling towards the eNodeB (e.g., E-RAB Modify Request).
doc.title: Modify Bearer Response

cause.doc: Indicates if the request was accepted or the reason for failure at the message level. Origin: The value is set by the SGW-C based on the overall outcome of processing the Modify Bearer Request.
cause.name: Cause
enum_doc: New PDN type due to network preference. (TS 29.274 §8.4)
New PDN type due to single address bearer only. (TS 29.274 §8.4)

bearer_contexts_modified.doc: A grouped IE containing the list of bearers that were successfully modified. It is conditional on a successful overall outcome. Origin: The SGW-C includes this IE to confirm which specific bearers were modified as requested by the MME.
bearer_contexts_modified.name: Bearer Contexts Modified

ebi.doc: Identifies the EPS bearer that was modified. Origin: This value is copied from the corresponding bearer context in the incoming Modify Bearer Request.
ebi.name: EPS Bearer ID (EBI)

bearer_cause.doc: Indicates the result for this specific bearer within the grouped IE. Origin: The SGW-C sets this value to report the outcome for the individual bearer modification.
bearer_cause.name: Cause

s1u_sgw_fteid.doc: The SGW's Fully Qualified TEID for the S1-U interface user plane traffic. This is sent if the SGW allocates a new F-TEID for the bearer. Origin: This F-TEID is allocated and provided by the SGW-C for the user plane path towards the eNodeB.
s1u_sgw_fteid.name: S1-U SGW F-TEID

fteid_if_type.doc: The interface type for which the F-TEID is applicable. For S1-U SGW F-TEID, this is 'S1-U SGW GTP-U interface'. Origin: This is a spec-defined enum value corresponding to the S1-U interface.
fteid_if_type.name: Interface Type

fteid_teid.doc: The Tunnel Endpoint Identifier for the user plane tunnel. Origin: This value is dynamically allocated by the SGW-U/SGW-C for the specific bearer's user plane traffic.
fteid_teid.name: TEID/GRE Key

fteid_ipv4.doc: The IPv4 address of the SGW user plane function (SGW-U). Origin: This is the configured IP address of the SGW-U node that will terminate the user plane tunnel.
fteid_ipv4.name: IPv4 address

recovery.doc: The Restart Counter of the SGW-C. This is included if the SGW-C has restarted since the last communication with the MME peer. Origin: This value is the SGW-C's locally maintained restart counter, sent to peers to detect node failures.
recovery.name: Recovery (Restart Counter)

private_extension.doc: A container for vendor-specific information elements. Origin: This IE is populated with proprietary data by the equipment vendor's implementation.
private_extension.name: Private Extension

doc.summary_en: The Modify Bearer Response is sent from the PGW-C to the SGW-C as a response to a Modify Bearer Request. It indicates whether the bearer modification, creation, or deletion was successful. NOTE: The direction specified in this task (SGW-C to PGW-C) is non-standard; the standard flow is PGW-C to SGW-C (TS 23.401 §5.4.5). This documentation reflects the standard IE content for this message type.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is used to acknowledge a Modify Bearer Request and report the result for each bearer context handled. It confirms the modification of existing bearers or the removal of bearers as requested. (TS 29.274 §7.2.2 V17.4.0)

### Triggers
A PGW-C sends this message upon receiving and processing a Modify Bearer Request from an SGW-C. The request itself is typically triggered by an MME/SGSN due to events like QoS changes or handovers.

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Overall result of the operation.
- **Bearer Contexts Modified**: A grouped IE containing the result for each successfully modified bearer, including its EBI and Cause.
- **Bearer Contexts Marked for Removal**: A grouped IE for bearers that are to be deleted.
- **Recovery**: The sender's restart counter for peer node failure detection.

### Error Handling
If the request is unsuccessful, the main 'Cause' IE will indicate the reason for failure (e.g., 'Context not found', 'Service denied'). Individual bearers can also have specific failure causes within the 'Bearer Contexts' grouped IEs.
doc.title: Modify Bearer Response

cause.name: Cause
cause.doc: Indicates the result of the Modify Bearer Request. A value of 'Request accepted' means the operation was successful. Origin: This value is determined by the responding node (typically PGW-C) based on the outcome of processing the bearer modification request.
enum_doc: New PDN type due to network preference

bearer_contexts_modified.name: Bearer Contexts Modified
bearer_contexts_modified.doc: A grouped IE that contains the list of bearers that were successfully modified. Origin: This IE is constructed by the responding node to list all bearers for which the modification procedure succeeded.

ebi.name: EPS Bearer ID (EBI)
ebi.doc: The EPS Bearer Identity uniquely identifies an EPS bearer for a UE. The value is copied from the request. Origin: This value is copied from the corresponding bearer context in the incoming Modify Bearer Request.

cause_in_group.name: Cause
cause_in_group.doc: Indicates the result for this specific bearer context. Origin: This value is set by the responding node to indicate the specific outcome for this individual bearer.

pgw_s5s8_fteid.name: PGW S5/S8 F-TEID
pgw_s5s8_fteid.doc: The F-TEID for the user plane traffic on the S5/S8 interface. It specifies the tunnel endpoint at the PGW-C. Origin: This F-TEID is provided by the PGW-C in the request and is included in the response by the SGW-C to confirm its acceptance.

fteid_if_type.name: Interface Type
fteid_if_type.doc: Specifies the GTP interface to which this F-TEID applies. Origin: This value is set according to the 3GPP specification for the specific interface being configured (e.g., S5/S8 PGW user plane).

fteid_v4_flag.name: V4
fteid_v4_flag.doc: A flag indicating if an IPv4 address is present in the F-TEID. Origin: This flag is set to true if the node allocates and includes an IPv4 address for the tunnel endpoint.

fteid_v6_flag.name: V6
fteid_v6_flag.doc: A flag indicating if an IPv6 address is present in the F-TEID. Origin: This flag is set to true if the node allocates and includes an IPv6 address for the tunnel endpoint.

fteid_teid.name: TEID or GRE Key
fteid_teid.doc: The Tunnel Endpoint Identifier or GRE Key used to identify the tunnel. Origin: This value is dynamically allocated by the node creating the tunnel endpoint (e.g., the PGW-C).

fteid_ipv4.name: IPv4 address
fteid_ipv4.doc: The IPv4 address of the user plane tunnel endpoint. Present if the V4 flag is set. Origin: This is the IP address of the user plane function associated with the control plane node that is creating the F-TEID.

recovery.name: Recovery (Restart Counter)
recovery.doc: The Restart Counter of the sending node. The receiver stores this value and uses it to detect if the peer node has restarted. Origin: This value is maintained locally by the sending node and incremented each time it restarts.

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: Contains vendor-specific or operator-specific information. Origin: This IE is populated by the sending node with proprietary data if required by a specific vendor's implementation.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends the Release Access Bearers Request to the SGW to request the release of all S1-U bearers for a specific UE, typically when the radio connection is lost.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This procedure is used by the MME to request the SGW to release the access network resources for a given UE. The SGW is expected to release all S1-U bearers associated with the control plane TEID provided in the GTP-C header but keep the S5/S8 bearers active. (TS 29.274 §7.2.20.1 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
This message is sent as part of the S1 Release procedure. It is typically triggered when the MME receives an S1AP UE CONTEXT RELEASE REQUEST from the eNodeB, indicating the loss of the RRC connection to the UE. (TS 23.401 §5.3.5 V18.4.0)

### Key IEs
The primary identifier for the UE context is the SGW C-Plane TEID in the GTP-C header. The message body itself has no mandatory IEs. Optional IEs like 'Node Type' can be included.

### Error Handling
If the SGW cannot find the UE context corresponding to the TEID in the header, it will send a Release Access Bearers Response with a cause value such as 'Context not found'. (TS 29.274 §7.2.20.3 V18.4.0)

### Ordering & State
This message is sent after the MME decides to release the S1 connection but before the UE context in the MME is fully cleared. The SGW transitions the bearers to a suspended state from its perspective, awaiting potential re-establishment or complete deactivation.
doc.title: Release Access Bearers Request

node_type.name: Node Type
node_type.doc: Indicates the type of the sending node, which is MME in this context. This helps the SGW distinguish requests from an MME versus an S4-SGSN. Origin: The value is set by the sending MME based on its own node type, as defined in the 3GPP specification.

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: A container for vendor-specific information that is not standardized by 3GPP. Origin: This IE is included and populated by the network equipment vendor to support proprietary features.

enterprise_id.name: Enterprise ID
enterprise_id.doc: The IANA-assigned enterprise number of the vendor that defined the proprietary information. Origin: This is a standardized identifier assigned by IANA to a specific vendor.

proprietary_info.name: Proprietary Information
proprietary_info.doc: The vendor-specific data itself. Its structure and meaning are defined by the vendor identified in the Enterprise ID. Origin: The content is defined and generated by the vendor's proprietary implementation.

doc.summary_en: The SGW sends the Release Access Bearers Response to the MME to acknowledge the Release Access Bearers Request. It indicates the success or failure of the operation to release the user plane bearers for a specific UE.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent by the SGW to the MME or S4-SGSN as a response to a Release Access Bearers Request message. It confirms the release of the S1-U bearers for all PDN connections of the UE. (TS 29.274 §7.2.14.1 V18.2.0)

### Triggers
The SGW receives a Release Access Bearers Request from the MME, typically triggered by events like S1 release or inter-RAT handover where the user plane is switched. The SGW then processes the request and sends this response. (TS 23.401 §5.3.5)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates whether the request was accepted or rejected. A value of 'Request accepted' signifies success.
- **Recovery (Restart Counter)**: This optional IE is included if the SGW has restarted since its last communication with the MME, allowing the MME to resynchronize state.

### Error Handling
If the SGW cannot fulfill the request, it includes a Cause IE with an appropriate rejection value, such as 'Mandatory IE incorrect' or 'System failure'. The MME then handles the failure according to its procedures. (TS 29.274 §8.4)
doc.title: Release Access Bearers Response

cause.name: Cause
cause.doc: Indicates if the request was accepted or if an error occurred. The value 'Request accepted' (16) signifies success. (TS 29.274 §8.4). Origin: The value is set by the SGW based on the outcome of processing the corresponding request.
enum_doc: New PDN type due to network preference
New PDN type due to single address bearer only
Semantic error in the TFT operation
Syntactic error in the TFT operation
Missing or unknown EPS bearer context

recovery.name: Recovery (Restart Counter)
recovery.doc: The Restart Counter. This IE is included if the SGW has restarted since the last communication with the MME. The value is a counter that is incremented upon each restart. (TS 29.274 §8.13). Origin: The value is a locally managed counter on the SGW that is incremented upon restart.

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: Container for proprietary or vendor-specific information. (TS 29.274 §8.62). Origin: The content is vendor-specific and depends on the implementation of the SGW.

doc.summary_en: The SGW-C sends the Release Access Bearers Response to the MME to acknowledge the release of all S1-U bearers for a specific UE.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent by the SGW-C to the MME as part of the Release Access Bearers procedure. It confirms that the SGW-C has released the user plane bearers (S1-U) associated with the UE's session as requested by the MME. (TS 29.274 §7.2.22.1 V18.2.0)

### Triggers
The SGW-C sends this message upon successfully processing a Release Access Bearers Request from the MME. This procedure is typically initiated by the MME when the UE moves to an idle state (e.g., ECM-IDLE) and the S1 connection is released. (TS 23.401 §5.3.5 V18.2.0)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates the success or failure of the operation. A value of 'Request accepted' signifies success.
- **Recovery (Restart Counter)**: Included if the SGW-C has restarted since the last communication with the MME, allowing the MME to re-establish context if necessary.

### Error Handling
If the SGW-C cannot fulfill the request, it sends a Release Access Bearers Response with an appropriate cause value indicating the reason for failure. The MME then handles the failure according to its internal logic. (TS 29.274 §7.2.22.2 V18.2.0)
doc.title: Release Access Bearers Response

cause.name: Cause
cause.doc: Indicates if the request was accepted or the reason for failure. A value of 'Request accepted' (16) is used for success. Origin: This value is determined by the SGW-C based on the outcome of processing the incoming Release Access Bearers Request.

recovery.name: Recovery (Restart Counter)
recovery.doc: Contains the Restart Counter of the sending node. This IE is included if the node has restarted since the last communication, allowing peer nodes to re-synchronize state. Origin: This value is the current restart counter maintained by the SGW-C, which is incremented each time the node restarts.

doc.summary_en: The PGW-C sends the Resume Acknowledge to the SGW-C to confirm the successful processing of a Resume Notification. This exchange indicates that the UE's signaling connection is re-established and bearers are active again, typically after a period of inactivity managed by ISR (Idle mode Signaling Reduction).
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Resume Acknowledge message is sent by the PGW-C to the SGW-C to acknowledge a Resume Notification message, confirming that the UE's bearers are now considered active. (TS 29.274 §7.2.23 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
This message is sent in direct response to a `Resume Notification` message received from the SGW-C. The SGW-C sends the notification when a UE, for which ISR was active, performs any procedure that requires signaling with the PGW-C. (TS 23.401 §5.3.3.2A V18.x.x)

### Key IEs
*   **Cause**: This mandatory IE indicates the outcome of the operation. A value of 'Request accepted' signifies success.

### Procedure Flow
1.  A UE in idle mode with ISR active initiates communication (e.g., Service Request).
2.  The SGW-C receives the uplink signaling and sends a `Resume Notification` to the PGW-C.
3.  The PGW-C processes the notification, marks the bearers as active, and responds with a `Resume Acknowledge` message to the SGW-C.
4.  Upon receiving a successful `Resume Acknowledge`, the SGW-C continues with the ongoing UE procedure.

### Error Handling
If the PGW-C cannot successfully process the Resume Notification, it sends a Resume Acknowledge with a Cause value indicating the reason for the failure. The SGW-C must then handle the failure, which may involve rejecting the UE's procedure. (TS 29.274 §7.2.23 V18.4.0)
doc.title: Resume Acknowledge

msg_type.doc: Identifies the GTPv2-C message type. For Resume Acknowledge, the value is 167. Origin: The value is fixed for this message type as defined in 3GPP TS 29.274 Table 6.1-1.

cause.doc: Indicates if the request was accepted or the reason for failure. A value of 1 ('Request accepted') signifies success. Other values indicate specific errors. Origin: The value is set by the PGW-C to indicate the outcome of processing the corresponding Resume Notification message.

private_extension.doc: Contains vendor-specific or operator-specific information, structured as an Enterprise ID followed by proprietary data. Origin: This IE is optionally included by the network equipment vendor for proprietary extensions.

enterprise_id.doc: A unique identifier for the vendor or organization defining the private extension, as assigned by IANA. Origin: The value is assigned by IANA to a specific vendor.

extension_value.doc: The proprietary data defined by the vendor. Its structure is not defined by 3GPP. Origin: The value is proprietary data generated by the sending node according to the vendor's specific implementation.

doc.summary_en: The SGW-C sends the Resume Acknowledge message to the MME as a response to a preceding Resume Notification message. This confirms that the SGW-C has resumed the suspended bearers for a UE that has become active again. (TS 29.274 §7.2.23 V17.4.0)
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is used by the SGW-C to acknowledge the MME's request to resume bearers that were previously suspended, typically when a UE transitions from an idle/power-saving state to a connected state. A successful acknowledgement indicates the SGW-C is ready to forward downlink data. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.3 V17.4.0, TS 29.274 §7.2.23 V17.4.0)

### Triggers
The SGW-C sends this message upon receiving a Resume Notification from the MME. The MME initiates this procedure when it needs to re-establish the S1-U bearers for a UE, for example, due to receiving uplink data or a service request from the UE. (TS 29.274 §7.2.22 V17.4.0)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: The most critical IE, indicating whether the resume operation was successful. 'Request accepted' is the success value.

### Error Handling
If the SGW-C cannot resume the bearers, it sends a Resume Acknowledge with an appropriate cause value (e.g., 'Context not found', 'Service denied'). The MME then handles the failure, which may involve rejecting the UE's service request. (TS 29.274 §8.4 V17.4.0)
doc.title: Resume Acknowledge

cause.name: Cause
cause.doc: Indicates if the Resume Notification was accepted or the reason for failure. A value of 'Request accepted' signifies success. Origin: This IE is mandatory in the response as defined by the specification to convey the outcome of the request.

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: A vendor-specific or operator-specific IE that can be used to convey additional information. Origin: This IE is optional and included for extensibility as defined by the specification.

enterprise_id.name: Enterprise ID
enterprise_id.doc: The IANA-assigned enterprise number of the entity that defined the private extension. Origin: This field is part of the Private Extension structure, identifying the vendor.

extension_value.name: Extension Value
extension_value.doc: The proprietary data for the private extension. Origin: This field contains the vendor-specific data within the Private Extension IE.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends a Resume Notification to the SGW-C to indicate that a UE, which was previously in a suspended state, has re-established contact and is now reachable for downlink data. This allows the SGW-C to resume forwarding buffered downlink packets to the UE.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This message is sent from the MME/SGSN to the SGW to indicate that the UE is available for end-to-end signalling, allowing the SGW to resume sending downlink data. This procedure is used when the UE was in an idle state with a suspend indication. (TS 29.274 § 7.2.21 V17.9.0)

### Triggers
The MME initiates this procedure when it receives a trigger indicating the UE has become active, such as a Service Request from the UE, after the UE's bearers were previously suspended. (TS 23.401 § 5.3.4.3)

### Key IEs
- **IMSI**: Uniquely identifies the UE whose session is being resumed.
- **EPS Bearer ID (EBI)**: Conditionally included if only a specific bearer is being resumed.
- **User Location Information (ULI)**: Provides the SGW with the UE's current location (e.g., TAI and ECGI).
doc.title: Resume Notification

imsi.name: International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI)
imsi.doc: A unique number associated with a specific user of a cellular network, used to identify the UE for whom the session is being resumed. Origin: The MME includes the IMSI from the UE's context to uniquely identify the subscriber's session at the SGW-C.

ebi.name: EPS Bearer ID (EBI)
ebi.doc: Identifies the specific Evolved Packet System (EPS) bearer to be resumed. If this IE is not included, it implies that all suspended bearers for the UE are to be resumed. Origin: The MME includes this IE if the resume notification applies only to a specific bearer, as determined by the triggering procedure.

uli.name: User Location Information (ULI)
uli.doc: Provides the SGW-C with the UE's current location, which may include TAI (Tracking Area Identity) and ECGI (E-UTRAN Cell Global Identifier). This is conditional on the MME having updated location information. Origin: The MME provides the latest known location of the UE, obtained during the procedure that triggered the resume notification (e.g., Service Request).

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: A container for proprietary or vendor-specific information that is not defined in the 3GPP standard. Origin: This is a standard optional container provided by the GTPv2-C specification for vendor-specific data.

doc.summary_en: The Serving Gateway Control Plane (SGW-C) sends a Resume Notification to the Packet Data Network Gateway Control Plane (PGW-C) to indicate that a UE has become reachable again. This allows the PGW-C to resume sending any buffered downlink data for that UE's PDN connection.
doc.steps:
1. The SGW-C detects that a UE has re-established its connection and is now reachable (e.g., following a Service Request procedure).
2. This typically occurs after the SGW-C had previously indicated the UE was unreachable and that downlink data was being buffered (e.g., via a PGW Downlink Triggering Notification).
3. The SGW-C sends the Resume Notification message to the PGW-C over the S5/S8 interface, identifying the specific PDN connection using the IMSI or LBI.
4. Upon receiving this notification, the PGW-C stops buffering downlink data and can proceed to send it towards the SGW.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Resume Notification message is sent from the SGW to the PGW to indicate that the UE is now available for end-to-end signalling and the PGW may send buffered downlink packets. (TS 29.274 §7.2.23 V18.2.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is triggered when the SGW receives an indication of UE activity for a UE whose downlink data path was previously suspended. A common trigger is the reception of a Modify Bearer Request from the MME/S4-SGSN during a UE-initiated Service Request procedure, especially when Idle mode Signaling Reduction (ISR) is active or after a period of extended inactivity. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.3)

### Key IEs
- **IMSI**: Used to identify the subscriber's session at the PGW.
- **Linked EPS Bearer ID (LBI)**: Identifies the default bearer associated with the PDN connection that is being resumed.

### Error Handling
The PGW-C responds with a Resume Acknowledge message. If the PGW-C cannot find the context for the UE (e.g., invalid TEID), it will return a cause value such as 'Context not found' in the acknowledgement. (TS 29.274 §7.2.24)
doc.title: Resume Notification

imsi.doc: International Mobile Subscriber Identity. A globally unique ID allocated to each mobile subscriber. It is sent if available to help the PGW-C identify the UE's PDN connection context. Origin: Sourced by the SGW-C from the UE's session context established during the attach procedure.

lbi.doc: Linked EPS Bearer ID. This IE contains the EPS Bearer Identity (EBI) of the default bearer to which this notification is linked, identifying the specific PDN connection. Origin: Set by the SGW-C to the EBI of the default bearer for the specific PDN connection being resumed.

private_extension.doc: Private Extension. This IE contains vendor-specific or operator-specific information that is not part of the 3GPP standard. Origin: Included by the sending node (SGW-C) for proprietary extensions; its content is vendor-defined.

doc.summary_en: The PGW-C sends the Suspend Acknowledge to the SGW-C to confirm the receipt of a Suspend Notification. This acknowledges that the UE has entered an idle state and the PGW-C has started buffering any downlink data for the UE.
doc.steps: ['1. The SGW-C detects that the UE has become idle and sends a Suspend Notification to the PGW-C.', '2. The PGW-C receives the notification, marks the corresponding bearer(s) as suspended, and starts buffering downlink packets.', '3. The PGW-C sends this Suspend Acknowledge message back to the SGW-C to confirm the action.', "4. A 'Request accepted' Cause value indicates success. Other values indicate failure to process the notification."]
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
The Suspend Acknowledge message is sent by the PGW-C to the SGW-C as a response to a Suspend Notification message. It confirms that the PGW-C has suspended the bearers for the UE. (TS 29.274 §7.2.24 V18.4.0)

### Triggers
This message is triggered when the PGW-C receives a Suspend Notification from the SGW-C, which is sent when the S1-U bearer is released for the UE while the S5/S8 bearer is preserved. (TS 23.401 §5.3.4.1.1)

### Key IEs
- **Cause**: Indicates whether the suspend request was successful. This is the primary indicator of the procedure's outcome.
- **Recovery (Restart Counter)**: Included if the PGW-C has restarted, to allow the SGW-C to re-establish or clear affected sessions.

### Error Handling
If the PGW-C cannot process the Suspend Notification (e.g., context not found), it will respond with a Suspend Acknowledge containing a Cause value indicating the reason for failure. (TS 29.274 §7.2.24 V18.4.0)
doc.title: Suspend Acknowledge

cause.name: Cause
cause.doc: Indicates if the Suspend Notification was accepted by the PGW-C. A value of 'Request accepted' (16) confirms successful processing. Origin: This IE is mandatory in the response to convey the outcome of the request, as per TS 29.274.

recovery.name: Recovery (Restart Counter)
recovery.doc: Contains the Restart Counter of the PGW-C. It is included if the PGW-C has restarted since the last communication with the SGW-C peer, allowing the peer to resynchronize state. Origin: This IE is conditionally included based on the node's restart status, as defined in TS 29.274.

private_extension.name: Private Extension
private_extension.doc: A container for proprietary information specific to a vendor, allowing for non-standard extensions. Origin: This IE is optional and its inclusion is determined by vendor-specific implementations.

doc.summary_en: The MME sends a Suspend Notification to the SGW to indicate that all bearers for a specific UE are suspended. This typically occurs when the UE moves to a non-E-UTRAN coverage area where ISR is not active, or due to S1 release from inactivity in certain 2G/3G cells. The SGW stops sending downlink data and may start buffering it.
doc.comment_md:
### Purpose
This procedure is used by the MME to notify the S-GW that all bearers of a UE are suspended. The S-GW shall mark all the bearers of the UE as suspended. (TS 29.274 §7.2.19.1 V17.9.0)

### Triggers
This procedure is initiated by the MME when it detects that a UE has moved from E-UTRAN to GERAN/UTRAN and ISR is not active.
